item.title: 基于预训练模型的自适应专家混合持续学习框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应MoE框架提升持续学习中的样本效率。



摘要： 本文针对持续学习中任务数据量有限且任务可能重叠的问题，提出了一种基于预训练模型的自适应专家混合（MoE）框架。该框架通过增量全局池化和实例级提示掩码两个创新组件，有效缓解了数据稀缺带来的挑战，并在任务重叠情况下避免负迁移。实验表明，该方法提升了样本效率并具有广泛适用性。

item.title: 基于对比学习的协作记忆框架MemCollab提升多模型推理性能
item.content:encoded:
MemCollab通过对比不同模型推理轨迹构建共享记忆，提升多模型推理效果。



摘要： 本文提出MemCollab，一种协作记忆框架，通过对比不同代理在同一任务上的推理轨迹，构建与代理无关的记忆。该方法提炼出任务级不变量，抑制代理特定偏差，并引入任务感知检索机制，在数学推理和代码生成任务中验证了其在准确率和推理效率上的优势。实验表明，协作构建的记忆可作为多种LLM代理的共享推理资源。

item.title: 多组学习的样本复杂度上界分析
item.content:encoded:
研究多组学习的样本复杂度，提出优化算法并验证其收敛率。



摘要： 本文证明了多组学习中最紧致的样本复杂度上界。通过扩展一包含图预测策略，并引入二分图b-匹配的一般化方法，提出了新的算法。在组可实现设定下，给出了下界证明，确认该算法的log n / n收敛率在一般情况下是最优的。若放松学习目标，使评估组别独立于样本选择，则在组可实现条件下，算法可达到最优的1/n收敛率。

item.title: 基于机器学习的重庆城市温湿度短期预测研究
item.content:encoded:
XGBoost在城市温湿度预测中表现最佳，准确率高。



摘要： 本文针对地形复杂的重庆城市，比较了七种机器学习模型在小时级空气温度和相对湿度预测中的表现。通过统一的数据预处理、滞后特征构建和时间序列验证框架，评估了各模型的预测精度和鲁棒性。结果表明，XGBoost在两项任务中均表现最优，温度预测的平均绝对误差为0.302°C，湿度预测的平均绝对误差为1.271%，R²值达到0.989。研究展示了树集成学习在结构化气象时间序列预测中的有效性，为山地城市智能气象预报提供了实用参考。

item.title: 基于LoRA适配器的作者风格迁移框架AuthorMix
item.content:encoded:
AuthorMix实现高效、精准的文本风格迁移，提升低资源目标的语义保留。



摘要： 本文提出AuthorMix，一个轻量、模块化且可解释的风格迁移框架。通过在少量高资源作者数据上训练风格特定的LoRA适配器，该方法能够快速为新目标生成专门的适配模型，仅需少量目标风格示例即可完成训练。相比现有最先进的风格迁移方法及GPT-5.1，AuthorMix在低资源目标上表现更优，显著提升了语义保留能力。

item.title: 多模态大语言模型对隐藏模式视觉幻觉的脆弱性及改进方法
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示MLLM对视觉幻觉的敏感性并提出改进方案。



摘要： 本文指出多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在面对隐藏模式的视觉幻觉时存在显著缺陷，模型难以识别人类明显可见的隐藏内容，这反映了模型与人类感知之间的不一致，并带来安全隐患。为此，作者构建了IlluChar数据集，发现高频率注意力偏差是导致模型失败的关键机制。为解决该问题，提出了多尺度感知策略（SMSP），通过抑制高频背景纹理，使模型更接近人类视觉感知。实验表明，SMSP显著提升了多个MLLM在幻觉图像上的性能，例如将Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct的准确率从13.0%提升至84.0%。该研究为MLLM的视觉感知提供了新见解，并提供了一个实用且稳健的解决方案。

item.title: 3DCity-LLM：面向城市级3D场景的统一视觉语言感知框架
item.content:encoded:
提出3DCity-LLM框架，提升城市级3D场景理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出3DCity-LLM，一个用于3D城市级视觉语言感知和理解的统一框架。该框架采用从粗到细的特征编码策略，包含目标物体、物体间关系和全局场景三个并行分支。为支持大规模训练，研究者构建了3DCity-LLM-1.2M数据集，包含约120万高质量样本，涵盖七类任务，从精细物体分析到多维场景规划。该数据集结合了明确的3D数值信息和多样化的用户模拟，提升了问答的多样性与真实性。此外，研究还采用基于文本相似度和LLM语义评估的多维评估协议，确保方法评估的准确性和全面性。实验表明，3DCity-LLM在两个基准测试中均优于现有最佳方法，为推进空间推理和城市智能提供了新方向。

item.title: 基于眼动信息的视觉语言模型增强框架
item.content:encoded:
通过引入眼动信息提升视觉语言模型对第一视角行为的理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于眼动信息的视觉语言模型增强框架，通过在训练过程中直接整合眼动数据，使模型能够动态关注由眼动突出的区域。该方法利用眼动生成查询，并通过眼动正则化机制确保模型注意力与人类注意力模式一致。实验表明，该方法在语义评分上相比未使用眼动数据的基线模型提升了近13%，显著增强了模型对未来事件预测的能力。

item.title: 基于人口代表性简历数据集的因果公平性审计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
PopResume数据集支持因果公平性评估，揭示招聘系统中的歧视模式。



摘要： 本文提出PopResume，一个基于人口统计学的简历数据集，用于对基于大语言模型和视觉语言模型的简历筛选系统进行因果公平性审计。与以往依赖人工注入人口信息和结果差异的基准不同，PopResume基于真实人口统计数据，保留了属性间的自然关系，支持路径特定效应（PSE）分析。通过区分业务必要路径和红线路径，该方法能够识别合法与非法的差异来源。在5个职业的60.8万份简历上评估4个LLM和4个VLM后，发现五种传统指标无法捕捉的歧视模式，证明PSE评估能揭示结果层面测量无法发现的公平性问题，强调了因果基础审计框架在AI招聘中的重要性。

item.title: SIMART：统一框架实现高保真可动3D资产生成
item.content:encoded:
SIMART通过稀疏3D VQ-VAE提升可动3D资产生成效率与质量。



摘要： 本文提出SIMART，一个统一的多模态大语言模型框架，用于生成高质量的可动3D资产。传统方法依赖多阶段流水线，容易累积误差，而SIMART通过引入稀疏3D VQ-VAE，将3D标记数量减少70%，显著降低内存开销并提升复杂可动物体的生成能力。该框架在PartNet-Mobility和真实场景AIGC数据集上表现优异，支持物理驱动的机器人仿真。

item.title: ZS-Fuse：结合零样本音频语言模型与专业模型的语音情感识别方法
item.content:encoded:
提出ZS-Fuse方法提升语音情感识别性能。



摘要： 本文提出ZS-Fuse，一种将零样本音频语言模型（ALM）与领域专家基础模型（FM）结合的后融合方法，用于提升语音情感识别（SER）性能。通过简单的提示集成和提示增强技术，该方法在三个SER数据集上优于现有最佳基线，如WavLM-Large。研究验证了ALM与专业模型协同工作的潜力，为未来研究提供了新方向。

item.title: 上下文图匹配的理论分析
item.content:encoded:
研究上下文图匹配中的信息理论阈值，揭示结构与上下文信息的相互作用。



摘要： 本文研究在高斯设定下的上下文图匹配问题，其中两个网络的边权重和节点特征具有相关性。文章推导了精确恢复的理论阈值，并根据图相关性、特征相关性、节点数量和特征维度等参数，识别出几乎精确恢复可能或不可能的条件。与标准图匹配中出现的全有或全无相变不同，上下文信息引入了更复杂的结构，使得精确和几乎精确恢复的阈值不再重合。该研究首次严谨地刻画了结构信息与上下文信息在图匹配中的交互作用，并为设计高效算法提供了基准。

item.title: 基于中间层结构的高效语言模型表示方法ILSE
item.content:encoded:
ILSE通过整合LLM中间层信息提升任务表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为ILSE的结构化方法，通过整合大型语言模型（LLM）的中间层表示来学习更有效的特征。ILSE的核心是Cayley-Encoder，利用扩展的Cayley图实现高效的层间信息传播。在13个分类和语义相似性任务中评估表明，ILSE在多个预训练LLM上均优于基线方法，准确率提升最高达44%，相似度指标提升25%。此外，ILSE在少样本场景下表现出数据高效性，使小型LLM能与大型模型竞争。

item.title: 制度变化下的学习理论框架研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出制度变化下的学习理论框架及其初步定理支持。



摘要： 本文研究在制度变化环境下学习的理论框架，重点探讨了可接受传输、保护核心保持和评估者意识学习演化等核心概念。文章提出了制度变化下的结构障碍论证，并引入了受保护稳定性模板及数值与符号实例，包括凸性和演绎性设置。同时，文章建立了评估者分解、同态、组合和部分内核对齐的定理层结果。通过一个双制度示例，明确了可接受性证书、保护评估核心和制度变化成本。本文旨在构建制度变化学习的结构化理论框架，并提供初步定理支持，而非完整的定量理论。

item.title: 大语言模型中巴以身份生成的分布模式与公平性表现分析
item.content:encoded:
研究分析大语言模型生成的巴以身份分布及公平性处理方式。



摘要： 本文研究了五种主流大语言模型在不同情境下生成的巴勒斯坦和以色列身份特征，发现战争情境下巴勒斯坦角色多呈现低社会经济地位和生存导向，而以色列角色则多为中产阶级和专业属性。尽管模型在提示避免偏见后出现性别非二元化或职业泛化等变化，但社会经济差异仍显著存在。分析还显示，模型推理中提及公平概念，但最终生成结果仍呈现多样化分布，表明其对公平性的理解与实际输出之间存在差异。

item.title: 基于多尺度对齐的全切片图像大语言模型MMLL-HWSI
item.content:encoded:
提出多尺度全切片图像大语言模型，提升病理诊断理解与推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为MLLM-HWSI的层次化全切片图像（WSI）大语言模型，通过在细胞、切片块、区域和整个WSI四个尺度上对齐视觉特征与病理语言，实现更精确的证据基础推理。该模型采用多尺度嵌入和一致性损失机制，结合轻量级的细胞注意力融合模块，将病理信息整合为紧凑的细胞令牌，并与文本令牌联合输入指令调优的大语言模型中，用于开放性推理、视觉问答、报告生成等任务。实验表明，MLLM-HWSI在13个WSI级别基准上取得最新SOTA结果，提升了病理分析的准确性和可解释性。

item.title: 基于毫米波雷达的材料分类方法在机器人导航中的应用
item.content:encoded:
毫米波雷达材料分类提升机器人导航性能，但受几何变化影响显著。



摘要： 本文提出一种适用于超低功耗边缘设备的轻量级毫米波雷达材料分类方法，采用紧凑的距离-范围强度描述符和多层感知机进行实时推理。虽然在标准训练几何条件下分类器达到94.2%的宏F1值，但在实际几何偏移（如传感器高度变化和小角度倾斜）下性能显著下降，宏F1值降至约68.5%。分析表明，系统性强度缩放和角度依赖的雷达散射截面效应导致特征分布偏离，进而影响分类效果。文章探讨了通过归一化、几何增强和运动感知特征提高鲁棒性的方向。

item.title: MedCausalX：基于因果推理的医学视觉语言模型框架
item.content:encoded:
MedCausalX提升医学诊断的因果可靠性与一致性。



摘要： 本文提出MedCausalX，一个专注于医学视觉语言模型中因果推理的端到端框架。针对现有医学链式思维模型在因果推理上的不足，作者指出三个核心挑战，并通过引入CRMed数据集和两阶段自适应反思架构加以解决。该框架利用因果和验证标记，增强模型对因果关系的识别与验证能力，同时通过误差归因强化学习优化推理轨迹，显著提升诊断一致性并减少幻觉现象。实验表明，MedCausalX在多个基准测试中表现优异，成为医学因果推理的新标准。

item.title: 多机器人协同交互的多模态框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出多模态框架提升人机多机器人协作能力。



摘要： 本文针对多机器人在社会环境中的人机交互问题，提出一种统一的多模态框架。该框架使每个机器人作为具有多模态感知和基于大语言模型的决策自主认知代理，同时通过集中式协调机制实现团队层面的轮流发言与行动管理，避免冲突。实验在双人形机器人上进行，展示了通过语音、手势、眼神和移动相结合的交互策略，实现连贯的多智能体协作。未来将进行更大规模的用户研究和深入探索社会情境下的多智能体互动动态。

item.title: 基于后选择的分布模型评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PS-DME框架，实现可靠模型比较与性能-可靠性权衡分析。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为后选择分布模型评估（PS-DME）的新方法，用于在模型预筛选后对测试时的KPI分布进行可靠估计。该方法基于e值，能够控制后选择错误覆盖率，相比传统样本分割方法更高效。实验表明，PS-DME能够在不同可靠性水平下可靠地比较候选模型配置，支持对性能与可靠性权衡的统计验证。

item.title: GLA-CLIP：提升无训练开放词汇语义分割的全局局部对齐框架
item.content:encoded:
提出GLA-CLIP解决滑动窗口分割中的语义不一致问题。



摘要： 本文提出GLA-CLIP框架，用于解决无训练开放词汇语义分割中因滑动窗口处理导致的语义差异问题。该方法通过扩展键值对以整合全局上下文信息，并引入代理锚点提供统一语义参考，同时采用动态归一化机制应对小目标场景。实验表明，GLA-CLIP能有效提升分割性能，且可集成至现有方法中扩大感受野。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于DINOv3的3D神经影像分割模型研究
item.content:encoded:
利用2D视觉模型提升3D神经影像分割精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种将2D视觉基础模型DINOv3适应到3D生物医学分割任务的方法，以解决3D神经影像数据获取困难和标注稀缺的问题。通过设计膨胀适配策略，将2D滤波器扩展为3D操作符，并引入拓扑感知骨架损失，提高神经元结构重建的准确性。实验在四个神经影像数据集上验证了该方法的有效性，结果显示其在多个指标上均优于现有方法。

item.title: 延迟反馈下多AI代理任务中的人类决策与归因偏差研究
item.content:encoded:
研究人类在多AI系统中因延迟反馈产生的决策偏差和责任误判。



摘要： 本文探讨了在涉及多个AI代理且结果延迟的环境下，人类决策如何受到认知偏差的影响。通过控制实验发现，参与者对负面结果的调整更为强烈，但常无法准确识别导致失败的行为，并错误地将责任归咎于不同的AI代理，从而做出与实际原因关联性较弱的决策。这种现象被称为归因偏差，表明在延迟反馈和多AI交互环境中，认知偏差可能被放大，因此需要改进决策支持系统以增强因果理解。

item.title: 基于动态偏好推断的序列决策方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种动态偏好推断框架，提升多目标强化学习适应性。



摘要： 本文研究在偏好权重随情境变化的环境下，如何进行序列决策。作者提出动态偏好推断（DPI）框架，模仿人类认知过程，通过概率信念更新和策略条件化来适应不断变化的目标。DPI结合变分偏好推断模块与偏好条件化的演员-评论家结构，利用向量回报作为隐含权衡的证据。实验表明，在队列、迷宫和多目标连续控制环境中，DPI能够快速适应新目标，并优于固定权重和启发式基线方法。

item.title: 基于时空网络的高分辨率遥感图像变化检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的时空网络用于高分辨率遥感图像变化检测。



摘要： 本文针对高分辨率遥感图像的变化检测任务，提出了一种基于时空网络的新方法。传统方法主要依赖于Conv-LSTM，缺乏空间特征。为解决这一问题，作者引入了Conv-LSTM结构，并提出了L-UNet和AL-UNet模型，通过替换部分卷积层并利用Atrous结构增强多尺度空间信息。实验结果表明，所提方法在数量和质量上均优于其他方法，具有更好的性能。

item.title: TreeTeaming：一种自动化的视觉语言模型红队测试框架
item.content:encoded:
TreeTeaming提升VLM安全测试效率与多样性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为TreeTeaming的自动化红队测试框架，旨在克服传统线性探索方法的局限性。该框架通过动态演化策略树的方式，实现对视觉语言模型（VLM）安全漏洞的高效发现。核心组件包括由大语言模型驱动的策略协调器和多模态执行器，能够自主优化攻击路径并拓展策略分支。实验表明，TreeTeaming在12个主流VLM上均取得优异表现，最高攻击成功率达87.60%，同时显著提升了攻击策略的多样性与隐蔽性。

item.title: AI驱动的需求工程系统 ReqFusion 提升软件需求提取效率
item.content:encoded:
ReqFusion利用多模型AI自动化提取和分析软件需求，提升效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文介绍了 ReqFusion，一个基于多个大型语言模型（LLM）的 AI 增强系统，用于自动化提取、分类和分析软件需求。该系统整合了 OpenAI GPT、Anthropic Claude 和 Groq 模型，能够从 PDF、DOCX 和 PPTX 等文档中提取功能性和非功能性需求，并采用 PEGS 方法进行结构化处理。实验表明，PEGS 引导提示方法在 F1 分数上优于通用提示（0.88 vs 0.71）。通过 18 个真实文档生成 226 条需求，其中 54.9% 为功能性需求，45.1% 为非功能性需求。扩展评估显示，系统在提取准确率和分析时间上均有显著提升，相比人工方法效率提高 78%。多模型架构提高了可靠性，PEGS 方法确保了需求覆盖的全面性。

item.title: SAM2在3D医学影像分割中的零样本应用研究
item.content:encoded:
SAM2可实现3D医学影像的零样本分割，无需微调。



摘要： 本文研究了Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) 在3D医学CT影像中的零样本自动分割应用，未进行任何微调或领域特定训练。分析表明，SAM2在处理3D数据时缺乏固有的体积感知能力，为此提出了一种仅在推理阶段进行的架构和流程改进，通过将CT切片视为有序序列来适应3D数据。在TotalSegmentator数据集的500例CT扫描中进行了系统消融实验，评估了提示策略、记忆传播方案和多轮优化效果。最终在2500例CT扫描上验证了最佳配置的性能，结果显示，在权重冻结的情况下，SAM2仍能生成连贯的3D分割结果，证明了全零样本方法在体积医学影像分割中的可行性。

item.title: ABSTRAL框架：多智能体系统设计的可检视与可迁移方法
item.content:encoded:
ABSTRAL通过自然语言文档优化多智能体系统设计，提升任务效率和可迁移性。



摘要： 本文介绍了ABSTRAL框架，该框架将多智能体系统（MAS）的架构视为一个不断演化的自然语言文档，通过对比追踪分析进行优化。研究发现，在固定回合预算下，集成系统仅达到26%的回合效率，但通过并行任务分解仍优于单智能体基线。此外，文档中编码的设计知识可跨领域迁移，显著提升新领域的初始性能。对比追踪分析还能发现原始设计中未包含的专业角色，这是现有系统所不具备的能力。在SOPBench基准测试中，ABSTRAL使用GPT-4o模型实现了70%的验证通过率和65.96%的测试通过率，并提供了可检查的设计依据。

item.title: Dari语言虚假信息检测研究与数据集构建
item.content:encoded:
首次构建Dari语言虚假信息数据集，提升内容审核效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了DariMis，首个针对阿富汗主要语言Dari的YouTube视频虚假信息标注数据集，包含9224条视频，按信息类型（虚假、部分真实、真实）和危害等级（低、中、高）进行标注。研究发现信息类型与危害等级存在结构性关联，虚假信息中有55.9%具有中等或高等危害。文章提出了一种双输入编码策略，通过BERT模型分别处理视频标题和描述，提升虚假信息识别效果。实验表明该方法在召回率上提升了7个百分点，ParsBERT模型在准确率和宏F1指标上表现最佳。

item.title: ProGRank: Probe-Gradient Reranking to Defend Dense-Retriever RAG from Corpus Poisoning
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves the reliability of large language model applications by grounding generation in retrieved evidence, but it also introduces a new attack surface: corpus poisoning. In this setting, an adversary injects or edits passages so that they are ranked into the Top-$K$ results for target queries and then affect downstream generation. Existing defences against corpus poisoning often rely on content filtering, auxiliary models, or generator-side reasoning, which can make deployment more difficult. We propose ProGRank, a post hoc, training-free retriever-side defence for dense-retriever RAG. ProGRank stress-tests each query--passage pair under mild randomized perturbations and extracts probe gradients from a small fixed parameter subset of the retriever. From these signals, it derives two instability signals, representational consistency and dispersion risk, and combines them with a score gate in a reranking step. ProGRank preserves the original passage content, requires no retraining, and also supports a surrogate-based variant when the deployed retriever is unavailable. Extensive experiments across three datasets, three dense retriever backbones, representative corpus poisoning attacks, and both retrieval-stage and end-to-end settings show that ProGRank provides stronger defence performance and a favorable robustness--utility trade-off. It also remains competitive under adaptive evasive attacks.

item.title: A PAC-Bayesian approach to generalization for quantum models
item.content:encoded: Generalization is a central concept in machine learning theory, yet for quantum models, it is predominantly analyzed through uniform bounds that depend on a model's overall capacity rather than the specific function learned. These capacity-based uniform bounds are often too loose and entirely insensitive to the actual training and learning process. Previous theoretical guarantees have failed to provide non-uniform, data-dependent bounds that reflect the specific properties of the learned solution rather than the worst-case behavior of the entire hypothesis class. To address this limitation, we derive the first PAC-Bayesian generalization bounds for a broad class of quantum models by analyzing layered circuits composed of general quantum channels, which include dissipative operations such as mid-circuit measurements and feedforward. Through a channel perturbation analysis, we establish non-uniform bounds that depend on the norms of learned parameter matrices; we extend these results to symmetry-constrained equivariant quantum models; and we validate our theoretical framework with numerical experiments. This work provides actionable model design insights and establishes a foundational tool for a more nuanced understanding of generalization in quantum machine learning.

item.title: Strain-Parameterized Coupled Dynamics and Dual-Camera Visual Servoing for Aerial Continuum Manipulators
item.content:encoded: Tendon-driven aerial continuum manipulators (TD-ACMs) combine the maneuverability of uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) with the compliance of lightweight continuum robots (CRs). Existing coupled dynamic modeling approaches for TD-ACMs incur high computational costs and do not explicitly account for aerial platform underactuation. To address these limitations, this paper presents a generalized dynamic formulation of a coupled TD-ACM with an underactuated base. The proposed approach integrates a strain-parameterized Cosserat rod model with a rigid-body model of the UAV into a unified Lagrangian ordinary differential equation (ODE) framework on $\mathrm{SE}(3)$, thereby eliminating computationally intensive symbolic derivations. Building upon the developed model, a robust dual-camera image-based visual servoing (IBVS) scheme is introduced. The proposed controller mitigates the field-of-view (FoV) limitations of conventional IBVS, compensates for attitude-induced image motion caused by UAV lateral dynamics, and incorporates a low-level adaptive controller to address modeling uncertainties with formal stability guarantees. Extensive simulations and experimental validation on a compact custom-built prototype demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed framework in real-world scenarios.

item.title: AgentRAE: Remote Action Execution through Notification-based Visual Backdoors against Screenshots-based Mobile GUI Agents
item.content:encoded: The rapid adoption of mobile graphical user interface (GUI) agents, which autonomously control applications and operating systems (OS), exposes new system-level attack surfaces. Existing backdoors against web GUI agents and general GenAI models rely on environmental injection or deceptive pop-ups to mislead the agent operation. However, these techniques do not work on screenshots-based mobile GUI agents due to the challenges of restricted trigger design spaces, OS background interference, and conflicts in multiple trigger-action mappings. We propose AgentRAE, a novel backdoor attack capable of inducing Remote Action Execution in mobile GUI agents using visually natural triggers (e.g., benign app icons in notifications). To address the underfitting caused by natural triggers and achieve accurate multi-target action redirection, we design a novel two-stage pipeline that first enhances the agent's sensitivity to subtle iconographic differences via contrastive learning, and then associates each trigger with a specific mobile GUI agent action through a backdoor post-training. Our extensive evaluation reveals that the proposed backdoor preserves clean performance with an attack success rate of over 90% across ten mobile operations. Furthermore, it is hard to visibly detect the benign-looking triggers and circumvents eight representative state-of-the-art defenses. These results expose an overlooked backdoor vector in mobile GUI agents, underscoring the need for defenses that scrutinize notification-conditioned behaviors and internal agent representations.

item.title: SynForceNet: A Force-Driven Global-Local Latent Representation Framework for Lithium-Ion Battery Fault Diagnosis
item.content:encoded: Online safety fault diagnosis is essential for lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicles(EVs), particularly under complex and rare safety-critical conditions in real-world operation. In this work, we develop an online battery fault diagnosis network based on a deep anomaly detection framework combining kernel one-class classification and minimum-volume estimation. Mechanical constraints and spike-timing-dependent plasticity(STDP)-based dynamic representations are introduced to improve complex fault characterization and enable a more compact normal-state boundary. The proposed method is validated using 8.6 million valid data points collected from 20 EVs. Compared with several advanced baseline methods, it achieves average improvements of 7.59% in TPR, 27.92% in PPV, 18.28% in F1 score, and 23.68% in AUC. In addition, we analyze the spatial separation of fault representations before and after modeling, and further enhance framework robustness by learning the manifold structure in the latent space. The results also suggest the possible presence of shared causal structures across different fault types, highlighting the promise of integrating deep learning with physical constraints and neural dynamics for battery safety diagnosis.

item.title: A Synchronized Audio-Visual Multi-View Capture System
item.content:encoded: Multi-view capture systems have been an important tool in research for recording human motion under controlling conditions. Most existing systems are specified around video streams and provide little or no support for audio acquisition and rigorous audio-video alignment, despite both being essential for studying conversational interaction where timing at the level of turn-taking, overlap, and prosody matters. In this technical report, we describe an audio-visual multi-view capture system that addresses this gap by treating synchronized audio and synchronized video as first-class signals. The system combines a multi-camera pipeline with multi-channel microphone recording under a unified timing architecture and provides a practical workflow for calibration, acquisition, and quality control that supports repeatable recordings at scale. We quantify synchronization performance in deployment and show that the resulting recordings are temporally consistent enough to support fine-grained analysis and data-driven modeling of conversation behavior.

item.title: Can Large Language Models Reason and Optimize Under Constraints?
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated great capabilities across diverse natural language tasks; yet their ability to solve abstraction and optimization problems with constraints remains scarcely explored. In this paper, we investigate whether LLMs can reason and optimize under the physical and operational constraints of Optimal Power Flow (OPF) problem. We introduce a challenging evaluation setup that requires a set of fundamental skills such as reasoning, structured input handling, arithmetic, and constrained optimization. Our evaluation reveals that SoTA LLMs fail in most of the tasks, and that reasoning LLMs still fail in the most complex settings. Our findings highlight critical gaps in LLMs' ability to handle structured reasoning under constraints, and this work provides a rigorous testing environment for developing more capable LLM assistants that can tackle real-world power grid optimization problems.

item.title: ViKey: Enhancing Temporal Understanding in Videos via Visual Prompting
item.content:encoded: Recent advancements in Video Large Language Models (VideoLLMs) have enabled strong performance across diverse multimodal video tasks. To reduce the high computational cost of processing dense video frames, efficiency-oriented methods such as frame selection have been widely adopted. While effective at minimizing redundancy, these methods often cause notable performance drops on tasks requiring temporal reasoning. Unlike humans, who can infer event progression from sparse visual cues, VideoLLMs frequently misinterpret temporal relations when intermediate frames are omitted. To address this limitation, we explore visual prompting (VP) as a lightweight yet effective way to enhance temporal understanding in VideoLLMs. Our analysis reveals that simply annotating each frame with explicit ordinal information helps the model perceive temporal continuity. This visual cue also supports frame-level referencing and mitigates positional ambiguity within a sparsely sampled sequence. Building on these insights, we introduce ViKey, a training-free framework that combines VP with a lightweight Keyword-Frame Mapping (KFM) module. KFM leverages frame indices as dictionary-like keys to link textual cues to the most relevant frames, providing explicit temporal anchors during inference. Despite its simplicity, our approach substantially improves temporal reasoning and, on some datasets, preserves dense-frame baseline performance with as few as 20% of frames.

item.title: LLM Olympiad: Why Model Evaluation Needs a Sealed Exam
item.content:encoded: Benchmarks and leaderboards are how NLP most often communicates progress, but in the LLM era they are increasingly easy to misread. Scores can reflect benchmark-chasing, hidden evaluation choices, or accidental exposure to test content -- not just broad capability. Closed benchmarks delay some of these issues, but reduce transparency and make it harder for the community to learn from results. We argue for a complementary practice: an Olympiad-style evaluation event where problems are sealed until evaluation, submissions are frozen in advance, and all entries run through one standardized harness. After scoring, the full task set and evaluation code are released so results can be reproduced and audited. This design aims to make strong performance harder to ``manufacture'' and easier to trust.

item.title: Span Modeling for Idiomaticity and Figurative Language Detection with Span Contrastive Loss
item.content:encoded: The category of figurative language contains many varieties, some of which are non-compositional in nature. This type of phrase or multi-word expression (MWE) includes idioms, which represent a single meaning that does not consist of the sum of its words. For language models, this presents a unique problem due to tokenization and adjacent contextual embeddings. Many large language models have overcome this issue with large phrase vocabulary, though immediate recognition frequently fails without one- or few-shot prompting or instruction finetuning. The best results have been achieved with BERT-based or LSTM finetuning approaches. The model in this paper contains one such variety. We propose BERT- and RoBERTa-based models finetuned with a combination of slot loss and span contrastive loss (SCL) with hard negative reweighting to improve idiomaticity detection, attaining state of the art sequence accuracy performance on existing datasets. Comparative ablation studies show the effectiveness of SCL and its generalizability. The geometric mean of F1 and sequence accuracy (SA) is also proposed to assess a model's span awareness and general performance together.

item.title: Cluster-Wise Spatio-Temporal Masking for Efficient Video-Language Pretraining
item.content:encoded: Large-scale video-language pretraining enables strong generalization across multimodal tasks but often incurs prohibitive computational costs. Although recent advances in masked visual modeling help mitigate this issue, they still suffer from two fundamental limitations: severe visual information loss under high masking ratios and temporal information leakage caused by inter-frame correlations. To address these challenges, we propose ClusterSTM, a Cluster-Wise Spatio-Temporal Masking strategy for efficient video-language pretraining. ClusterSTM first performs intra-frame clustering to partition visual tokens into multiple semantically independent clusters, then conducts cluster-wise masking by retaining the token with the highest temporal density within each cluster. Our masking strategy ensure that the retained tokens capture holistic video content while exhibit strong temporal correlation. Additionally, we introduce a video-text relevance reconstruction objective that aligns high-level multimodal semantics beyond conventional visual reconstruction. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ClusterSTM achieves superior performance on video-text retrieval, video question answering, and video captioning tasks, establishing a new state-of-the-art among efficient video-language models.

item.title: KARMA: Knowledge-Action Regularized Multimodal Alignment for Personalized Search at Taobao
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) are equipped with profound semantic knowledge, making them a natural choice for injecting semantic generalization into personalized search systems. However, in practice we find that directly fine-tuning LLMs on industrial personalized tasks (e.g. next item prediction) often yields suboptimal results. We attribute this bottleneck to a critical Knowledge--Action Gap: the inherent conflict between preserving pre-trained semantic knowledge and aligning with specific personalized actions by discriminative objectives. Empirically, action-only training objectives induce Semantic Collapse, such as attention ``sinks''. This degradation severely cripples the LLM's generalization, failing to bring improvements to personalized search systems. We propose KARMA (Knowledge--Action Regularized Multimodal Alignment), a unified framework that treats semantic reconstruction as a train-only regularizer. KARMA optimizes a next-interest embedding for retrieval (Action) while enforcing semantic decodability (Knowledge) through two complementary objectives: (i) history-conditioned semantic generation, which anchors optimization to the LLM's native next-token distribution, and (ii) embedding-conditioned semantic reconstruction, which constrains the interest embedding to remain semantically recoverable. On Taobao search system, KARMA mitigates semantic collapse (attention-sink analysis) and improves both action metrics and semantic fidelity. In ablations, semantic decodability yields up to +22.5 HR@200. With KARMA, we achieve +0.25 CTR AUC in ranking, +1.86 HR in pre-ranking and +2.51 HR in recalling. Deployed online with low inference overhead at ranking stage, KARMA drives +0.5% increase in Item Click.

item.title: Parametric Knowledge and Retrieval Behavior in RAG Fine-Tuning for Electronic Design Automation
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) fine-tuning has shown substantial improvements over vanilla RAG, yet most studies target document question answering and often rely on standard NLP metrics that can obscure factual differences. We evaluate RAG fine-tuning for long-form text generation in electronic design automation, adapting a 7B model under five context augmentation strategies with varying retrieval conditions. We introduce TriFEX, a human-validated, triple-based evaluation pipeline that attributes generated claims to their origin-user query, context and reference-and propose Parametric Knowledge Precision (PKP), which isolates internalized knowledge by filtering out claims leaked in the prompt. We show that ROUGE and BERTScore fail to detect factual differences that our triple-based evaluation reveals. Additionally, we demonstrate that an existing metric for knowledge internalization is retrieva-sensitive, with about 75% of its cross-condition variance driven by changes in the rate at which internal knowledge is expressed (PR), rather than by changes in its actual correctness (PKP). The fine-tuned 7B variants outperform a 72B baseline on most metrics, further showing generalization across conditions and on a related benchmark. These results underscore the limitations of available metrics in RAG evaluation and show that smaller models could be reasonably well adapted to specialized tasks for cost-efficient, on-premises deployment.

item.title: DA-Flow: Degradation-Aware Optical Flow Estimation with Diffusion Models
item.content:encoded: Optical flow models trained on high-quality data often degrade severely when confronted with real-world corruptions such as blur, noise, and compression artifacts. To overcome this limitation, we formulate Degradation-Aware Optical Flow, a new task targeting accurate dense correspondence estimation from real-world corrupted videos. Our key insight is that the intermediate representations of image restoration diffusion models are inherently corruption-aware but lack temporal awareness. To address this limitation, we lift the model to attend across adjacent frames via full spatio-temporal attention, and empirically demonstrate that the resulting features exhibit zero-shot correspondence capabilities. Based on this finding, we present DA-Flow, a hybrid architecture that fuses these diffusion features with convolutional features within an iterative refinement framework. DA-Flow substantially outperforms existing optical flow methods under severe degradation across multiple benchmarks.

item.title: Multimodal Industrial Anomaly Detection via Geometric Prior
item.content:encoded: The purpose of multimodal industrial anomaly detection is to detect complex geometric shape defects such as subtle surface deformations and irregular contours that are difficult to detect in 2D-based methods. However, current multimodal industrial anomaly detection lacks the effective use of crucial geometric information like surface normal vectors and 3D shape topology, resulting in low detection accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel Geometric Prior-based Anomaly Detection network (GPAD). Firstly, we propose a point cloud expert model to perform fine-grained geometric feature extraction, employing differential normal vector computation to enhance the geometric details of the extracted features and generate geometric prior. Secondly, we propose a two-stage fusion strategy to efficiently leverage the complementarity of multimodal data as well as the geometric prior inherent in 3D points. We further propose attention fusion and anomaly regions segmentation based on geometric prior, which enhance the model's ability to perceive geometric defects. Extensive experiments show that our multimodal industrial anomaly detection model outperforms the State-of-the-art (SOTA) methods in detection accuracy on both MVTec-3D AD and Eyecandies datasets.

item.title: Describe-Then-Act: Proactive Agent Steering via Distilled Language-Action World Models
item.content:encoded: Deploying safety-critical agents requires anticipating the consequences of actions before they are executed. While world models offer a paradigm for this proactive foresight, current approaches relying on visual simulation incur prohibitive latencies, often exceeding several seconds per step. In this work, we challenge the assumption that visual processing is necessary for failure prevention. We show that a trained policy's latent state, combined with its planned actions, already encodes sufficient information to anticipate action outcomes, making visual simulation redundant for failure prevention. To this end, we introduce DILLO (DIstiLLed Language-ActiOn World Model), a fast steering layer that shifts the paradigm from "simulate-then-act" to "describe-then-act." DILLO is trained via cross-modal distillation, where a privileged Vision Language Model teacher annotates offline trajectories and a latent-conditioned Large Language Model student learns to predict semantic outcomes. This creates a text-only inference path, bypassing heavy visual generation entirely, achieving a 14x speedup over baselines. Experiments on MetaWorld and LIBERO demonstrate that DILLO produces high-fidelity descriptions of the next state and is able to steer the policy, improving episode success rate by up to 15 pp and 9.3 pp on average across tasks.

item.title: Balancing Safety and Efficiency in Aircraft Health Diagnosis: A Task Decomposition Framework with Heterogeneous Long-Micro Scale Cascading and Knowledge Distillation-based Interpretability
item.content:encoded: Whole-aircraft diagnosis for general aviation faces threefold challenges: data uncertainty, task heterogeneity, and computational inefficiency. Existing end-to-end approaches uniformly model health discrimination and fault characterization, overlooking intrinsic receptive field conflicts between global context modeling and local feature extraction, while incurring prohibitive training costs under severe class imbalance. To address these, this study proposes the Diagnosis Decomposition Framework (DDF), explicitly decoupling diagnosis into Anomaly Detection (AD) and Fault Classification (FC) subtasks via the Long-Micro Scale Diagnostician (LMSD). Employing a "long-range global screening and micro-scale local precise diagnosis" strategy, LMSD utilizes Convolutional Tokenizer with Multi-Head Self-Attention (ConvTokMHSA) for global operational pattern discrimination and Multi-Micro Kernel Network (MMK Net) for local fault feature extraction. Decoupled training separates "large-sample lightweight" and "small-sample complex" optimization pathways, significantly reducing computational overhead. Concurrently, Keyness Extraction Layer (KEL) via knowledge distillation furnishes physically traceable explanations for two-stage decisions, materializing interpretability-by-design. Experiments on the NGAFID real-world aviation dataset demonstrate approximately 4-8% improvement in Multi-Class Weighted Penalty Metric (MCWPM) over baselines with substantially reduced training time, validating comprehensive advantages in task adaptability, interpretability, and efficiency. This provides a deployable methodology for general aviation health management.

item.title: UniGRPO: Unified Policy Optimization for Reasoning-Driven Visual Generation
item.content:encoded: Unified models capable of interleaved generation have emerged as a promising paradigm, with the community increasingly converging on autoregressive modeling for text and flow matching for image generation. To advance this direction, we propose a unified reinforcement learning framework tailored for interleaved generation. We validate our approach on its fundamental unit: a single round of reasoning-driven image generation, where the model first expands the user prompt through reasoning, followed by image synthesis. Formulating this multimodal generation process as a Markov Decision Process with sparse terminal rewards, we introduce UniGRPO to jointly optimize text and image generation policies using GRPO. Adopting a minimalist methodology to avoid over-design, we leverage established training recipes for both modalities by seamlessly integrating standard GRPO for reasoning and FlowGRPO for visual synthesis. To ensure scalability to multi-round interleaved generation, we introduce two critical modifications to the original FlowGRPO: (1) eliminating classifier-free guidance to maintain linear, unbranched rollouts, which is essential for scaling to complex scenarios involving multi-turn interactions and multi-condition generation (e.g., editing); and (2) replacing the standard latent KL penalty with an MSE penalty directly on the velocity fields, providing a more robust and direct regularization signal to mitigate reward hacking effectively. Our experiments demonstrate that this unified training recipe significantly enhances image generation quality through reasoning, providing a robust and scalable baseline for the future post-training of fully interleaved models.

item.title: Towards The Implicit Bias on Multiclass Separable Data Under Norm Constraints
item.content:encoded: Implicit bias induced by gradient-based algorithms is essential to the generalization of overparameterized models, yet its mechanisms can be subtle. This work leverages the Normalized Steepest Descent} (NSD) framework to investigate how optimization geometry shapes solutions on multiclass separable data. We introduce NucGD, a geometry-aware optimizer designed to enforce low rank structures through nuclear norm constraints. Beyond the algorithm itself, we connect NucGD with emerging low-rank projection methods, providing a unified perspective. To enable scalable training, we derive an efficient SVD-free update rule via asynchronous power iteration. Furthermore, we empirically dissect the impact of stochastic optimization dynamics, characterizing how varying levels of gradient noise induced by mini-batch sampling and momentum modulate the convergence toward the expected maximum margin solutions.Our code is accessible at: https://github.com/Tsokarsic/observing-the-implicit-bias-on-multiclass-seperable-data.

item.title: From Synthetic to Native: Benchmarking Multilingual Intent Classification in Logistics Customer Service
item.content:encoded: Multilingual intent classification is central to customer-service systems on global logistics platforms, where models must process noisy user queries across languages and hierarchical label spaces. Yet most existing multilingual benchmarks rely on machine-translated text, which is typically cleaner and more standardized than native customer requests and can therefore overestimate real-world robustness. We present a public benchmark for hierarchical multilingual intent classification constructed from real logistics customer-service logs. The dataset contains approximately 30K de-identified, stand-alone user queries curated from 600K historical records through filtering, LLM-assisted quality control, and human verification, and is organized into a two-level taxonomy with 13 parent and 17 leaf intents. English, Spanish, and Arabic are included as seen languages, while Indonesian, Chinese, and additional test-only languages support zero-shot evaluation. To directly measure the gap between synthetic and real evaluation, we provide paired native and machine-translated test sets and benchmark multilingual encoders, embedding models, and small language models under flat and hierarchical protocols. Results show that translated test sets substantially overestimate performance on noisy native queries, especially for long-tail intents and cross-lingual transfer, underscoring the need for more realistic multilingual intent benchmarks.

item.title: 基于提示工程的合成数据生成方法在知识注入中的应用
item.content:encoded:
SPA方法通过精心设计的提示生成大量合成数据，提升知识注入效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为SPA（Scaling Prompt-engineered Augmentation）的简单但高效的合成数据生成方法，用于知识注入。该方法利用少量精心设计的提示生成大规模合成数据，实验表明其优于多种现有基线方法。研究还指出，基于强化学习的方法在小规模数据中表现良好，但在大规模数据中会出现多样性崩溃问题；而多阶段提示方法虽在初期表现优异，但经过精细调优后优势消失。结果表明，结合精心设计的提示与大规模增强策略在知识注入中具有显著效果，SPA可作为未来研究的重要基准。

item.title: UniMotion：统一的人体动作、自然语言与RGB图像处理框架
item.content:encoded:
UniMotion是首个统一处理人体动作、自然语言和RGB图像的框架。



摘要： UniMotion是首个将人体动作、自然语言和RGB图像在同一架构中进行理解和生成的统一框架。它解决了现有模型仅处理有限模态组合以及依赖离散标记化导致量化误差和时间连续性破坏的问题。通过将动作视为与RGB同等的连续模态，UniMotion引入了Cross-Modal Aligned Motion VAE（CMA-VAE）和对称双路径嵌入器，构建了并行的连续路径。此外，通过Dual-Posterior KL Alignment（DPA）和Latent Reconstruction Alignment（LRA）技术，提升了模型在无图像输入情况下的视觉语义先验注入能力和冷启动问题的解决效果。UniMotion在七个跨模态任务中取得领先性能，尤其在跨模态组合任务中表现突出。

item.title: RAG系统安全分析与防御技术综述
item.content:encoded:
RAG系统存在多种安全威胁，本文系统分析并提出防御方案。



摘要： 本文针对检索增强生成（RAG）系统中存在的安全漏洞进行深入分析，识别了数据污染、对抗攻击和成员推断攻击等核心威胁。从输入和输出两个阶段出发，总结了数据保护和泄露预防的防御技术，包括动态访问控制、同态加密检索、联邦学习隔离等方法。同时，文章构建了一个统一的基准测试体系，整合权威数据集、安全标准和评估框架。作为首个针对RAG系统安全的全面综述，本文不仅系统梳理了威胁模型和防御机制，还为未来研究提供了统一的分析视角和实验基础。

item.title: WorldCache：提升视频世界模型推理速度的动态特征缓存方法
item.content:encoded:
WorldCache通过动态特征重用提升视频生成速度，保持高质量。



摘要： 本文提出WorldCache，一种感知约束的动态特征缓存框架，旨在优化Diffusion Transformers（DiTs）在视频生成中的推理效率。传统方法依赖静态特征重用，导致动态场景中出现模糊和运动不一致问题。WorldCache引入运动自适应阈值、显著性加权漂移估计、混合与变形优化以及阶段感知阈值调度，实现更准确的特征重用。实验表明，在Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B数据集上，WorldCache实现了2.3倍的推理加速，同时保持99.4%的基线质量，优于现有无训练缓存方法。

item.title: 基于神经高度场的折射表面视图合成方法
item.content:encoded:
RefacGS通过解耦折射表面与场景，实现高质量视图合成。



摘要： 本文提出RefacGS，一种用于非平面折射表面视图合成的新框架。传统方法如NeRF和3DGS在光线直线传播假设下表现优异，但在存在复杂折射时会产生严重失真。RefacGS通过将折射表面建模为神经高度场，并将场景表示为3D高斯场，实现了对折射边界与目标物体的显式解耦。该方法引入了考虑折射的高斯光线追踪算法，利用斯涅尔定律计算非线性光线轨迹，并高效渲染底层高斯场。通过端到端联合优化，RefacGS在合成和真实场景中均表现出优于现有方法的视觉质量，且训练速度提升15倍，支持实时200帧每秒渲染。

item.title: Gumbel Distillation提升并行语言模型生成质量
item.content:encoded:
Gumbel Distillation提升并行语言模型的生成质量。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Gumbel Distillation的新颖蒸馏技术，用于提升并行解码器对词序列联合分布的学习能力。该方法利用Gumbel-Max技巧，从潜在的Gumbel噪声空间映射到高性能自回归教师模型的输出标记。作为一种与模型无关的技术，Gumbel Distillation可无缝集成到多种并行解码架构中，如MDLM和BD3-LM。实验结果显示，在LM1B和OpenWebText数据集上，该方法显著提升了并行语言模型的生成质量，MAUVE分数提升30.0%，生成困惑度降低10.5%。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于不确定性引导的超球视觉语言模型方法UNCHA
item.content:encoded:
UNCHA提升超球视觉语言模型的层次结构建模能力。



摘要： 本文提出UNCHA方法，通过引入不确定性引导机制增强超球视觉语言模型（Hyperbolic VLMs）对部分与整体关系的建模能力。UNCHA利用超球不确定性对部分的语义代表性进行建模，将更具代表性的部分赋予更低的不确定性，并在对比目标函数中引入不确定性加权。此外，通过熵正则化的蕴含损失进一步校准不确定性。该方法提升了图像中复杂多对象场景的理解能力，在零样本分类、检索和多标签分类任务中取得了最佳性能。

item.title: LoRA$^2：自适应调整微调秩以优化扩散模型个性化
item.content:encoded:
LoRA$^2通过自适应调整秩实现更优的模型微调。



摘要： 本文提出LoRA$^2方法，通过在微调过程中动态调整各层的秩，解决传统LoRA固定秩带来的性能与内存消耗之间的权衡问题。该方法借鉴变分方法的思想，根据每一层的重要性分配不同的秩，从而在29个个性化主题上实现了与DINO、CLIP-I和CLIP-T相当的性能，同时显著降低内存占用和秩值。实验表明，LoRA$^2在保持高质量生成的同时提升了效率。

item.content:encoded:
提出DEBIR方法提升突发图像修复质量。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于动态曝光的突发图像修复方法（DEBIR），通过预测适合拍摄环境的曝光时间来提升图像恢复质量。该方法包含Burst Auto-Exposure Network (BAENet)，用于根据预览图像、运动幅度和增益估计最佳曝光时间，随后利用突发图像恢复网络生成高质量图像。作者还引入了可微分的突发模拟器和三阶段训练策略，并在真实相机系统上验证了方法的有效性。

提出一种轻量级AI图像检测方法，提升检测效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需训练的AI生成图像检测方法，通过测量图像对结构化频率扰动的敏感性来检测细微篡改。该方法仅需一次傅里叶变换即可生成扰动，计算效率高，推理速度比大多数无训练检测器快一到两个数量级。在多个基准测试中，该方法表现出色，尤其在OpenFake数据集上，AUC指标相比现有最佳方法提升了近10%，同时保持较低的计算成本。

item.title: 跨场景图像去雨适应框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需配对数据的跨场景去雨方法，提升模型泛化能力。



摘要： 图像去雨在低级计算机视觉中具有重要作用，尤其在户外监控和自动驾驶系统中。尽管深度学习在对齐数据中表现优异，但在未见的分布外场景中性能下降严重。本文提出一种创新的跨场景去雨适应框架，无需目标域的带雨图像，仅使用无雨背景图像进行训练。通过超像素生成模块提取结构先验，并利用分辨率自适应融合策略实现源域结构与目标背景的对齐，最后采用多阶段噪声生成模拟真实雨条纹。实验表明，该方法在分布外场景中提升了32%至59%的PSNR，同时加快了训练收敛速度。

item.title: 基于状态依赖扰动的模拟到现实控制策略训练方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型模拟到现实控制策略训练方法，提升机器人运动鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种替代现有模拟到现实（sim-to-real）方法的新方案，用于通过模拟经验训练控制策略。与以往依赖固定参数集域随机化的方法不同，该方法在正向仿真中注入状态依赖的扰动，以更广泛地模拟现实差距，且无需额外训练。通过使用神经网络作为灵活的扰动生成器，该方法能够表示复杂的、状态依赖的不确定性，如非线性执行器动力学和接触柔顺性，这些是传统参数随机化无法捕捉的。实验结果表明，该方法使拟人机器人运动策略在模拟和真实部署中都能表现出更强的鲁棒性，应对复杂未知的现实差距。

SparseDVFS通过稀疏性优化提升边缘设备推理能效。

提出一种MoE架构优化框架，提升资源分配效率。



摘要： 本文研究了大规模语言模型中混合专家（MoE）架构的优化问题。现有研究受限于实验预算，难以准确建立资源分配与架构配置之间的关系。作者提出了一种可复用的框架，通过引入FLOPs、活跃参数和总参数的联合约束，有效缩小了16维的搜索空间，并验证了其在多个计算规模下的有效性。结果表明，随着模型规模增大，最优配置范围变宽，为实际部署提供了更多灵活性。

item.title: 无配准高光谱与多光谱图像融合方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无监督框架，同时提升HSI和MSI的分辨率。



摘要： 本文研究了在无配准条件下高光谱图像（HSI）与多光谱图像（MSI）的融合问题。HSI具有高光谱分辨率但低空间分辨率，而MSI则相反。现有方法大多仅关注MSI超分辨率，未充分考虑HSI的提升。本文提出一种无监督框架，结合耦合光谱解混与潜在空间对抗学习，同时实现HSI和MSI的超分辨率。理论分析表明，在合理生成模型下，该方法可有效恢复图像分辨率，为无配准HMF提供了首个理论支持。实验验证了该方法在多种条件下的有效性。

通过代理角色实现AI在科学领域的自适应解释，提升理解与决策效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于强化学习的科学解释生成方法，引入代理角色（agentic personas）来模拟专家的推理策略，使AI能够根据不同的认知需求和知识立场生成自适应解释。在药物发现领域进行的实验表明，该方法在保持预测性能的同时，显著提升了解释的适应性与用户满意度，并大幅减少了对人类反馈的依赖。这种方法为复杂高风险领域中的AI可解释性提供了可扩展的解决方案。

item.content:encoded:
提出MISApp框架提升用户下一应用预测准确性。



摘要： 本文针对移动应用预测中的挑战，提出了一种无需用户画像的多跳会话图学习框架MISApp。该框架通过构建多跳会话图来捕捉不同结构范围内的转移依赖，利用轻量级图传播学习会话表示，并结合时间和空间上下文以刻画会话状态，同时从最近交互中捕捉意图演变。实验表明，MISApp在标准和冷启动设置下均优于现有基线方法，在预测准确性和实际效率之间取得良好平衡。进一步分析显示，学习到的跳跃级别注意力权重与结构相关性高度一致，验证了多跳建模策略的有效性。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的文档级机器翻译优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出两阶段微调策略提升LLM在文档级翻译中的表现。



摘要： 文章探讨了大语言模型（LLM）在机器翻译中的应用，指出其虽在句级任务中表现不佳，但在文档级翻译中具有建模上下文的优势。然而，面临数据稀缺和生成过程中易产生幻觉与遗漏的问题。为此，研究提出一种两阶段微调策略，首先通过LLM增强文档级数据并进行多指标过滤，再结合句级MT资源进行分阶段训练，以提升翻译质量与连贯性。

item.title: 基于多尺度网络的视网膜血管分割方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出HMS-VesselNet提升视网膜薄血管分割精度。



摘要： 本文针对传统视网膜血管分割方法在识别细小外围血管时表现不佳的问题，提出一种基于多尺度网络的HMS-VesselNet模型。该模型通过四个并行分支在不同分辨率下处理眼底图像，并利用学习到的融合权重结合输出结果。训练损失函数结合Dice、二元交叉熵和中心线Dice，以优化血管重叠区域和连续性。实验表明，该模型在多个数据集上取得了较高的Dice系数、灵敏度和AUC值，特别是在提升细小外围血管的召回率方面有显著改进，对糖尿病视网膜病变的早期检测具有重要意义。

item.title: 基于视觉的多模态RAG系统在眼科临床问答中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出Oph-Guid-RAG系统提升眼科临床决策支持效果。



摘要： 本文提出Oph-Guid-RAG，一个用于眼科临床问答和决策支持的多模态视觉RAG系统。该系统将每页指南视为独立证据单元，直接检索图像以保留表格、流程图等信息，并设计可控检索框架以减少噪声。系统整合查询分解、重写、检索、重排序和多模态推理，提供可追溯的输出。在HealthBench上的评估显示，与GPT-5.2相比，该方法在困难子集上的总体得分从0.2969提升至0.3861，准确率从0.5956提升至0.6576；与GPT-5.4相比，准确率提升更大。消融实验表明，重排序、路由和检索设计对系统稳定性至关重要。研究展示了结合视觉检索与可控推理在临床AI中的优势，但也指出仍需进一步改进。

QBET框架提升量子模型筛选效率。

item.title: 大型语言模型记忆行为的跨系列分析
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示LLM记忆行为的共性与差异。



摘要： 本文通过分析多个大型语言模型（如Pythia、OpenLLaMa、StarCoder等）的记忆行为，探讨其在统计和内部层面的共性和差异。研究发现，记忆率随模型规模呈对数线性增长，且记忆序列可进一步压缩。此外，不同模型在记忆序列的频率和领域分布上存在共性，但也有各自特征。在内部机制方面，模型能去除部分扰动，但记忆序列更敏感。通过解码中间层和注意力头分析，揭示了通用解码过程及关键注意力头，但不同家族模型的关键头分布存在差异。该研究为理解LLM的记忆机制提供了新视角。

item.title: 基于多视角的叙事相似性预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
通过多模型合奏提升叙事相似性预测准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了叙事相似性预测作为一项本质上具有解释性的任务，不同解读可能导致不同的相似性判断。为应对这一挑战，作者提出将多视角纳入预测系统决策过程。通过构建31个LLM角色的集合，在SemEval-2026 Task 4数据集上取得0.705的准确率。实验表明，随着集合规模增大，准确率提升，符合类似Condorcet Jury定理的动态。实践者角色个体表现较差，但误差相关性低，多数投票下提升更显著。错误分析显示，以性别为导向的解释词汇与准确率呈负相关，暗示评估框架需考虑解释多样性。

item.title: DualCoT-VLA：提升多步骤机器人任务的视觉-语言-动作模型
item.content:encoded:
DualCoT-VLA通过并行推理提升复杂任务处理能力。



摘要： 本文提出DualCoT-VLA，一种结合视觉和语言链式思维的视觉-语言-动作模型，旨在解决传统VLA模型在处理复杂多步骤任务时的不足。该模型引入并行推理机制，同时整合视觉链式思维用于低层次空间理解，以及语言链式思维用于高层次任务规划，有效提升模型对精细操作和逻辑推理的能力。实验表明，DualCoT-VLA在多个基准测试中表现优异，具有更高的效率和更低的延迟。

item.title: StreamingClaw：面向具身智能的实时视频理解框架
item.content:encoded:
StreamingClaw解决实时视频理解与具身智能中的关键问题。



摘要： 本文提出StreamingClaw，一个统一的代理框架，用于实时视频理解和具身智能。该框架解决了现有系统在实时推理、多模态长期记忆和主动交互方面的不足。它具备五个核心能力：实时流式推理、未来事件推理与主动交互、多模态长期存储与高效检索、感知-决策-行动闭环，以及与OpenClaw框架兼容。通过将决策转化为可执行动作，StreamingClaw实现了对物理世界的直接控制，支持具身交互的实际部署。

item.title: 基于残差加速度场学习的软体机器人模拟优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出RAFL框架提升软体机器人模拟精度与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为残差加速度场学习（RAFL）的框架，用于增强基础模拟器的动态建模能力。该框架通过引入可迁移的、基于单元的校正动力学场，能够适应不同的几何结构，并在共享局部特征的基础上不依赖全局网格拓扑。该模型通过稀疏标记观测进行端到端训练，在模拟到模拟和模拟到现实的实验中均表现出对未见形态的零样本改进，优于传统系统识别方法。此外，该框架支持持续优化，使模拟精度在形态优化过程中逐步提升。

item.title: 优化移动应用中特征提取以降低模型执行延迟
item.content:encoded:
通过优化特征提取提升移动设备模型执行效率。



摘要： 本文研究了移动应用中模型执行的瓶颈，发现从原始日志中提取输入特征是影响性能的关键因素。为此，作者提出AutoFeature系统，通过图抽象、图优化和高效缓存技术，减少冗余操作，提升特征提取速度。实验结果显示，在多个工业级移动服务中，AutoFeature显著降低了模型执行延迟，白天和夜间分别提升了1.33倍至3.93倍和1.43倍至4.53倍。

item.title: 视觉隐私风险的组合性评估框架与模型优化
item.content:encoded:
提出组合隐私风险分类体系，提升隐私评估准确性。



摘要： 现有视觉隐私基准多将隐私视为二元属性，基于可见敏感内容进行标记。本文认为隐私本质上具有组合性，单独无害的属性可能组合后造成严重隐私泄露。为此，提出组合隐私风险分类体系（CPRT），根据属性的独立可识别性和组合危害潜力进行分类，并定义四个等级的严重程度。同时设计了一个可解释的评分函数，用于分配连续隐私严重度分数。研究构建了包含6.7K图像的数据集并生成真实组合风险分数。实验发现，前沿模型在结构化引导下能较好匹配组合严重性，但系统性低估组合风险；小型模型难以内化分级隐私推理。为此，引入一个8B参数的监督微调模型，以接近前沿模型的组合隐私评估性能。

item.title: CoRA: Boosting Time Series Foundation Models for Multivariate Forecasting through Correlation-aware Adapter
item.content:encoded: Most existing Time Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) use channel independent modeling and focus on capturing and generalizing temporal dependencies, while neglecting the correlations among channels or overlooking the different aspects of correlations. However, these correlations play a vital role in Multivariate time series forecasting. To address this, we propose a CoRrelation-aware Adapter (CoRA), a lightweight plug-and-play method that requires only fine-tuning with TSFMs and is able to capture different types of correlations, so as to improve forecast performance. Specifically, to reduce complexity, we innovatively decompose the correlation matrix into low-rank Time-Varying and Time-Invariant components. For the Time-Varying component, we further design learnable polynomials to learn dynamic correlations by capturing trends or periodic patterns. To learn positive and negative correlations that appear only among some channels, we introduce a novel dual contrastive learning method that identifies correlations through projection layers, regulated by a Heterogeneous-Partial contrastive loss during training, without introducing additional complexity in the inference stage. Extensive experiments on 10 real-world datasets demonstrate that CoRA can improve TSFMs in multivariate forecasting performance.

item.title: SecureBreak -- A dataset towards safe and secure models
item.content:encoded: Large language models are becoming pervasive core components in many real-world applications. As a consequence, security alignment represents a critical requirement for their safe deployment. Although previous related works focused primarily on model architectures and alignment methodologies, these approaches alone cannot ensure the complete elimination of harmful generations. This concern is reinforced by the growing body of scientific literature showing that attacks, such as jailbreaking and prompt injection, can bypass existing security alignment mechanisms. As a consequence, additional security strategies are needed both to provide qualitative feedback on the robustness of the obtained security alignment at the training stage, and to create an ``ultimate'' defense layer to block unsafe outputs possibly produced by deployed models. To provide a contribution in this scenario, this paper introduces SecureBreak, a safety-oriented dataset designed to support the development of AI-driven solutions for detecting harmful LLM outputs caused by residual weaknesses in security alignment. The dataset is highly reliable due to careful manual annotation, where labels are assigned conservatively to ensure safety. It performs well in detecting unsafe content across multiple risk categories. Tests with pre-trained LLMs show improved results after fine-tuning on SecureBreak. Overall, the dataset is useful both for post-generation safety filtering and for guiding further model alignment and security improvements.

item.title: ThinkJEPA: Empowering Latent World Models with Large Vision-Language Reasoning Model
item.content:encoded: Recent progress in latent world models (e.g., V-JEPA2) has shown promising capability in forecasting future world states from video observations. Nevertheless, dense prediction from a short observation window limits temporal context and can bias predictors toward local, low-level extrapolation, making it difficult to capture long-horizon semantics and reducing downstream utility. Vision--language models (VLMs), in contrast, provide strong semantic grounding and general knowledge by reasoning over uniformly sampled frames, but they are not ideal as standalone dense predictors due to compute-driven sparse sampling, a language-output bottleneck that compresses fine-grained interaction states into text-oriented representations, and a data-regime mismatch when adapting to small action-conditioned datasets. We propose a VLM-guided JEPA-style latent world modeling framework that combines dense-frame dynamics modeling with long-horizon semantic guidance via a dual-temporal pathway: a dense JEPA branch for fine-grained motion and interaction cues, and a uniformly sampled VLM \emph{thinker} branch with a larger temporal stride for knowledge-rich guidance. To transfer the VLM's progressive reasoning signals effectively, we introduce a hierarchical pyramid representation extraction module that aggregates multi-layer VLM representations into guidance features compatible with latent prediction. Experiments on hand-manipulation trajectory prediction show that our method outperforms both a strong VLM-only baseline and a JEPA-predictor baseline, and yields more robust long-horizon rollout behavior.

item.title: Timing In stand-up Comedy: Text, Audio, Laughter, Kinesics (TIC-TALK): Pipeline and Database for the Multimodal Study of Comedic Timing
item.content:encoded: Stand-up comedy, and humor in general, are often studied through their verbal content. Yet live performance relies just as much on embodied presence and audience feedback. We introduce TIC-TALK, a multimodal resource with 5,400+ temporally aligned topic segments capturing language, gesture, and audience response across 90 professionally filmed stand-up comedy specials (2015-2024). The pipeline combines BERTopic for 60 s thematic segmentation with dense sentence embeddings, Whisper-AT for 0.8 s laughter detection, a fine-tuned YOLOv8-cls shot classifier, and YOLOv8s-pose for raw keypoint extraction at 1 fps. Raw 17-joint skeletal coordinates are retained without prior clustering, enabling the computation of continuous kinematic signals-arm spread, kinetic energy, and trunk lean-that serve as proxies for performance dynamics. All streams are aligned by hierarchical temporal containment without resampling, and each topic segment stores its sentence-BERT embedding for downstream similarity and clustering tasks. As a concrete use case, we study laughter dynamics across 24 thematic topics: kinetic energy negatively predicts audience laughter rate (r = -0.75, N = 24), consistent with a stillness-before-punchline pattern; personal and bodily content elicits more laughter than geopolitical themes; and shot close-up proportion correlates positively with laughter (r = +0.28), consistent with reactive montage.

item.title: Back to Point: Exploring Point-Language Models for Zero-Shot 3D Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: Zero-shot (ZS) 3D anomaly detection is crucial for reliable industrial inspection, as it enables detecting and localizing defects without requiring any target-category training data. Existing approaches render 3D point clouds into 2D images and leverage pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for anomaly detection. However, such strategies inevitably discard geometric details and exhibit limited sensitivity to local anomalies. In this paper, we revisit intrinsic 3D representations and explore the potential of pre-trained Point-Language Models (PLMs) for ZS 3D anomaly detection. We propose BTP (Back To Point), a novel framework that effectively aligns 3D point cloud and textual embeddings. Specifically, BTP aligns multi-granularity patch features with textual representations for localized anomaly detection, while incorporating geometric descriptors to enhance sensitivity to structural anomalies. Furthermore, we introduce a joint representation learning strategy that leverages auxiliary point cloud data to improve robustness and enrich anomaly semantics. Extensive experiments on Real3D-AD and Anomaly-ShapeNet demonstrate that BTP achieves superior performance in ZS 3D anomaly detection. Code will be available at \href{https://github.com/wistful-8029/BTP-3DAD}{https://github.com/wistful-8029/BTP-3DAD}.

item.title: SARe: Structure-Aware Large-Scale 3D Fragment Reassembly
item.content:encoded: 3D fragment reassembly aims to recover the rigid poses of unordered fragment point clouds or meshes in a common object coordinate system to reconstruct the complete shape. The problem becomes particularly challenging as the number of fragments grows, since the target shape is unknown and fragments provide weak semantic cues. Existing end-to-end approaches are prone to cascading failures due to unreliable contact reasoning, most notably inaccurate fragment adjacencies. To address this, we propose Structure-Aware Reassembly (SARe), a generative framework with SARe-Gen for Euclidean-space assembly generation and SARe-Refine for inference-time refinement, with explicit contact modeling. SARe-Gen jointly predicts fracture-surface token probabilities and an inter-fragment contact graph to localize contact regions and infer candidate adjacencies. It adopts a query-point-based conditioning scheme and extracts aligned local geometric tokens at query locations from a frozen geometry encoder, yielding queryable structural representations without additional structural pretraining. We further introduce an inference-time refinement stage, SARe-Refine. By verifying candidate contact edges with geometric-consistency checks, it selects reliable substructures and resamples the remaining uncertain regions while keeping verified parts fixed, leading to more stable and consistent assemblies in the many-fragment regime. We evaluate SARe across three settings, including synthetic fractures, simulated fractures from scanned real objects, and real physically fractured scans. The results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, with more graceful degradation and higher success rates as the fragment count increases in challenging large-scale reassembly.

item.title: Triangulating Temporal Dynamics in Multilingual Swiss Online News
item.content:encoded: Analyzing news coverage in multilingual societies can offer valuable insights into the dynamics of public discourse and the development of collective narratives, yet comprehensive studies that account for linguistic and cultural diversity within national media ecosystems remain limited, particularly in complex contexts such as Switzerland. This paper studies temporal trends in Swiss digital media across the country's three main linguistic regions, French, German, and Italian, using a triangulated methodology that combines quantitative analyses with qualitative insights. We collected and processed over 1.7 million news articles, applying lexical metrics, named entity recognition and Wikidata-based linking, targeted sentiment analysis, and consensus-based change-point detection. To enable principled cross-language comparisons and to connect to theories of domestication and cultural proximity, we derive domestication profiles together with a proximity salience ratio. Our analysis spans thematic, recurrent, and singular events. By integrating quantitative data with qualitative interpretation, we provide new insights into the dynamics of Swiss digital media and demonstrate the usefulness of triangulation in media studies. The findings reveal distinct temporal patterns and highlight how linguistic and cultural contexts influence reporting. Our approach offers a framework applicable to other multilingual or culturally diverse media environments, contributing to a deeper understanding of how news is shaped by linguistic and cultural factors.

item.title: On the Interplay of Priors and Overparametrization in Bayesian Neural Network Posteriors
item.content:encoded: Bayesian neural network (BNN) posteriors are often considered impractical for inference, as symmetries fragment them, non-identifiabilities inflate dimensionality, and weight-space priors are seen as meaningless. In this work, we study how overparametrization and priors together reshape BNN posteriors and derive implications allowing us to better understand their interplay. We show that redundancy introduces three key phenomena that fundamentally reshape the posterior geometry: balancedness, weight reallocation on equal-probability manifolds, and prior conformity. We validate our findings through extensive experiments with posterior sampling budgets that far exceed those of earlier works, and demonstrate how overparametrization induces structured, prior-aligned weight posterior distributions.

item.title: Computationally lightweight classifiers with frequentist bounds on predictions
item.content:encoded: While both classical and neural network classifiers can achieve high accuracy, they fall short on offering uncertainty bounds on their predictions, making them unfit for safety-critical applications. Existing kernel-based classifiers that provide such bounds scale with $\mathcal O (n^{\sim3})$ in time, making them computationally intractable for large datasets. To address this, we propose a novel, computationally efficient classification algorithm based on the Nadaraya-Watson estimator, for whose estimates we derive frequentist uncertainty intervals. We evaluate our classifier on synthetically generated data and on electrocardiographic heartbeat signals from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia database. We show that the method achieves competitive accuracy $>$\SI{96}{\percent} at $\mathcal O(n)$ and $\mathcal O(\log n)$ operations, while providing actionable uncertainty bounds. These bounds can, e.g., aid in flagging low-confidence predictions, making them suitable for real-time settings with resource constraints, such as diagnostic monitoring or implantable devices.

item.title: The Presupposition Problem in Representation Genesis
item.content:encoded: Large language models are the first systems to achieve high cognitive performance without clearly undergoing representation genesis: the transition from a non-representing physical system to one whose states guide behavior in a content-sensitive way. Prior cognitive systems had already made this transition before we could examine it, and philosophy of mind treated genesis as a background condition rather than an explanatory target. LLMs provide a case that does not clearly involve this transition, making the genesis question newly urgent: if genesis did not occur, which cognitive capacities are affected, and why? We currently lack the conceptual resources to answer this. The reason, this paper argues, is structural. Major frameworks in philosophy of mind, including the Language of Thought hypothesis, teleosemantics, predictive processing, enactivism, and genetic phenomenology, share a common feature when applied to the genesis question: at some explanatory step, each deploys concepts whose explanatory purchase depends on the system already being organized as a representer. This pattern, which we call the Representation Presupposition structure, generates systematic explanatory deferral. Attempts to explain the first acquisition of content-manipulable representation within the existing categorical vocabulary import resources from the representational side of the transition itself. We call this the Representation Regress. The paper offers a conceptual diagnosis rather than a new theory, establishing the structure of the problem and deriving two minimum adequacy conditions for any account that avoids this pattern. LLMs make the absence of such a theory consequential rather than merely theoretical.

item.title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training
item.content:encoded: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) offer attractive advantages over Auto-Regressive (AR) models, such as full-attention parallel decoding and flexible generation. However, they suffer from a notable train-inference mismatch: DLMs are trained with a static, single-step masked prediction objective, but deployed through a multi-step progressive denoising trajectory. We propose MemDLM (Memory-Enhanced DLM), which narrows this gap by embedding a simulated denoising process into training via Bi-level Optimization. An inner loop updates a set of fast weights, forming a Parametric Memory that captures the local trajectory experience of each sample, while an outer loop updates the base model conditioned on this memory. By offloading memorization pressure from token representations to parameters, MemDLM yields faster convergence and lower training loss. Moreover, the inner loop can be re-enabled at inference time as an adaptation step, yielding additional gains on long-context understanding. We find that, when activated at inference time, this Parametric Memory acts as an emergent in-weight retrieval mechanism, helping MemDLM further reduce token-level attention bottlenecks on challenging Needle-in-a-Haystack retrieval tasks. Code: https://github.com/JarvisPei/MemDLM.

item.title: STENet: Superpixel Token Enhancing Network for RGB-D Salient Object Detection
item.content:encoded: Transformer-based methods for RGB-D Salient Object Detection (SOD) have gained significant interest, owing to the transformer's exceptional capacity to capture long-range pixel dependencies. Nevertheless, current RGB-D SOD methods face challenges, such as the quadratic complexity of the attention mechanism and the limited local detail extraction. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel Superpixel Token Enhancing Network (STENet), which introduces superpixels into cross-modal interaction. STENet follows the two-stream encoder-decoder structure. Its cores are two tailored superpixel-driven cross-modal interaction modules, responsible for global and local feature enhancement. Specifically, we update the superpixel generation method by expanding the neighborhood range of each superpixel, allowing for flexible transformation between pixels and superpixels. With the updated superpixel generation method, we first propose the Superpixel Attention Global Enhancing Module to model the global pixel-to-superpixel relationship rather than the traditional global pixel-to-pixel relationship, which can capture region-level information and reduce computational complexity. We also propose the Superpixel Attention Local Refining Module, which leverages pixel similarity within superpixels to filter out a subset of pixels (i.e., local pixels) and then performs feature enhancement on these local pixels, thereby capturing concerned local details. Furthermore, we fuse the globally and locally enhanced features along with the cross-scale features to achieve comprehensive feature representation. Experiments on seven RGB-D SOD datasets reveal that our STENet achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art methods. The code and results of our method are available at https://github.com/Mark9010/STENet.

item.title: Kolmogorov Complexity Bounds for LLM Steganography and a Perplexity-Based Detection Proxy
item.content:encoded: Large language models can rewrite text to embed hidden payloads while preserving surface-level meaning, a capability that opens covert channels between cooperating AI systems and poses challenges for alignment monitoring. We study the information-theoretic cost of such embedding. Our main result is that any steganographic scheme that preserves the semantic load of a covertext~$M_1$ while encoding a payload~$P$ into a stegotext~$M_2$ must satisfy $K(M_2) \geq K(M_1) + K(P) - O(\log n)$, where $K$ denotes Kolmogorov complexity and $n$ is the combined message length. A corollary is that any non-trivial payload forces a strict complexity increase in the stegotext, regardless of how cleverly the encoder distributes the signal. Because Kolmogorov complexity is uncomputable, we ask whether practical proxies can detect this predicted increase. Drawing on the classical correspondence between lossless compression and Kolmogorov complexity, we argue that language-model perplexity occupies an analogous role in the probabilistic regime and propose the Binoculars perplexity-ratio score as one such proxy. Preliminary experiments with a color-based LLM steganographic scheme support the theoretical prediction: a paired $t$-test over 300 samples yields $t = 5.11$, $p < 10^{-6}$.

item.title: Uncertainty Quantification for Distribution-to-Distribution Flow Matching in Scientific Imaging
item.content:encoded: Distribution-to-distribution generative models support scientific imaging tasks ranging from modeling cellular perturbation responses to translating medical images across conditions. Trustworthy generation requires both reliability (generalization across labs, devices, and experimental conditions) and accountability (detecting out-of-distribution cases where predictions may be unreliable). Uncertainty quantification (UQ) based approaches serve as promising candidates for these tasks, yet UQ for distribution-to-distribution generative models remains underexplored. We present a unified UQ framework, Bayesian Stochastic Flow Matching (BSFM), that disentangles aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty. The Stochastic Flow Matching (SFM) component augments deterministic flows with a diffusion term to improve model generalization to unseen scenarios. For UQ, we develop a scalable Bayesian approach -- MCD-Antithetic -- that combines Monte Carlo Dropout with sample-efficient antithetic sampling to produce effective anomaly scores for out-of-distribution detection. Experiments on cellular imaging (BBBC021, JUMP) and brain fMRI (Theory of Mind) across diverse scenarios show that SFM improves reliability while MCD-Antithetic enhances accountability.

item.title: Beyond Strict Pairing: Arbitrarily Paired Training for High-Performance Infrared and Visible Image Fusion
item.content:encoded: Infrared and visible image fusion(IVIF) combines complementary modalities while preserving natural textures and salient thermal signatures. Existing solutions predominantly rely on extensive sets of rigidly aligned image pairs for training. However, acquiring such data is often impractical due to the costly and labour-intensive alignment process. Besides, maintaining a rigid pairing setting during training restricts the volume of cross-modal relationships, thereby limiting generalisation performance. To this end, this work challenges the necessity of Strictly Paired Training Paradigm (SPTP) by systematically investigating UnPaired and Arbitrarily Paired Training Paradigms (UPTP and APTP) for high-performance IVIF. We establish a theoretical objective of APTP, reflecting the complementary nature between UPTP and SPTP. More importantly, we develop a practical framework capable of significantly enriching cross-modal relationships even with severely limited and unaligned training data. To validate our propositions, three end-to-end lightweight baselines, alongside a set of innovative loss functions, are designed to cover three classic frameworks (CNN, Transformer, GAN). Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed APTP and UPTP are feasible and capable of training models on a severely limited and content-inconsistent infrared and visible dataset, achieving performance comparable to that of a dataset 100$\times$ larger in SPTP. This finding fundamentally alleviates the cost and difficulty of data collection while enhancing model robustness from the data perspective, delivering a feasible solution for IVIF studies. The code is available at \href{https://github.com/yanglinDeng/IVIF_unpair}{\textcolor{blue}{https://github.com/yanglinDeng/IVIF\_unpair}}.

item.title: The Golden Subspace: Where Efficiency Meets Generalization in Continual Test-Time Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Continual Test-Time Adaptation (CTTA) aims to enable models to adapt online to unlabeled data streams under distribution shift without accessing source data. Existing CTTA methods face an efficiency-generalization trade-off: updating more parameters improves adaptation but severely reduces online inference efficiency. An ideal solution is to achieve comparable adaptation with minimal feature updates; we call this minimal subspace the golden subspace. We prove its existence in a single-step adaptation setting and show that it coincides with the row space of the pretrained classifier. To enable online maintenance of this subspace, we introduce the sample-wise Average Gradient Outer Product (AGOP) as an efficient proxy for estimating the classifier weights without retraining. Building on these insights, we propose Guided Online Low-rank Directional adaptation (GOLD), which uses a lightweight adapter to project features onto the golden subspace and learns a compact scaling vector while the subspace is dynamically updated via AGOP. Extensive experiments on classification and segmentation benchmarks, including autonomous-driving scenarios, demonstrate that GOLD attains superior efficiency, stability, and overall performance. Our code is available at https://github.com/AIGNLAI/GOLD.

item.title: GeoFlow: Real-Time Fine-Grained Cross-View Geolocalization via Iterative Flow Prediction
item.content:encoded: Accurate and fast localization is vital for safe autonomous navigation in GPS-denied areas. Fine-Grained Cross-View Geolocalization (FG-CVG) aims to estimate the precise 2-Degree-of-Freedom (2-DoF) location of a ground image relative to a satellite image. However, current methods force a difficult trade-off, with high-accuracy models being slow for real-time use. In this paper, we introduce GeoFlow, a new approach that offers a lightweight and highly efficient framework that breaks this accuracy-speed trade-off. Our technique learns a direct probabilistic mapping, predicting the displacement (in distance and direction) required to correct any given location hypothesis. This is complemented by our novel inference algorithm, Iterative Refinement Sampling (IRS). Instead of trusting a single prediction, IRS refines a population of hypotheses, allowing them to iteratively 'flow' from random starting points to a robust, converged consensus. Even its iterative nature, this approach offers flexible inference-time scaling, allowing a direct trade-off between performance and computation without any re-training. Experiments on the KITTI and VIGOR datasets show that GeoFlow achieves state-of-the-art efficiency, running at real-time speeds of 29 FPS while maintaining competitive localization accuracy. This work opens a new path for the development of practical real-time geolocalization systems.

item.title: PRISM: Breaking the O(n) Memory Wall in Long-Context LLM Inference via O(1) Photonic Block Selection
item.content:encoded: Long-context LLM inference is bottlenecked not by compute but by the O(n) memory bandwidth cost of scanning the KV cache at every decode step -- a wall that no amount of arithmetic scaling can break. Recent photonic accelerators have demonstrated impressive throughput for dense attention computation; however, these approaches inherit the same O(n) memory scaling as electronic attention when applied to long contexts. We observe that the real leverage point is the coarse block-selection step: a memory-bound similarity search that determines which KV blocks to fetch. We identify, for the first time, that this task is structurally matched to the photonic broadcast-and-weight paradigm -- the query fans out to all candidates via passive splitting, signatures are quasi-static (matching electro-optic MRR programming), and only rank order matters (relaxing precision to 4-6 bits). Crucially, the photonic advantage grows with context length: as N increases, the electronic scan cost rises linearly while the photonic evaluation remains O(1). We instantiate this insight in PRISM (Photonic Ranking via Inner-product Similarity with Microring weights), a thin-film lithium niobate (TFLN) similarity engine. Hardware-impaired needle-in-a-haystack evaluation on Qwen2.5-7B confirms 100% accuracy from 4K through 64K tokens at k=32, with 16x traffic reduction at 64K context. PRISM achieves a four-order-of-magnitude energy advantage over GPU baselines at practical context lengths (n >= 4K).

item.title: Do Papers Match Code? A Benchmark and Framework for Paper-Code Consistency Detection in Bioinformatics Software
item.content:encoded: Ensuring consistency between research papers and their corresponding software implementations is fundamental to software reliability and scientific reproducibility. However, this problem remains underexplored, particularly in the domain of bioinformatics, where discrepancies between methodological descriptions in papers and their actual code implementations are prevalent. To address this gap, this paper introduces a new task, namely paper-code consistency detection, and curates a collection of 48 bioinformatics software projects along with their associated publications. We systematically align sentence-level algorithmic descriptions from papers with function-level code snippets. Combined with expert annotations and a hybrid negative sampling strategy, we construct the first benchmark dataset in the bioinformatics domain tailored to this task, termed BioCon. Based on this benchmark, we further propose a cross-modal consistency detection framework designed to model the semantic relationships between natural language descriptions and code implementations. The framework adopts a unified input representation and leverages pre-trained models to capture deep semantic alignment between papers and code. To mitigate the effects of class imbalance and hard samples, we incorporate a weighted focal loss to enhance model robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework effectively identifies consistency between papers and code in bioinformatics, achieving an accuracy of 0.9056 and an F1 score of 0.8011. Overall, this study opens a new research direction for paper-code consistency analysis and lays the foundation for automated reproducibility assessment and cross-modal understanding in scientific software.

item.title: PEARL: Geometry Aligns Semantics for Training-Free Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Training-free open-vocabulary semantic segmentation (OVSS) promises rapid adaptation to new label sets without retraining. Yet, many methods rely on heavy post-processing or handle text and vision in isolation, leaving cross-modal geometry underutilized. Others introduce auxiliary vision backbones or multi-model pipelines, which increase complexity and latency while compromising design simplicity. We present PEARL, \textbf{\underline{P}}rocrust\textbf{\underline{e}}s \textbf{\underline{a}}lignment with text-awa\textbf{\underline{r}}e \textbf{\underline{L}}aplacian propagation, a compact two-step inference that follows an align-then-propagate principle. The Procrustes alignment step performs an orthogonal projection inside the last self-attention block, rotating keys toward the query subspace via a stable polar iteration. The text-aware Laplacian propagation then refines per-pixel logits on a small grid through a confidence-weighted, text-guided graph solve: text provides both a data-trust signal and neighbor gating, while image gradients preserve boundaries. In this work, our method is fully training-free, plug-and-play, and uses only fixed constants, adding minimal latency with a small per-head projection and a few conjugate-gradient steps. Our approach, PEARL, sets a new state-of-the-art in training-free OVSS without extra data or auxiliary backbones across standard benchmarks, achieving superior performance under both with-background and without-background protocols.

item.title: 基于图像块关系的零水印技术研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需修改原图的零水印方法，提升数字图像真实性验证能力。



摘要： 本文针对扩散模型对数字图像真实性的威胁，提出一种基于图像块关系的零水印技术（Rel-Zero）。该方法利用AI编辑过程中图像块对之间相对距离的不变性，构建无需修改原图的水印系统。与传统方法相比，Rel-Zero通过结构一致性而非外观特征进行内容认证，有效提升了对复杂编辑操作的鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法在多种编辑模型和操作下均表现出显著优势。

item.title: 基于序列学习与优化的不确定性决策方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新方法处理不确定决策中的偏好问题。



摘要： 本文针对决策问题中由于人类偏好异质性和情境依赖性带来的不确定性，提出了一种序列学习与优化的框架。该方法通过学习偏好分布，并将其用于下游风险规避型决策问题。研究聚焦于可建模为（整数）线性规划的人类选择场景，传统逆优化和选择建模方法通常只能得到点估计或无法捕捉情境变化，不适合风险规避决策。本文使用有界方差得分函数梯度估计器训练预测模型，将上下文特征映射到可参数化的分布类。该方法生成未见情境下的场景，用于后续优化。在合成拼车环境中，该方法相比风险中性方法和领先的风险规避基线，平均后决策惊讶度分别降低了114倍和25倍。

item.title: INTERPOL：一种用于模型识别的深度风格分析框架
item.content:encoded:
INTERPOL通过深度风格分析提升模型识别准确性，揭示投票排行榜的隐私漏洞。



摘要： 本文提出INTERPOL，一种基于模型驱动的识别框架，旨在克服传统方法在区分风格相似模型时的不足。该框架通过合成硬负样本和自适应课程学习策略，捕捉深层风格模式，从而显著提升模型识别准确率。实验表明，INTERPOL优于现有基线方法，并通过排名操纵模拟验证了其在真实场景中的威胁性。研究强调了模型响应匿名性对投票排行榜可靠性的重要性。

item.title: 基于多模态大语言模型的无参考图像质量评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出GT-PCQA框架提升图像质量评估性能。



摘要： 随着多模态大语言模型（MLLM）的快速发展，基于MLLM的图像质量评估（IQA）方法展现出良好的泛化能力。然而，直接将其应用于感知图像质量评估（PCQA）仍面临挑战。一方面，现有PCQA数据集规模有限，难以支持MLLM的有效指令微调；另一方面，由于大规模图文预训练，MLLM更依赖纹理推理，对PCQA中关键的几何结构退化不够敏感。为此，本文提出一种新的无参考PCQA框架GT-PCQA，包含两个关键策略：2D-3D联合训练策略和几何-纹理解耦策略。实验表明，GT-PCQA在性能和泛化能力上均表现出色。

item.title: 人工智能在司法决策中的应用与挑战
item.content:encoded:
AI在司法决策中提升效率但引发透明性与公平性争议。



摘要： 文章探讨了人工智能（AI）技术在司法决策中的应用，包括预审、量刑和假释等环节，分析了其在透明性、可靠性及责任归属方面的挑战。同时指出，AI的引入凸显了人类判断的局限性，并强调了法官与AI工具之间互动的重要性。研究聚焦于刑事司法风险评估，综合计算机科学、经济学、法律、犯罪学和心理学等多个领域的研究成果，发现AI对司法决策的影响有限，但仍存在诸多研究空白。文章呼吁加强跨学科合作，以更好地评估AI工具的表现并理解法官如何应对算法建议。

item.title: 非凸损失下的差分隐私无线联邦学习分析
item.content:encoded:
研究非凸损失下DPWFL的隐私与收敛性，提出隐私-效用平衡模型。



摘要： 本文针对差分隐私无线联邦学习（DPWFL）在非凸损失目标下的隐私损失和收敛性问题进行深入分析。现有研究受限于严格的凸性假设或忽略梯度裁剪的影响，而本文提出一种更通用的分析框架，结合设备选择和小批量采样，证明隐私损失可随迭代次数收敛至常数而非发散。同时，文章建立了梯度裁剪下的收敛保证，并推导出明确的隐私-效用权衡关系。实验结果验证了理论分析的正确性。

item.title: 基于最优传输的EPI几何失真校正方法SuCor
item.content:encoded:
SuCor通过最优传输校正EPI图像失真，提升配准精度。



摘要： 本文提出SuCor方法，利用最优传输（OT）沿相位编码方向校正回波平面成像（EPI）中的磁敏感引起的几何失真。该方法基于一对相反相位编码的EPI体积，将每个列的失真场建模为对抗极性强度分布之间的Wasserstein-2凸组合位移。在频域进行正则化，使用弯曲能量惩罚，并通过Morozov偏差原理自动选择惩罚强度，无需手动调参。在人类连接组计划（HCP）数据集上，SuCor与T1结构图像的平均体积互信息达到0.341，优于FSL TOPUP的0.317，且仅需单核CPU运行约12秒。

item.title: 基于几何感知的执行图任务调度优化研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种几何感知的路由风险估计方法，提升任务调度效率。



摘要： 本文研究了高级AI推理系统中符号图网络的架构模式，指出当前调度器在处理树状与循环结构中的故障传播时存在可观测性缺陷。作者提出了一种轻量级的几何感知控制方法，结合欧几里得时空传播基线、双曲路由风险模型和几何选择器，显著提升了任务调度的稳定性与成功率。实验表明，在Genesis 3基准测试中，该方法将最困难的非树状场景胜率从64%-72%提升至92%，整体胜率达87.2%。此外，该方法通过引入拓扑统计特征和几何信号，有效缓解了几何盲视导致的故障扩散问题。

item.title: 基于4D雷达与视觉融合的3D目标检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RadarXFormer框架提升自动驾驶感知性能。



摘要： 本文针对自动驾驶系统在复杂环境下的感知可靠性问题，提出RadarXFormer框架，通过融合4D毫米波雷达数据与RGB图像实现高效的3D目标检测。该方法利用原始雷达谱数据构建紧凑的3D表示，解决了传统雷达点云稀疏和数据量大的问题，同时提升了在恶劣天气和光照条件下的检测精度与实时性。实验表明，该方法在K-Radar数据集上表现出更强的鲁棒性和准确性。
