item.title: Optimal uncertainty bounds for multivariate kernel regression under bounded noise: A Gaussian process-based dual function
item.content:encoded: Non-conservative uncertainty bounds are essential for making reliable predictions about latent functions from noisy data--and thus, a key enabler for safe learning-based control. In this domain, kernel methods such as Gaussian process regression are established techniques, thanks to their inherent uncertainty quantification mechanism. Still, existing bounds either pose strong assumptions on the underlying noise distribution, are conservative, do not scale well in the multi-output case, or are difficult to integrate into downstream tasks. This paper addresses these limitations by presenting a tight, distribution-free bound for multi-output kernel-based estimates. It is obtained through an unconstrained, duality-based formulation, which shares the same structure of classic Gaussian process confidence bounds and can thus be straightforwardly integrated into downstream optimization pipelines. We show that the proposed bound generalizes many existing results and illustrate its application using an example inspired by quadrotor dynamics learning.

item.title: More Rounds, More Noise: Why Multi-Turn Review Fails to Improve Cross-Context Verification
item.content:encoded: Cross-Context Review (CCR) improves LLM verification by separating production and review into independent sessions. A natural extension is multi-turn review: letting the reviewer ask follow-up questions, receive author responses, and review again. We call this Dynamic Cross-Context Review (D-CCR). In a controlled experiment with 30 artifacts and 150 injected errors, we tested four D-CCR variants against the single-pass CCR baseline. Single-pass CCR (F1 = 0.376) significantly outperformed all multi-turn variants, including D-CCR-2b with question-and-answer exchange (F1 = 0.303, $p < 0.001$, $d = -0.59$). Multi-turn review increased recall (+0.08) but generated 62% more false positives (8.5 vs. 5.2), collapsing precision from 0.30 to 0.20. Two mechanisms drive this degradation: (1) false positive pressure -- reviewers in later rounds fabricate findings when the artifact's real errors have been exhausted, and (2) Review Target Drift -- reviewers provided with prior Q&A exchanges shift from reviewing the artifact to critiquing the conversation itself. Independent re-review without prior context (D-CCR-2c) performed worst (F1 = 0.263), confirming that mere repetition degrades rather than helps. The degradation stems from false positive pressure in additional rounds, not from information amount -- within multi-turn conditions, more information actually helps (D-CCR-2b > D-CCR-2a). The problem is not what the reviewer sees, but that reviewing again invites noise.

item.title: Semantic One-Dimensional Tokenizer for Image Reconstruction and Generation
item.content:encoded: Visual generative models based on latent space have achieved great success, underscoring the significance of visual tokenization. Mapping images to latents boosts efficiency and enables multimodal alignment for scaling up in downstream tasks. Existing visual tokenizers primarily map images into fixed 2D spatial grids and focus on pixel-level restoration, which hinders the capture of representations with compact global semantics. To address these issues, we propose \textbf{SemTok}, a semantic one-dimensional tokenizer that compresses 2D images into 1D discrete tokens with high-level semantics. SemTok sets a new state-of-the-art in image reconstruction, achieving superior fidelity with a remarkably compact token representation. This is achieved via a synergistic framework with three key innovations: a 2D-to-1D tokenization scheme, a semantic alignment constraint, and a two-stage generative training strategy. Building on SemTok, we construct a masked autoregressive generation framework, which yields notable improvements in downstream image generation tasks. Experiments confirm the effectiveness of our semantic 1D tokenization. Our code will be open-sourced.

item.title: Generative AI for Quantum Circuits and Quantum Code: A Technical Review and Taxonomy
item.content:encoded: We review thirteen generative systems and five supporting datasets for quantum circuit and quantum code generation, identified through a structured scoping review of Hugging Face, arXiv, and provenance tracing (January-February 2026). We organize the field along two axes: artifact type (Qiskit code, OpenQASM programs, circuit graphs); crossed with training regime (supervised fine-tuning, verifier-in-the-loop RL, diffusion/graph generation, agentic optimization); and systematically apply a three-layer evaluation framework covering syntactic validity, semantic correctness, and hardware executability. The central finding is that while all reviewed systems address syntax and most address semantics to some degree, none reports end-to-end evaluation on quantum hardware (Layer 3b), leaving a significant gap between generated circuits and practical deployment. Scope note: quantum code refers throughout to quantum program artifacts (QASM, Qiskit); we do not cover generation of quantum error-correcting codes (QEC).

item.title: Locate-then-Sparsify: Attribution Guided Sparse Strategy for Visual Hallucination Mitigation
item.content:encoded: Despite the significant advancements in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), their tendency to generate hallucinations undermines reliability and restricts broader practical deployment. Among the hallucination mitigation methods, feature steering emerges as a promising approach that reduces erroneous outputs in LVLMs without increasing inference costs. However, current methods apply uniform feature steering across all layers. This heuristic strategy ignores inter-layer differences, potentially disrupting layers unrelated to hallucinations and ultimately leading to performance degradation on general tasks. In this paper, we propose a plug-and-play framework called Locate-Then-Sparsify for Feature Steering (LTS-FS), which controls the steering intensity according to the hallucination relevance of each layer. We first construct a synthetic dataset comprising token-level and sentence-level hallucination cases. Based on this dataset, we introduce an attribution method based on causal interventions to quantify the hallucination relevance of each layer. With the attribution scores across layers, we propose a layerwise strategy that converts these scores into feature steering intensities for individual layers, enabling more precise adjustments specifically on hallucination-relevant layers. Extensive experiments across multiple LVLMs and benchmarks demonstrate that our LTS-FS framework effectively mitigates hallucination while preserving strong performance.

item.title: VIGIL: Towards Edge-Extended Agentic AI for Enterprise IT Support
item.content:encoded: Enterprise IT support is constrained by heterogeneous devices, evolving policies, and long-tail failure modes that are difficult to resolve centrally. We present VIGIL, an edge-extended agentic AI system that deploys desktop-resident agents to perform situated diagnosis, retrieval over enterprise knowledge, and policy-governed remediation directly on user devices with explicit consent and end-to-end observability. In a 10-week pilot of VIGIL's operational loop on 100 resource-constrained endpoints, VIGIL reduces interaction rounds by 39%, achieves at least 4 times faster diagnosis, and supports self-service resolution in 82% of matched cases. Users report excellent usability, high trust, and low cognitive workload across four validated instruments, with qualitative feedback highlighting transparency as critical for trust. Notably, users rated the system higher when no historical matches were available, suggesting on-device diagnosis provides value independent of knowledge base coverage. This pilot establishes safety and observability foundations for fleet-wide continuous improvement.

item.title: Follow the Clues, Frame the Truth: Hybrid-evidential Deductive Reasoning in Open-Vocabulary Multimodal Emotion Recognition
item.content:encoded: Open-Vocabulary Multimodal Emotion Recognition (OV-MER) is inherently challenging due to the ambiguity of equivocal multimodal cues, which often stem from distinct unobserved situational dynamics. While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) offer extensive semantic coverage, their performance is often bottlenecked by premature commitment to dominant data priors, resulting in suboptimal heuristics that overlook crucial, complementary affective cues across modalities. We argue that effective affective reasoning requires more than surface-level association; it necessitates reconstructing nuanced emotional states by synthesizing multiple evidence-grounded rationales that reconcile these observations from diverse latent perspectives. We introduce HyDRA, a Hybrid-evidential Deductive Reasoning Architecture that formalizes inference as a Propose-Verify-Decide protocol. To internalize this abductive process, we employ reinforcement learning with hierarchical reward shaping, aligning the reasoning trajectories with final task performance to ensure they best reconcile the observed multimodal cues. Systematic evaluations validate our design choices, with HyDRA consistently outperforming strong baselines--especially in ambiguous or conflicting scenarios--while providing interpretable, diagnostic evidence traces.

item.title: SIA: A Synthesize-Inject-Align Framework for Knowledge-Grounded and Secure E-commerce Search LLMs with Industrial Deployment
item.content:encoded: Large language models offer transformative potential for e-commerce search by enabling intent-aware recommendations. However, their industrial deployment is hindered by two critical challenges: (1) knowledge hallucination due to insufficient encoding of dynamic, fine-grained product knowledge, and (2) security vulnerabilities under jailbreak attacks that threaten compliance. To address these issues, we propose SI--a Synthesize-Inject-Align framework for building knowledgeable and secure e-commerce search LLMs. Our approach first synthesizes high-quality natural language corpus by combining structured knowledge graphs with unstructured behavioral logs, augmented with reasoning chains and safety-aware data.We then introduce a parameter-efficient pre-training strategy based on Depth Up-Scaling to inject domain knowledge while preserving general capabilities. Finally, a dual-path alignment method via multi-task instruction tuning and adversarial training strengthens both task performance and safety robustness. The framework has been deployed at JD.com, China's largest self-operated e-commerce platform, where A/B tests across five core search scenarios demonstrate significant improvements in key business metrics, validating its industrial effectiveness and scalability.

item.title: Physics-integrated neural differentiable modeling for immersed boundary systems
item.content:encoded: Accurately, efficiently, and stably computing complex fluid flows and their evolution near solid boundaries over long horizons remains challenging. Conventional numerical solvers require fine grids and small time steps to resolve near-wall dynamics, resulting in high computational costs, while purely data-driven surrogate models accumulate rollout errors and lack robustness under extrapolative conditions. To address these issues, this study extends existing neural PDE solvers by developing a physics-integrated differentiable framework for long-horizon prediction of immersed-boundary flows. A key design aspect of the framework includes an important improvement, namely the structural integration of physical principles into an end-to-end differentiable architecture incorporating a PDE-based intermediate velocity module and a multi-direct forcing immersed boundary module, both adhering to the pressure-projection procedure for incompressible flow computation. The computationally expensive pressure projection step is substituted with a learned implicit correction using ConvResNet blocks to reduce cost, and a sub-iteration strategy is introduced to separate the embedded physics module's stability requirement from the surrogate model's time step, enabling stable coarse-grid autoregressive rollouts with large effective time increments. The framework uses only single-step supervision for training, eliminating long-horizon backpropagation and reducing training time to under one hour on a single GPU. Evaluations on benchmark cases of flow past a stationary cylinder and a rotationally oscillating cylinder at Re=100 show the proposed model consistently outperforms purely data-driven, physics-loss-constrained, and coarse-grid numerical baselines in flow-field fidelity and long-horizon stability, while achieving an approximately 200-fold inference speedup over the high-resolution solver.

item.title: MessyKitchens: Contact-rich object-level 3D scene reconstruction
item.content:encoded: Monocular 3D scene reconstruction has recently seen significant progress. Powered by the modern neural architectures and large-scale data, recent methods achieve high performance in depth estimation from a single image. Meanwhile, reconstructing and decomposing common scenes into individual 3D objects remains a hard challenge due to the large variety of objects, frequent occlusions and complex object relations. Notably, beyond shape and pose estimation of individual objects, applications in robotics and animation require physically-plausible scene reconstruction where objects obey physical principles of non-penetration and realistic contacts. In this work we advance object-level scene reconstruction along two directions. First, we introduceMessyKitchens, a new dataset with real-world scenes featuring cluttered environments and providing high-fidelity object-level ground truth in terms of 3D object shapes, poses and accurate object contacts. Second, we build on the recent SAM 3D approach for single-object reconstruction and extend it with Multi-Object Decoder (MOD) for joint object-level scene reconstruction. To validate our contributions, we demonstrate MessyKitchens to significantly improve previous datasets in registration accuracy and inter-object penetration. We also compare our multi-object reconstruction approach on three datasets and demonstrate consistent and significant improvements of MOD over the state of the art. Our new benchmark, code and pre-trained models will become publicly available on our project website: https://messykitchens.github.io/.

item.title: ManiTwin: Scaling Data-Generation-Ready Digital Object Dataset to 100K
item.content:encoded: Learning in simulation provides a useful foundation for scaling robotic manipulation capabilities. However, this paradigm often suffers from a lack of data-generation-ready digital assets, in both scale and diversity. In this work, we present ManiTwin, an automated and efficient pipeline for generating data-generation-ready digital object twins. Our pipeline transforms a single image into simulation-ready and semantically annotated 3D asset, enabling large-scale robotic manipulation data generation. Using this pipeline, we construct ManiTwin-100K, a dataset containing 100K high-quality annotated 3D assets. Each asset is equipped with physical properties, language descriptions, functional annotations, and verified manipulation proposals. Experiments demonstrate that ManiTwin provides an efficient asset synthesis and annotation workflow, and that ManiTwin-100K offers high-quality and diverse assets for manipulation data generation, random scene synthesis, and VQA data generation, establishing a strong foundation for scalable simulation data synthesis and policy learning. Our webpage is available at https://manitwin.github.io/.

item.title: VIEW2SPACE: Studying Multi-View Visual Reasoning from Sparse Observations
item.content:encoded: Multi-view visual reasoning is essential for intelligent systems that must understand complex environments from sparse and discrete viewpoints, yet existing research has largely focused on single-image or temporally dense video settings. In real-world scenarios, reasoning across views requires integrating partial observations without explicit guidance, while collecting large-scale multi-view data with accurate geometric and semantic annotations remains challenging. To address this gap, we leverage physically grounded simulation to construct diverse, high-fidelity 3D scenes with precise per-view metadata, enabling scalable data generation that remains transferable to real-world settings. Based on this engine, we introduce VIEW2SPACE, a multi-dimensional benchmark for sparse multi-view reasoning, together with a scalable, disjoint training split supporting millions of grounded question-answer pairs. Using this benchmark, a comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art vision-language and spatial models reveals that multi-view reasoning remains largely unsolved, with most models performing only marginally above random guessing. We further investigate whether training can bridge this gap. Our proposed Grounded Chain-of-Thought with Visual Evidence substantially improves performance under moderate difficulty, and generalizes to real-world data, outperforming existing approaches in cross-dataset evaluation. We further conduct difficulty-aware scaling analyses across model size, data scale, reasoning depth, and visibility constraints, indicating that while geometric perception can benefit from scaling under sufficient visibility, deep compositional reasoning across sparse views remains a fundamental challenge.

item.title: What if Pinocchio Were a Reinforcement Learning Agent: A Normative End-to-End Pipeline
item.content:encoded: In the past decade, artificial intelligence (AI) has developed quickly. With this rapid progression came the need for systems capable of complying with the rules and norms of our society so that they can be successfully and safely integrated into our daily lives. Inspired by the story of Pinocchio in ``Le avventure di Pinocchio - Storia di un burattino'', this thesis proposes a pipeline that addresses the problem of developing norm compliant and context-aware agents. Building on the AJAR, Jiminy, and NGRL architectures, the work introduces \pino, a hybrid model in which reinforcement learning agents are supervised by argumentation-based normative advisors. In order to make this pipeline operational, this thesis also presents a novel algorithm for automatically extracting the arguments and relationships that underlie the advisors' decisions. Finally, this thesis investigates the phenomenon of \textit{norm avoidance}, providing a definition and a mitigation strategy within the context of reinforcement learning agents. Each component of the pipeline is empirically evaluated. The thesis concludes with a discussion of related work, current limitations, and directions for future research.

item.title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
item.content:encoded: We present Polyglot-Lion, a family of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models tailored for the linguistic landscape of Singapore, covering English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. Our models are obtained by fine-tuning Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and Qwen3-ASR-1.7B exclusively on publicly available speech corpora, using a balanced sampling strategy that equalizes the number of training utterances per language and deliberately omits language-tag conditioning so that the model learns to identify languages implicitly from audio. On 12 benchmarks spanning the four target languages, Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves an average error rate of 14.85, competitive with MERaLiON-2-10B-ASR (14.32) - a model 6x larger - while incurring a training cost of \$81 on a single RTX PRO 6000 GPU compared to \$18,862 for the 128-GPU baseline. Inference throughput is approximately 20x faster than MERaLiON at 0.10 s/sample versus 2.02 s/sample. These results demonstrate that linguistically balanced fine-tuning of moderate-scale pretrained models can yield deployment-ready multilingual ASR at a fraction of the cost of larger specialist systems.

item.title: ODIN-Based CPU-GPU Architecture with Replay-Driven Simulation and Emulation
item.content:encoded: Integration of CPU and GPU technologies is a key enabler for modern AI and graphics workloads, combining control-oriented processing with massive parallel compute capability. As systems evolve toward chiplet-based architectures, pre-silicon validation of tightly coupled CPU-GPU subsystems becomes increasingly challenging due to complex validation framework setup, large design scale, high concurrency, non-deterministic execution, and intricate protocol interactions at chiplet boundaries, often resulting in long integration cycles. This paper presents a replay-driven validation methodology developed during the integration of a CPU subsystem, multiple Xe GPU cores, and a configurable Network-on-Chip (NoC) within a foundational SoC building block targeting the ODIN integrated chiplet architecture. By leveraging deterministic waveform capture and replay across both simulation and emulation using a single design database, complex GPU workloads and protocol sequences can be reproduced reliably at the system level. This approach significantly accelerates debug, improves integration confidence, and enables end-to-end system boot and workload execution within a single quarter, demonstrating the effectiveness of replay-based validation as a scalable methodology for chiplet-based systems.

item.title: DriveFix: Spatio-Temporally Coherent Driving Scene Restoration
item.content:encoded: Recent advancements in 4D scene reconstruction, particularly those leveraging diffusion priors, have shown promise for novel view synthesis in autonomous driving. However, these methods often process frames independently or in a view-by-view manner, leading to a critical lack of spatio-temporal synergy. This results in spatial misalignment across cameras and temporal drift in sequences. We propose DriveFix, a novel multi-view restoration framework that ensures spatio-temporal coherence for driving scenes. Our approach employs an interleaved diffusion transformer architecture with specialized blocks to explicitly model both temporal dependencies and cross-camera spatial consistency. By conditioning the generation on historical context and integrating geometry-aware training losses, DriveFix enforces that the restored views adhere to a unified 3D geometry. This enables the consistent propagation of high-fidelity textures and significantly reduces artifacts. Extensive evaluations on the Waymo, nuScenes, and PandaSet datasets demonstrate that DriveFix achieves state-of-the-art performance in both reconstruction and novel view synthesis, marking a substantial step toward robust 4D world modeling for real-world deployment.

item.title: Answer Bubbles: Information Exposure in AI-Mediated Search
item.content:encoded: Generative search systems are increasingly replacing link-based retrieval with AI-generated summaries, yet little is known about how these systems differ in sources, language, and fidelity to cited material. We examine responses to 11,000 real search queries across four systems -- vanilla GPT, Search GPT, Google AI Overviews, and traditional Google Search -- at three levels: source diversity, linguistic characterization of the generated summary, and source-summary fidelity. We find that generative search systems exhibit significant \textit{source-selection} biases in their citations, favoring certain sources over others. Incorporating search also selectively attenuates epistemic markers, reducing hedging by up to 60\% while preserving confidence language in the AI-generated summaries. At the same time, AI summaries further compound the citation biases: Wikipedia and longer sources are disproportionately overrepresented, whereas cited social media content and negatively framed sources are substantially underrepresented. Our findings highlight the potential for \textit{answer bubbles}, in which identical queries yield structurally different information realities across systems, with implications for user trust, source visibility, and the transparency of AI-mediated information access.

item.title: Federated Learning with Multi-Partner OneFlorida+ Consortium Data for Predicting Major Postoperative Complications
item.content:encoded: Background: This study aims to develop and validate federated learning models for predicting major postoperative complications and mortality using a large multicenter dataset from the OneFlorida Data Trust. We hypothesize that federated learning models will offer robust generalizability while preserving data privacy and security. Methods: This retrospective, longitudinal, multicenter cohort study included 358,644 adult patients admitted to five healthcare institutions, who underwent 494,163 inpatient major surgical procedures from 2012-2023. We developed and internally and externally validated federated learning models to predict the postoperative risk of intensive care unit (ICU) admission, mechanical ventilation (MV) therapy, acute kidney injury (AKI), and in-hospital mortality. These models were compared with local models trained on data from a single center and central models trained on a pooled dataset from all centers. Performance was primarily evaluated using area under the receiver operating characteristics curve (AUROC) and the area under the precision-recall curve (AUPRC) values. Results: Our federated learning models demonstrated strong predictive performance, with AUROC scores consistently comparable or superior performance in terms of AUROC and AUPRC across all outcomes and sites. Our federated learning models also demonstrated strong generalizability, with comparable or superior performance in terms of both AUROC and AUPRC compared to the best local learning model at each site. Conclusions: By leveraging multicenter data, we developed robust, generalizable, and privacy-preserving predictive models for major postoperative complications and mortality. These findings support the feasibility of federated learning in clinical decision support systems.

item.title: Open-Source Reproduction and Explainability Analysis of Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation
item.content:encoded: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation (CRAG) improves the robustness of RAG systems by evaluating retrieved document quality and triggering corrective actions. However, the original implementation relies on proprietary components including the Google Search API and closed model weights, limiting reproducibility. In this work, we present a fully open-source reproduction of CRAG, replacing proprietary web search with the Wikipedia API and the original LLaMA-2 generator with Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct. We evaluate on PopQA and ARC-Challenge, demonstrating that our open-source pipeline achieves comparable performance to the original system. Furthermore, we contribute the first explainability analysis of CRAG's T5-based retrieval evaluator using SHAP, revealing that the evaluator primarily relies on named entity alignment rather than semantic similarity. Our analysis identifies key failure modes including domain transfer limitations on science questions. All code and results are available at https://github.com/suryayalavarthi/crag-reproduction.

item.title: What DINO saw: ALiBi positional encoding reduces positional bias in Vision Transformers
item.content:encoded: Vision transformers (ViTs) - especially feature foundation models like DINOv2 - learn rich representations useful for many downstream tasks. However, architectural choices (such as positional encoding) can lead to these models displaying positional biases and artefacts independent of semantic content. This makes zero-shot adaption difficult in fields like material science, where images are often cross-sections of homogeneous microstructure (i.e. having no preferred direction). In this work, we investigate the positional bias in ViTs via linear probing, finding it present across a range of objectives and positional encodings, and subsequently reduce it by finetuning models to use ALiBi relative positional encoding. We demonstrate that these models retain desirable general semantics and their unbiased features can be used successfully in trainable segmentation of complex microscopy images.

item.title: 基于ResFIM的个性化联邦学习框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出pFL-ResFIM框架提升联邦学习个性化效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的个性化联邦学习框架pFL-ResFIM，通过引入残差Fisher信息矩阵（ResFIM）来量化模型参数对领域差异的敏感性，并采用谱迁移策略生成模拟数据以估计ResFIM。该方法将模型参数分为领域敏感和领域不变部分，仅在服务器聚合领域不变参数，从而为每个客户端构建个性化模型。实验表明，该框架在多个公开数据集上均优于现有方法，验证了其有效性。

item.title: AdapterTune：提升视觉Transformer冻结主干迁移性能的方法
item.content:encoded:
AdapterTune提升视觉Transformer迁移学习效果，减少参数量。



摘要： 本文提出AdapterTune，解决视觉Transformer在冻结主干迁移时的优化不稳定和适配器容量设置缺乏指导的问题。通过在每个Transformer块中引入残差低秩瓶颈，确保模型从预训练状态开始，避免早期表示漂移。研究分析了适配器秩作为特征空间任务变化的容量预算，并通过实验验证其精度提升呈递减趋势。在9个数据集和3种主干规模上测试，AdapterTune平均提升14.9个百分点，仅需0.92倍全微调参数量，且在10/15对数据集-主干组合中优于全微调。

item.title: 基于PrototypeNAS的高效边缘设备DNN模型设计与优化
item.content:encoded:
PrototypeNAS实现快速DNN模型部署，适应不同MCU硬件约束。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为PrototypeNAS的零样本神经网络架构搜索方法，旨在加速并自动化针对不同微控制器单元（MCU）的深度神经网络（DNN）选择、压缩和定制。该方法通过三个步骤优化DNN设计：首先构建包含多种架构类型及剪枝量化配置的搜索空间；其次使用集成的零样本代理进行优化；最后利用超体积子集选择从多目标优化的帕累托前沿中提取具有代表性的模型。实验在12个数据集上的三种任务（图像分类、时间序列分类、目标检测）中验证了PrototypeNAS的有效性，结果表明其能在短时间内生成适用于现成MCU的小型DNN模型，且准确率接近大型DNN模型。

item.title: 跨句动词交替模式的语料库与评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究跨句动词交替模式，构建多语言数据集并评估模型表现。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在跨句语义模式理解方面的不足，构建了涵盖英语、德语、意大利语和希伯来语的动词交替数据集，包括变化状态和宾语省略等现象。通过Blackbird Language Matrices任务，测试模型对句法和语义规则的掌握能力，并采用不同复杂度的模板和数据增强策略进行实验，展示了这些数据集在诊断模型能力方面的有效性。

item.title: HalDec-Bench：一种用于评估视觉-语言模型幻觉检测能力的基准
item.content:encoded:
提出HalDec-Bench基准以评估视觉-语言模型的幻觉检测能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了HalDec-Bench，一个用于评估视觉-语言模型（VLM）在幻觉检测任务中表现的基准。该基准包含由多种VLM生成的 captions，以及人工标注的幻觉信息、详细的幻觉类型分类和逐段标签。HalDec-Bench 提供了不同难度的任务，揭示了现有多模态推理或对齐基准中未发现的模型性能差异。研究还发现，检测器倾向于将响应开头的句子视为正确，且使用强VLM作为过滤器可有效减少数据集噪声。

item.title: 基于光子量子增强的知识蒸馏框架研究
item.content:encoded:
光子量子增强知识蒸馏提升机器学习效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种混合量子光子-经典框架PQKD，通过可编程光子电路生成条件信号，指导学生网络在知识蒸馏过程中进行高效学习。该方法用字典卷积替代全可训练卷积核，每层仅学习少量共享空间基滤波器，样本依赖的通道混合权重则来自有限采样的光子特征。训练过程交替进行学生网络的梯度优化和光子参数的无梯度更新，避免直接对光子硬件求导。实验表明，PQKD在MNIST、Fashion-MNIST和CIFAR-10数据集上表现出可控的压缩-精度边界，在简单基准测试中保持接近教师模型的性能。有限采样下的性能退化符合光子噪声规律，指数移动平均特征平滑有效抑制高频噪声波动，扩展了实际应用范围。

item.title: 多模态虚假视频检测方法 MAGIC3 的研究与应用
item.content:encoded:
MAGIC3 提升多模态虚假视频检测效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出 MAGIC3，一种用于检测多模态虚假视频的系统。通过建模文本、视觉和音频之间的跨模态一致性，MAGIC3 在 FakeSV 和 FakeTT 数据集上表现优异。研究发现真实视频在文本-视觉一致性上较高，而虚假视频则表现出相反模式。MAGIC3 结合了多粒度的一致性建模、跨模态注意力机制以及多风格 LLM 重写技术，提升了检测性能，并在计算成本上具有显著优势，实现高吞吐量和低显存占用。

item.title: 神经符号记忆框架提升多模态智能体推理能力
item.content:encoded:
NS-Mem提升多模态智能体推理准确性。



摘要： 本文提出NS-Mem，一种结合神经记忆与显式符号结构的长期神经符号记忆框架，以增强多模态智能体的推理能力。该框架包含三层记忆架构、基于SK-Gen的知识构建与维护机制以及混合检索机制，有效提升了复杂推理任务的准确率。实验表明，NS-Mem在真实世界多模态推理基准上平均提升了4.35%，在受限推理查询中最高提升12.5%。

item.title: 大语言模型的对齐困境与优先级图分析
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示LLM对齐难题及优先级图机制，提出运行时验证以增强安全性。



摘要： 本文探讨了随着大语言模型（LLMs）能力提升，其在多种场景中面临的冲突与伦理困境。作者首先对这些冲突进行分类和总结，随后构建了一个优先级图来建模LLM的选择偏好，其中指令和价值观作为节点，边表示由模型输出分布决定的上下文优先级。研究发现，统一稳定的LLM对齐极具挑战性，因为该图在不同上下文中可能变化且不一致。此外，还发现了潜在漏洞——优先级劫持，即攻击者可通过构造欺骗性上下文操纵模型行为。为应对这一问题，文章提出运行时验证机制，使LLM能通过外部信息源校验上下文，提高抗操控能力。但同时指出，许多伦理和价值困境在哲学上难以解决，仍是AI对齐领域的长期挑战。

item.title: 面向高效部署的脉冲神经网络剪枝方法SLAMP
item.content:encoded:
SLAMP提升SNN效率与稳定性，降低连接与尖峰操作成本。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于理论指导的脉冲神经网络剪枝框架SLAMP，旨在解决传统幅度剪枝在时间累积、非均匀时间步贡献和膜稳定性方面的不足。SLAMP通过控制各层和时间步的最坏情况输出失真，实现时间感知的层重要性评分，并在单时间步极限下退化为传统层自适应剪枝。该方法采用两阶段流程，结合时间评分估计、全局稀疏分配和带重训练的幅度剪枝以恢复稳定性。实验表明，SLAMP在CIFAR10、CIFAR100和CIFAR10-DVS数据集上显著减少连接和尖峰操作成本，同时保持准确率，为高效可部署的SNN推理提供了可行方案。

item.title: 预测器校准的分解分析与应用
item.content:encoded:
文章提出预测器校准的分解方法，分析信息依赖性与损失关系。



摘要： 本文研究了预测器校准的条件性质，通过任意适当损失函数的分解公式，揭示了预测器在不同信息水平下的期望损失构成。文章指出，预测器的损失可分解为可靠性项和残余不确定性项，并通过嵌套信息层级的链式分解量化信息增益。应用于分类任务时，得到包含偏差、信息损失和不可约不确定性的三部分表达式。该框架用于分析后校准、模型聚合及分阶段构建，提供了Brier损失和对数损失的明确形式。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的RGB到RAW图像转换方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出SpiralDiff框架提升RGB到RAW图像转换质量。



摘要： 与RGB图像相比，RAW图像在复杂成像条件下具有更高的保真度和更丰富的场景信息。为降低数据采集成本，现有方法尝试从RGB图像合成RAW图像，但忽略了像素强度差异带来的重建难度变化以及多相机转换中需要相机特异性适配的问题。为此，本文提出SpiralDiff框架，采用信号依赖的噪声加权策略，以适应不同强度级别的重建精度。同时引入CamLoRA模块，实现统一模型对不同相机ISP特性的轻量级适配。实验表明，该方法在四个基准数据集上表现出色，并提升了基于RAW图像的目标检测性能。

item.title: 基于尺度变换的物理信息神经网络求解反应扩散方程
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效通用的PINN框架求解多维反应扩散方程。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于尺度变换的物理信息神经网络（scaled TW-PINN）框架，用于计算具有不同反应和扩散系数的n维反应扩散方程的行波解。通过将原问题转换为一维尺度反应扩散方程，该框架利用单一训练好的PINN求解器适应不同的系数和空间维度。文章证明了该求解器在行波解上的通用逼近性，并通过一维和二维数值实验验证了其准确性、灵活性和优越性。最后，还探讨了该框架在Fisher方程中的扩展应用。

item.title: EECVS：一种自适应事件相机压缩框架
item.content:encoded:
提出EECVS框架，实现高效事件相机压缩与高精度重建。



摘要： 本文提出EECVS（Efficient Event Camera Volume System），一种基于连续时间狄拉克脉冲序列建模的事件相机压缩框架。该框架通过动态选择DCT、DTFT和DWT变换，并结合针对不同域的系数剪枝策略，有效消除时间分箱伪影并适应实时事件密度变化。实验表明，EECVS在EHPT-XC和MVSEC数据集上表现出色，DTFT方法在重建精度上优于其他方法。在下游分割任务中，EECVS展现出良好的泛化能力，尤其在MVSEC数据集上，其平均IoU达到0.87，远超传统体素网格方法。ROS2实现支持实时部署，DCT处理延迟仅为1.5ms，吞吐量比其他方法高出2.7倍，为机器人应用提供了高效的自适应事件压缩方案。

item.title: 多智能体系统跨视角场景理解与CoRL框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出E2W基准和CoRL框架，提升多智能体跨视角场景理解能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了多智能体系统从分布式、局部视角理解世界的核心挑战，并引入E2W基准来评估视觉-语言模型在全局计数、关系定位推理和动作导向抓取任务中的表现。为应对这一问题，作者提出了CoRL框架，结合思维链监督微调与基于群体相对策略优化的强化学习。其核心组件Cross-View Spatial Reward (CVSR) 通过将推理步骤与视觉证据关联，提供密集的任务对齐反馈，确保跨视角实体解析的一致性并引导模型得出正确预测。实验表明，CoRL在多个指标上优于现有基线，并在外部空间推理基准和真实多机器人操作中表现出良好的泛化能力。E2W与CoRL为从分布式视角学习世界中心场景理解提供了理论基础，推动了协作式具身AI的发展。

item.title: 大语言模型在生成错误选项中的推理机制分析
item.content:encoded:
研究分析大语言模型如何生成合理错误选项，发现其与教学科学最佳实践一致。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型（LLMs）在生成多选题错误选项时的推理过程，该任务需要模型生成看似合理但错误的答案。作者提出一种分类法，分析当前最先进的LLMs在生成错误选项时所采用的策略，并将其与学习科学中的最佳实践进行比较。研究发现，模型通常先正确解答问题，再模拟多种可能的误解，并最终选择一组错误选项。分析还显示，错误主要源于未能正确恢复正确答案或在选项中选择不当，而非模拟错误或结构问题。实验表明，在提示中提供正确答案可使模型生成的错误选项更接近人类作者，强调了在生成错误推理时以正确答案为锚点的重要性。整体而言，该研究为理解LLMs在建模学生错误推理方面的能力提供了结构化且可解释的视角。

item.title: 基于提示的完整人体图像生成方法PHAC
item.content:encoded:
PHAC实现用户可控的人体图像补全，提升生成质量与提示对齐度。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PHAC的新任务，用于在满足可见外观约束和用户提示的情况下完成被遮挡的人体图像。用户通过简单的点提示（如目标姿态关节或区域边界框）进行控制，这些提示由专门的ControlNet模块编码，并注入预训练扩散模型中。仅微调交叉注意力块以保持生成先验不变，同时提升提示对齐度。此外，引入基于修复的优化模块，确保可见区域忠实保留并实现无缝融合。实验表明，该方法在HAC和PGPIS基准上优于现有方法，生成结果更符合物理合理性且质量更高。

item.title: 基于多任务遗传算法与多粒度编码的蛋白质-核苷酸结合位点预测方法
item.content:encoded:
MTGA-MGE提升蛋白质-核苷酸结合位点预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出MTGA-MGE框架，结合多任务遗传算法和多粒度编码技术，以提升蛋白质-核苷酸结合位点的预测精度。该方法通过多尺度卷积与自注意力机制提取特征，并利用遗传算法动态优化任务融合策略，增强模型泛化能力。同时引入外部邻域机制促进跨任务信息共享。实验表明，MTGA-MGE在多种数据集上表现优异，适用于高资源和低资源场景，为后基因组时代蛋白质-配体相互作用研究提供新方案。

item.title: 基于Koopman算子理论的动态验证与误差分析
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种验证Koopman近似可靠性的方法，用于量化特征空间误差。



摘要： 本文研究了Koopman算子理论在非线性动态系统中的应用，指出有限维特征空间常导致闭包失败和投影误差，从而产生误导性的Koopman模式和过度自信的预测。为解决这一问题，作者提出了一种统一的后验方法，通过主角分析量化Koopman不变性，并引入主可观测量和主角分解（PAD）以替代SVD截断，显著提升性能。同时，文章推导了多步误差界，并结合高斯过程误差代理模型，实现了验证的谱分析、可信预测和字典优化。该方法在混沌系统和高维基准数据集上得到验证。

item.title: 终身智能代理的推理框架选择与结构失效问题
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨了智能代理在决策时无法正确选择推理框架的问题。



摘要： 文章指出，当前终身智能代理的主要问题并非知识不足，而是无法决定如何推理。当面对如“这枚硬币是否公平？”这样的问题时，代理需要判断是否采用频率学假设检验或贝叶斯后验推断，这两种方法在认识论上不兼容。混合使用会导致结构性错误并影响决策链。作者将此问题定义为“宇宙路由问题”，即在调用专用求解器前对问题进行分类。研究发现，硬性路由到异构求解器在保持准确率的同时提升了7倍速度，且语义推理优于表面匹配。此外，基于重放的持续学习在扩展新信念空间时表现优异，表明模块化架构更适合终身学习。

item.title: PRISM：基于仿真的联合后验模型选择方法
item.content:encoded:
PRISM实现高效模型选择，支持复杂度动态控制。



摘要： PRISM是一种基于仿真的编码器-解码器方法，能够同时推断离散模型结构和连续参数的联合后验分布。它在测试时通过可调模型先验控制模型复杂度，解决了传统贝叶斯工作流在大规模模型家族中的不实用性问题。该方法在符号回归任务中展示了对数以十亿计模型实例的扩展性，并在扩散MRI生物物理建模中验证了其在多组分模型选择中的有效性，适用于合成和真实神经影像数据。

item.title: RESTA防御在视觉语言模型中的应用与效果评估
item.content:encoded:
RESTA有效降低多模态越狱攻击成功率，提升VLM安全性。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）在代理AI系统中的安全性和可靠性问题，并针对越狱攻击提出使用RESTA防御方法。通过在JailBreakV-28K基准测试中评估，发现RESTA能有效减少攻击成功率，尤其在引入方向性嵌入噪声时效果更佳。该方法作为轻量级的推理阶段防御层，有助于构建更安全的VLM系统。

item.title: 生物启发式视觉表示方法提升视觉语言模型效率
item.content:encoded:
提出LLMind框架，提升VLM在有限像素下的性能。



摘要： 本文提出LLMind，一种无需训练的生物启发式视觉表示框架，模仿人类视觉的聚焦编码和皮层放大机制，实现更高效、自适应的视觉语言模型（VLM）表示。通过引入生物启发式自适应采样策略（BASS）和闭环语义反馈（CSF），LLMind在有限像素预算下显著提升了VQA任务的性能，相比均匀采样平均提升20%以上。实验表明，LLMind仅使用1%、3%和5%的像素即可保留82%、92%和97%的全分辨率性能，且具有轻量、可插拔和兼容性等优势。

item.title: LOOM-CFM：提升连续归一化流采样效率的新方法
item.content:encoded:
LOOM-CFM优化噪声数据配对，提升生成模型效率。



摘要： LOOM-CFM是一种改进的条件流匹配方法，通过扩展最小批量最优传输的范围，跨批次保留并优化噪声与数据的配对，从而提升采样速度和质量。该方法在多个数据集上表现出色，同时增强了蒸馏初始化效果，并支持高分辨率合成。相比传统方法，LOOM-CFM在大规模数据集上的表现更优，为图像和视频生成提供了更高效的解决方案。

item.title: Safe Flow Q-Learning：一种安全离线强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
SafeFQL通过结合安全值函数和流策略，提升离线强化学习的安全性和效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Safe Flow Q-Learning (SafeFQL) 的安全离线强化学习方法，旨在在严格安全约束下从静态数据集中学习最大化奖励的策略。SafeFQL通过结合基于Hamilton-Jacobi可达性的安全值函数与高效的单步流策略，实现了安全动作选择。该方法通过自洽性贝尔曼递归学习安全值，并通过行为克隆训练流策略，再将其压缩为单步执行器，无需拒绝采样。为了处理有限数据带来的误差，还引入了符合预测校准步骤以调整安全阈值并提供概率安全性覆盖。实验表明，SafeFQL在船只导航和Safety Gymnasium MuJoCo任务中表现优异，显著减少了约束违反情况，同时相比扩散式安全生成基线方法具有更低的推理延迟。

item.title: 基于稀疏电路的模型编辑框架SCAN提升大语言模型的稳定性
item.content:encoded:
SCAN通过构建知识电路实现高效编辑，显著提升模型在连续编辑中的稳定性。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在顺序知识编辑过程中出现的灾难性遗忘和崩溃问题，提出了一种名为SCAN的稀疏编辑框架。该框架基于稀疏电路锚定神经元机制，通过构建知识电路实现更精确的模型编辑，避免传统密集编辑方法对已有知识的破坏。实验表明，SCAN在Gemma2、Qwen3和Llama3.1等多个模型上表现优异，在CounterFact、ZsRE和WikiFactDiff等基准测试中保持了较高的性能，即使经过3000次连续编辑，模型在MMLU和GSM8K等任务上的表现依然稳定，而其他方法则随着编辑次数增加逐渐退化，最终导致模型崩溃。

摘要： 本文针对工业检测中物体计数的挑战，提出一种基于多视角图像的3D物体计数方法。该方法通过分解任务为3D几何重建和占用率估计，结合深度学习与几何分析，有效解决了堆叠物品因遮挡导致计数困难的问题。实验在大规模合成数据和真实场景数据上验证了该方法的鲁棒性和准确性，适用于复杂工业环境下的库存跟踪和质量检测。

item.title: FreeTalk：基于情绪条件的3D说话头动画生成框架
item.content:encoded:
提出FreeTalk框架，实现情绪驱动的3D说话头动画生成。



摘要： 本文提出FreeTalk，一个两阶段的情绪条件3D说话头动画生成框架。该框架首先通过Audio-To-Sparse模块从语音音频中预测情绪相关的3D特征点位移序列，再通过Sparse-To-Mesh模块将这些位移转移到任意拓扑结构的3D网格上，无需模板匹配或对应监督。实验表明，FreeTalk在已知领域内表现与专用基线相当，并在未见身份和网格拓扑上表现出更强的鲁棒性。代码和预训练模型将公开发布。

item.title: Beyond the Covariance Trap: Unlocking Generalization in Same-Subject Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: While locate-then-edit knowledge editing efficiently updates knowledge encoded within Large Language Models (LLMs), a critical generalization failure mode emerges in the practical same-subject knowledge editing scenario: models fail to recall the updated knowledge when following user instructions, despite successfully recalling it in the original edited form. This paper identifies the geometric root of this generalization collapse as a fundamental conflict where the inner activation drifts induced by prompt variations exceed the model's geometric tolerance for generalization after editing. We attribute this instability to a dual pathology: (1) The joint optimization with orthogonal gradients collapses solutions into sharp minima with narrow stability, and (2) the standard covariance constraint paradoxically acts as a Covariance Trap that amplifies input perturbations. To resolve this, we introduce RoSE (Robust Same-subject Editing), which employs Isotropic Geometric Alignment to minimize representational deviation and Hierarchical Knowledge Integration to smooth the optimization landscape. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RoSE significantly improves instruction-following capabilities, laying the foundation for robust interactive parametric memory of LLM agents.

item.title: One CT Unified Model Training Framework to Rule All Scanning Protocols
item.content:encoded: Non-ideal measurement computed tomography (NICT), which lowers radiation at the cost of image quality, is expanding the clinical use of CT. Although unified models have shown promise in NICT enhancement, most methods require paired data, which is an impractical demand due to inevitable organ motion. Unsupervised approaches attempt to overcome this limitation, but their assumption of homogeneous noise neglects the variability of scanning protocols, leading to poor generalization and potential model collapse. We further observe that distinct scanning protocols, which correspond to different physical imaging processes, produce discrete sub-manifolds in the feature space, contradicting these assumptions and limiting their effectiveness. To address this, we propose an Uncertainty-Guided Manifold Smoothing (UMS) framework to bridge the gaps between sub-manifolds. A classifier in UMS identifies sub-manifolds and predicts uncertainty scores, which guide the generation of diverse samples across the entire manifold. By leveraging the classifier's capability, UMS effectively fills the gaps between discrete sub-manifolds, and promotes a continuous and dense feature space. Due to the complexity of the global manifold, it's hard to directly model it. Therefore, we propose to dynamically incorporate the global- and sub-manifold-specific features. Specifically, we design a global- and sub-manifold-driven architecture guided by the classifier, which enables dynamic adaptation to subdomain variations. This dynamic mechanism improves the network's capacity to capture both shared and domain-specific features, thereby improving reconstruction performance. Extensive experiments on public datasets are conducted to validate the effectiveness of our method across different generation paradigms.

item.title: Iterative Learning Control-Informed Reinforcement Learning for Batch Process Control
item.content:encoded: A significant limitation of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is the stochastic uncertainty in actions generated during exploration-exploitation, which poses substantial safety risks during both training and deployment. In industrial process control, the lack of formal stability and convergence guarantees further inhibits adoption of DRL methods by practitioners. Conversely, Iterative Learning Control (ILC) represents a well-established autonomous control methodology for repetitive systems, particularly in batch process optimization. ILC achieves desired control performance through iterative refinement of control laws, either between consecutive batches or within individual batches, to compensate for both repetitive and non-repetitive disturbances. This study introduces an Iterative Learning Control-Informed Reinforcement Learning (IL-CIRL) framework for training DRL controllers in dual-layer batch-to-batch and within-batch control architectures for batch processes. The proposed method incorporates Kalman filter-based state estimation within the iterative learning structure to guide DRL agents toward control policies that satisfy operational constraints and ensure stability guarantees. This approach enables the systematic design of DRL controllers for batch processes operating under multiple disturbance conditions.

item.title: Sampling-guided exploration of active feature selection policies
item.content:encoded: Determining the most appropriate features for machine learning predictive models is challenging regarding performance and feature acquisition costs. In particular, global feature choice is limited given that some features will only benefit a subset of instances. In previous work, we proposed a reinforcement learning approach to sequentially recommend which modality to acquire next to reach the best information/cost ratio, based on the instance-specific information already acquired. We formulated the problem as a Markov Decision Process where the state's dimensionality changes during the episode, avoiding data imputation, contrary to existing works. However, this only allowed processing a small number of features, as all possible combinations of features were considered. Here, we address these limitations with two contributions: 1) we expand our framework to larger datasets with a heuristic-based strategy that focuses on the most promising feature combinations, and 2) we introduce a post-fit regularisation strategy that reduces the number of different feature combinations, leading to compact sequences of decisions. We tested our method on four binary classification datasets (one involving high-dimensional variables), the largest of which had 56 features and 4500 samples. We obtained better performance than state-of-the-art methods, both in terms of accuracy and policy complexity.

item.title: A Tutorial on ALOS2 SAR Utilization: Dataset Preparation, Self-Supervised Pretraining, and Semantic Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Masked auto-encoders (MAE) and related approaches have shown promise for satellite imagery, but their application to synthetic aperture radar (SAR) remains limited due to challenges in semantic labeling and high noise levels. Building on our prior work with SAR-W-MixMAE, which adds SAR-specific intensity-weighted loss to standard MixMAE for pretraining, we also introduce SAR-W-SimMIM; a weighted variant of SimMIM applied to ALOS-2 single-channel SAR imagery. This method aims to reduce the impact of speckle and extreme intensity values during self-supervised pretraining. We evaluate its effect on semantic segmentation compared to our previous trial with SAR-W-MixMAE and random initialization, observing notable improvements. In addition, pretraining and fine-tuning models on satellite imagery pose unique challenges, particularly when developing region-specific models. Imbalanced land cover distributions such as dominant water, forest, or desert areas can introduce bias, affecting both pretraining and downstream tasks like land cover segmentation. To address this, we constructed a SAR dataset using ALOS-2 single-channel (HH polarization) imagery focused on the Japan region, marking the initial phase toward a national-scale foundation model. This dataset was used to pretrain a vision transformer-based autoencoder, with the resulting encoder fine-tuned for semantic segmentation using a task-specific decoder. Initial results demonstrate significant performance improvements compared to training from scratch with random initialization. In summary, this work provides a guide to process and prepare ALOS2 observations to create dataset so that it can be taken advantage of self-supervised pretraining of models and finetuning downstream tasks such as semantic segmentation.

item.title: Neural Networks as Local-to-Global Computations
item.content:encoded: We construct a cellular sheaf from any feedforward ReLU neural network by placing one vertex for each intermediate quantity in the forward pass and encoding each computational step - affine transformation, activation, output - as a restriction map on an edge. The restricted coboundary operator on the free coordinates is unitriangular, so its determinant is $1$ and the restricted Laplacian is positive definite for every activation pattern. It follows that the relative cohomology vanishes and the forward pass output is the unique harmonic extension of the boundary data. The sheaf heat equation converges exponentially to this output despite the state-dependent switching introduced by piecewise linear activations. Unlike the forward pass, the heat equation propagates information bidirectionally across layers, enabling pinned neurons that impose constraints in both directions, training through local discrepancy minimization without a backward pass, and per-edge diagnostics that decompose network behavior by layer and operation type. We validate the framework experimentally on small synthetic tasks, confirming the convergence theorems and demonstrating that sheaf-based training, while not yet competitive with stochastic gradient descent, obeys quantitative scaling laws predicted by the theory.

item.title: A proof-of-concept for automated AI-driven stellarator coil optimization with in-the-loop finite-element calculations
item.content:encoded: Finding feasible coils for stellarator fusion devices is a critical challenge of realizing this concept for future power plants. Years of research work can be put into the design of even a single reactor-scale stellarator design. To rapidly speed up and automate the workflow of designing stellarator coils, we have designed an end-to-end ``runner'' for performing stellarator coil optimization. The entirety of pre and post-processing steps have been automated; the user specifies only a few basic input parameters, and final coil solutions are updated on an open-source leaderboard. Two policies are available for performing non-stop automated coil optimizations through a genetic algorithm or a context-aware LLM. Lastly, we construct a novel in-the-loop optimization of Von Mises stresses in the coils, opening up important future capabilities for in-the-loop finite-element calculations.

item.title: AgentTrace: Causal Graph Tracing for Root Cause Analysis in Deployed Multi-Agent Systems
item.content:encoded: As multi-agent AI systems are increasingly deployed in real-world settings - from automated customer support to DevOps remediation - failures become harder to diagnose due to cascading effects, hidden dependencies, and long execution traces. We present AgentTrace, a lightweight causal tracing framework for post-hoc failure diagnosis in deployed multi-agent workflows. AgentTrace reconstructs causal graphs from execution logs, traces backward from error manifestations, and ranks candidate root causes using interpretable structural and positional signals - without requiring LLM inference at debugging time. Across a diverse benchmark of multi-agent failure scenarios designed to reflect common deployment patterns, AgentTrace localizes root causes with high accuracy and sub-second latency, significantly outperforming both heuristic and LLM-based baselines. Our results suggest that causal tracing provides a practical foundation for improving the reliability and trustworthiness of agentic systems in the wild.

item.title: Clinically Aware Synthetic Image Generation for Concept Coverage in Chest X-ray Models
item.content:encoded: The clinical deployment of AI diagnostic models demands more than benchmark accuracy - it demands robustness across the full spectrum of disease presentations. However, publicly available chest radiographic datasets systematically underrepresent critical clinical feature combinations, leaving models under-trained precisely where clinical stakes are highest. We present CARS, a clinically aware and anatomically grounded framework that addresses this gap through principled synthetic image generation. CARS applies targeted perturbations to clinical feature vectors, enabling controlled insertion and deletion of pathological findings while explicitly preserving anatomical structure. We evaluate CARS across seven backbone architectures by fine-tuning models on synthetic subsets and testing on a held-out MIMIC-CXR benchmark. Compared to prior feature perturbation approaches, fine-tuning on CARS-generated images consistently improves precision-recall performance, reduces predictive uncertainty, and improves model calibration. Structural and semantic analyses demonstrate high anatomical fidelity, strong feature alignment, and low semantic uncertainty. Independent evaluation by two expert radiologists further confirms realism and clinical agreement. As the field moves toward regulated clinical AI, CARS demonstrates that anatomically faithful synthetic data generation for better feature space coverage is a viable and effective strategy for improving both the performance and trustworthiness of chest X-ray classification systems - without compromising clinical integrity.

item.title: PASTE: Physics-Aware Scattering Topology Embedding Framework for SAR Object Detection
item.content:encoded: Current deep learning-based object detection for Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery mainly adopts optical image methods, treating targets as texture patches while ignoring inherent electromagnetic scattering mechanisms. Though scattering points have been studied to boost detection performance, most methods still rely on amplitude-based statistical models. Some approaches introduce frequency-domain information for scattering center extraction, but they suffer from high computation cost and poor compatibility with diverse datasets. Thus, effectively embedding scattering topological information into modern detection frameworks remains challenging. To solve these problems, this paper proposes the Physics-Aware Scattering Topology Embedding Framework (PASTE), a novel closed-loop architecture for comprehensive scattering prior integration. By building the full pipeline from topology generation, injection to joint supervision, PASTE elegantly integrates scattering physics into modern SAR detectors. Specifically, it designs a scattering keypoint generation and automatic annotation scheme based on the Attributed Scattering Center (ASC) model to produce scalable and physically consistent priors. A scattering topology injection module guides multi-scale feature learning, and a scattering prior supervision strategy constrains network optimization by aligning predictions with scattering center distributions. Experiments on real datasets show that PASTE is compatible with various detectors and brings relative mAP gains of 2.9% to 11.3% over baselines with acceptable computation overhead. Visualization of scattering maps verifies that PASTE successfully embeds scattering topological priors into feature space, clearly distinguishing target and background scattering regions, thus providing strong interpretability for results.

item.title: VorTEX: Various overlap ratio for Target speech EXtraction
item.content:encoded: Target speech extraction (TSE) aims to recover a target speaker's voice from a mixture. While recent text-prompted approaches have shown promise, most approaches assume fully overlapped mixtures, limiting insight into behavior across realistic overlap ratios. We introduce VorTEX (Various overlap ratio for Target speech EXtraction), a text-prompted TSE architecture with a Decoupled Adaptive Multi-branch (DAM) Fusion block that separates primary extraction from auxiliary regularization pathways. To enable controlled analysis, we construct PORTE, a two-speaker dataset spanning overlap ratios from 0% to 100%. We further propose Suppression Ratio on Energy (SuRE), a diagnostic metric that detects suppression behavior not captured by conventional measures. Experiments show that existing models exhibit suppression or residual interference under overlap, whereas VorTEX achieves the highest separation fidelity across 20-100% overlap (e.g., 5.50 dB at 20% and 2.04 dB at 100%) while maintaining zero SuRE, indicating robust extraction without suppression-driven artifacts.

item.title: PCodeTrans: Translate Decompiled Pseudocode to Compilable and Executable Equivalent
item.content:encoded: Decompilation is foundational to binary analysis, yet conventional tools prioritize human readability over strict recompilability and verifiable runtime correctness. While recent LLM-based approaches attempt to refine decompiled pseudocode, they typically either optimize solely for readability or rely on static analysis for evaluation. This makes them prone to "semantic hallucinations" that compromise accuracy and fail to resolve actual runtime failures. For critical tasks like software modernization and vulnerability remediation, recovered code must not only compile but replicate the original binary's behavior. We present PCodeTrans, a feedback-driven framework that bridges the gap between decompilation, recompilation, and rigorous function-level dynamic validation. After extracting a minimal yet coherent context to guarantee recompilability, PCodeTrans employs an in situ substitutable engine to hot-swap the compiled function directly into the unmodified binary, natively preserving its authentic execution context and global dependencies. Guided by fine-grained differential tracing, PCodeTrans generates precise runtime feedback to iteratively guide an LLM in repairing semantic discrepancies. Evaluated on Coreutils and Binutils, PCodeTrans achieves unprecedented recovery performance when rectifying raw Hex-Rays outputs, attaining 100% function-level compilability on unstripped binaries alongside 99.55% and 99.89% test-validated behavioral consistency, respectively. In doing so, it resolves 76.56% and 79.74% of logic errors exposed by official test suites. Exhibiting exceptional resilience, PCodeTrans maintains over 96% behavioral consistency even on fully stripped binaries. By significantly outperforming all existing baselines, PCodeTrans paves a practical path to reliably translate decompiled pseudocode into compilable and executable equivalents.

item.title: AttnRes：改进残差连接以提升大语言模型性能
item.content:encoded:
AttnRes通过注意力机制优化残差连接，提升模型深度表现。



摘要： 本文提出AttnRes，一种基于注意力机制的残差连接方法，替代传统固定权重的残差聚合方式。AttnRes通过softmax注意力动态选择性地聚合前层输出，提升模型对深度信息的控制能力。为适应大规模训练，作者引入Block AttnRes，将层分块处理以降低内存开销。实验表明，AttnRes在不同模型规模上均有效，且在Kimi Linear架构中显著改善输出分布和下游任务性能。

item.title: 基于多模态融合的太阳能辐照度预测方法Baguan-solar
item.content:encoded:
Baguan-solar提升太阳能辐照度预测精度与稳定性。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为Baguan-solar的两阶段多模态框架，用于提高太阳能辐照度的预测精度。该方法结合了全球天气基础模型Baguan的预测结果与高分辨率静止卫星图像，实现了公里级的24小时辐照度预测。其分阶段设计首先预测昼夜连续的中间变量（如云量），再推断辐照度，同时通过多模态融合保留卫星图像中的精细云结构和Baguan预测的大尺度约束。在东亚地区使用CLDAS数据评估后，Baguan-solar优于多个基准模型，RMSE降低16.08%，并已在中国东部省份部署应用。

item.title: AI在未解数学问题上的进展探索
item.content:encoded:
AI在未解数学问题上取得潜在突破，需专家验证。



摘要： 文章介绍了HorizonMath，一个包含100多个主要未解问题的基准测试，覆盖8个计算和应用数学领域。该平台配有开源评估框架，用于自动化验证。由于这些问题尚未解决，因此不受数据污染影响，目前最先进的模型得分接近0%。研究发现，GPT 5.4 Pro提出了两个改进现有研究成果的解决方案，可能具有原创性。文章呼吁开放社区参与，以推动数学领域的创新。

item.title: 基于全差距矩阵的随机对抗强化学习最优臂识别研究
item.content:encoded:
提出新方法提升对抗强化学习中最优臂识别效率。



摘要： 本文研究在存在Condorcet优胜者的情况下，如何通过利用完整的差距矩阵来提高随机对抗强化学习中最优臂识别的效率。与以往仅关注优胜者与其他臂之间差距的方法不同，本文引入的新方法考虑了所有臂之间的差距信息，从而在高概率下实现了更优的样本复杂度。同时，作者还提出了新的下界分析，揭示了定位和估计差距矩阵中关键信息的内在成本，并证明了所提方法的非渐近界是优化的。研究结果展示了传统渐近分析未能捕捉到的样本复杂性新范式和权衡。

item.title: AdaAnchor：一种自适应的隐空间推理框架
item.content:encoded:
AdaAnchor提升推理效率并减少计算步骤。



摘要： 本文提出AdaAnchor，一种基于隐空间的推理框架，通过迭代优化输入相关的潜在锚点向量进行无声计算。该方法引入自适应停止机制，在锚点动态稳定时终止迭代，从而在相同最大步骤限制下减少平均推理步骤48-60%，同时提升准确率5%。相比传统推理方法，AdaAnchor显著降低生成文本量92-93%，实现更优的准确性与效率平衡。

item.title: RAZOR：一种高效的Transformer模型去遗忘框架
item.content:encoded:
RAZOR实现高效、精准的模型去遗忘，提升安全性与合规性。



摘要： 本文提出RAZOR，一种轻量级、模型无关的去遗忘框架，用于在不重新训练的情况下有效移除Transformer模型中的敏感或不需要信息。RAZOR通过识别对目标数据遗忘贡献最大的层和注意力头，并使用正则化规则进行更新，以避免影响整体性能。该方法在CLIP、Stable Diffusion和视觉语言模型上进行了评估，结果显示其在身份、风格和对象擦除任务中具有高精度和稳定性，且比以往方法更高效、更实用。

item.title: Cross-RAG：一种改进零样本时间序列预测的检索增强框架
item.content:encoded:
Cross-RAG提升零样本时间序列预测效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Cross-RAG的零样本时间序列预测增强框架，旨在解决现有方法在未见数据集上泛化能力不足的问题。该框架通过查询与检索样本之间的交叉注意力机制，选择性地关注与查询相关的样本，从而提高预测准确性。实验表明，Cross-RAG在多种时间序列基础模型和检索增强方法中均表现出色，并在不同检索场景下验证了其有效性。相关代码已开源。

item.title: 基于几何引导的新型视角合成方法GeoNVS
item.content:encoded:
GeoNVS提升视角合成的几何一致性和相机可控性。



摘要： 本文提出GeoNVS，一种基于几何引导的新视角合成方法，通过显式3D几何指导提升图像生成的几何保真度和相机控制能力。核心创新是Gaussian Splat Feature Adapter (GS-Adapter)，它将输入视角的扩散特征提升到3D高斯表示，生成几何约束的新视角特征，并自适应融合到扩散特征中以修正几何不一致。与以往在输入层注入几何的方法不同，GS-Adapter在特征空间操作，避免了视图依赖的颜色噪声。其即插即用设计支持多种前向几何模型，无需额外训练，且可适配其他视频扩散模型。实验显示GeoNVS在9个场景、18种设置中表现优异，相比SEVA和CameraCtrl分别提升11.3%和14.9%，翻译误差减少2倍，Chamfer距离减少7倍。

item.title: 基于机器学习的4D-STEM极化方向检测研究
item.content:encoded:
机器学习用于4D-STEM图像中极化方向检测，提升材料分析精度。



摘要： 本文研究了利用多种机器学习模型（如ResNet、VGG、自定义CNN和PCA增强k-NN）从铁电材料的4D-STEM衍射图中自动检测极化方向。尽管基于合成数据的模型在理想条件下表现良好，但模拟与实验之间的域差距仍是实际应用的主要障碍。通过自定义训练方案、PCA方法、数据增强和过滤，可以有效缩小这一差距。误差分析显示存在周期性误分类现象，表明并非所有衍射图都包含足够的分类信息。此外，模型预测模式的不规则性与晶体结构缺陷相关，表明监督模型可用于检测结构缺陷。该研究为电子显微镜分析提供了可迁移的机器学习工具。

item.title: 基于认知状态空间的视觉-语言模型幻觉检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型幻觉检测框架，提升VLM可信度。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的幻觉检测范式，将视觉-语言模型（VLM）的幻觉视为动态认知病理过程而非静态输出错误。该框架基于计算理性原则，通过信息理论探针将模型生成过程映射到可解释的低维认知状态空间。研究发现，认知轨迹的几何异常与信息理论中的意外性存在本质等价关系，从而将幻觉检测转化为几何异常检测问题。该方法在多种任务中表现优异，且在弱监督和数据污染环境下仍具高鲁棒性。它能够对失败进行因果归因，区分感知不稳定、逻辑因果失败和决策模糊等病理状态，为构建可透明、可审计、可诊断的AI系统提供新路径。

E2EGS通过事件流实现无姿态3D重建，提升动态场景建模精度。



摘要： 本文提出E2EGS，一种完全依赖事件流、无需已知姿态的3D重建框架。该方法利用边缘信息作为结构线索，通过分析事件流的时空特性提取边缘，有效抑制噪声，并引导高斯初始化和边缘加权损失函数。实验表明，E2EGS在合成与真实数据集上均实现了更高质量的重建和轨迹准确性，为事件相机的3D重建提供了新的范式。

item.title: 基于强化学习的交通信号控制算法研究
item.content:encoded:
强化学习在交通信号控制中表现优异，优于传统方法。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于强化学习的交通信号控制算法，能够实现与实际信号控制器一致的八相位环形结构。该算法在不同交通需求条件下进行训练和评估，并与现有感应信号控制方法进行对比。研究通过多组实验验证了其鲁棒性，并采用分布式异步训练架构提升训练效率。结果表明，该算法在平均延误方面比优化后的感应控制提升了11%-32%。模型在相似需求模式下表现良好，但在差异较大的需求下性能下降，而经过多样化需求训练的模型则表现出更强的适应能力。

Fast SAM 3D Body实现单目3D人体重建的实时化，提升10倍以上速度。



摘要： 本文提出Fast SAM 3D Body，一种无需训练的加速框架，通过解耦空间依赖和架构感知剪枝，实现多裁剪并行特征提取和简化Transformer解码，显著提升单目3D人体网格恢复的速度。同时，采用直接前馈映射替代迭代网格拟合，使关节级运动捕捉效率提升超10,000倍。该框架在保持与3DB相当重建精度的同时，实现10.9倍端到端加速，并成功应用于仅需RGB视频流的实时人形控制与操作策略采集系统。

Muon优化器在重尾噪声下实现稳定收敛与更快训练。



摘要： 本文研究了Muon优化器在具有重尾随机噪声的非凸Hölder光滑经验风险最小化问题中的表现。Muon通过将梯度投影到Stiefel流形上，实现了参数更新的正交性，从而提高了大规模深度神经网络训练的稳定性和效率。实验表明，在考虑重尾噪声的有界条件下，Muon能够收敛至经验风险的平稳点，并且其收敛速度优于小批量SGD。

item.title: 低资源语言机器翻译研究：以英语-埃菲克语为例
item.content:encoded:
研究评估了多语言神经机器翻译模型在英语-埃菲克语中的表现。



摘要： 本文研究了低资源语言埃菲克语的机器翻译效果，使用13,865句对的社区构建平行语料库，对mT5和NLLB200模型进行了微调。结果表明，NLLB200在英语-埃菲克和埃菲克-英语翻译中表现更优，BLEU分数分别为26.64和31.21，chrF分数分别为51.04和47.92，显示出更高的流畅性和语义准确性。研究证明了为低资源语言开发实用翻译工具的可行性，并强调了包容性数据实践和文化相关评估的重要性。

item.title: 基于物理先验的视觉-语言模型动态异常检测方法
item.content:encoded:
引入物理先验提升VLM在动态异常检测中的性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种融合物理先验的指令微调框架，通过结构化提示编码物体属性、运动模式和动态约束，提升视觉-语言模型（VLM）在动态异常检测中的表现。该方法通过多轮对话传递物理先验，将因果推理分解为逐步过程，增强了对正常与异常动态的鲁棒表征。在Phys-AD基准测试中，该方法在视频级检测上达到96.7%的AUROC，显著优于现有最佳方法（66.9%），并提供了更优的因果解释能力（LLM评分0.777）。研究展示了结构化物理先验如何使VLM成为可靠的动态异常检测工具。

item.title: WorldDrive：融合视觉与运动表示的端到端自动驾驶框架
item.content:encoded:
WorldDrive通过统一视觉与运动表示提升自动驾驶规划性能。



摘要： 本文提出WorldDrive，一个将场景生成与实时规划相结合的框架。该框架通过轨迹感知的驾驶世界模型，确保视觉动态与运动意图的一致性，从而生成多样且合理的未来场景。视觉和运动编码器被转移到下游多模态规划器中，使驾驶策略基于预优化的表示运行。此外，Future-aware Rewarder利用世界模型的预见性，通过未来潜在表示评估并选择最优轨迹。实验表明，WorldDrive在视觉-only方法中表现领先，同时保持高质量视频生成能力，验证了统一视觉与运动表示的有效性。

item.title: MP-GNN的区分能力与图同构问题研究
item.content:encoded:
MP-GNN区分能力有限，无法处理大量非同构图。



摘要： 本文研究了消息传递图神经网络（MP-GNN）的区分能力，发现其能产生的等价类数量仅为多项式级别，而非同构图的数量却是双指数级。通过对比颜色细化算法（CR），发现仅需两次迭代就能产生指数级等价类，表明MP-GNN在区分能力上存在明显不足。尽管已有研究表明MP-GNN可匹配完整的CR，但该结论基于较弱的区分标准，且不同图大小需要不同的MP-GNN模型。本文从区分非等价顶点和非同构图两个角度探讨了MP-GNN的能力边界。

item.content:encoded:
GlyphPrinter提升文本渲染准确性，减少字形错误。



摘要： 本文提出GlyphPrinter，一种基于偏好优化的文本渲染方法，旨在提高字形准确性。传统方法依赖大量高质量文本图像训练，但存在字形覆盖不足和过度风格化的问题。GlyphPrinter通过消除对显式奖励模型的依赖，结合区域级偏好标注数据集GlyphCorrector和Region-Grouped DPO（R-GDPO）优化策略，提升局部字形精度。同时引入区域奖励引导机制，在保持风格化与精确度平衡的前提下，显著优于现有方法。

item.title: 英语方言中大型语言模型的性能与数据质量研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨英语方言中大模型性能受限原因及改进策略。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）在英语方言中的表现问题，重点关注数据质量和可用性对模型改进的影响。通过评估Yorkshire、Geordie、Cornish、非洲裔美国英语和西弗里斯语等方言，发现人类间的一致性直接影响LLM作为评判者的性能。研究指出，LLM与人类的共识一致性模式相似，这在人口较少、一致性较低的地区带来挑战。同时，微调可能加剧这一模式，但也发现部分模型能生成高质量数据，为可扩展性提供希望。研究强调需谨慎评估数据以实现公平和包容的模型改进，并呼吁开发新工具应对数据稀缺问题。

item.title: Fusian：实现细粒度连续人格控制的框架
item.content:encoded:
Fusian实现对语言模型人格强度的精确连续控制。



摘要： 本文提出Fusian框架，用于在大型语言模型中实现细粒度、连续的人格控制。该框架分为两个阶段：轨迹收集阶段通过保存LoRA适配器序列来捕捉人格演变过程；强化学习动态融合阶段则利用策略网络动态计算适配器混合权重，从而根据用户指定的数值强度精准调整模型输出。实验表明，Fusian在Qwen3-14B模型上显著优于现有方法，实现了更高的控制精度。

item.title: 基于3D基础模型的多视角HOI视频重演方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MVHOI框架，实现复杂3D物体交互视频生成。



摘要： 本文提出MVHOI，一种两阶段的HOI视频重演框架，通过3D基础模型（3DFM）连接多视角参考条件与视频基础模型。3DFM首先生成跨视角一致的物体先验，再通过可控视频生成模型结合多视角参考图像合成高保真纹理，确保外观一致性。该框架在推理阶段相互增强，显著提升复杂3D物体交互视频的生成效果。

item.title: 基于物理信息的图神经扩散框架PiGRAND在热传导建模中的应用
item.content:encoded:
PiGRAND提升3D打印热传导预测精度与效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为PiGRAND的物理信息图神经扩散框架，用于优化热传导建模。该方法结合了机器学习与物理模型，通过高效图结构构建和迁移学习提升计算性能。研究在3D打印的热图像数据上验证了其有效性，结果表明PiGRAND在预测精度和计算效率方面均优于传统方法。该框架将偏微分方程理论融入学习模型，增强了对复杂热传输过程的模拟能力。

item.title: 基于极端值理论的束搜索宽度分析与优化
item.content:encoded:
束搜索宽度受评分器信噪比影响，过宽反而降低性能。



摘要： 本文基于极端值理论分析了束搜索宽度对大语言模型推理的影响。研究发现，当评分器噪声较高时，过宽的束搜索会导致系统性高估偏差，从而降低输出质量。通过实验验证，不同评分方式（如困惑度和PRM）对最佳束宽度有显著影响。高噪声评分（如困惑度）的最佳束宽度为1，而低噪声评分（如PRM）的最佳宽度可达4以上，性能提升达8.9个百分点。研究强调了评分器信噪比在束宽度选择中的核心作用，并提出了实际应用中的诊断指标。

item.title: 基于2D图像的视频生成与编辑框架ViFeEdit
item.content:encoded:
ViFeEdit实现无需视频数据的高效视频生成与编辑。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为ViFeEdit的视频生成与编辑框架，该框架无需任何视频训练数据，仅依赖2D图像即可实现高质量的视频生成和编辑。其核心思想是对视频扩散模型的架构进行重新参数化，将空间独立性与3D注意力机制解耦，从而在保持时间一致性的同时实现视觉忠实的编辑。此外，该框架采用双路径流水线结构，支持多种条件信号，并仅需少量2D图像训练即可取得良好效果。实验表明，ViFeEdit在可控视频生成和编辑任务中表现优异。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的反应图谱解析方法改进
item.content:encoded:
提升VLM在反应图谱解析中的性能与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言模型（VLM）在反应图谱解析（RxnDP）中的应用挑战，提出两种改进方法。首先，引入IdtVP方法，利用分子标识符激活预训练化学知识，增强零样本和分布外推理能力。其次，提出Re3-DAPO算法，通过可验证奖励优化反应级指标，提升微调效果。同时发布ScannedRxn基准数据集，用于评估模型鲁棒性与泛化能力。实验表明，这些方法显著提升了VLM在反应图谱解析任务中的准确性和适应性。

item.title: 基于上下文的符号回归在KAN中的应用
item.content:encoded:
研究KAN中符号回归的上下文方法，提升模型可解释性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN) 中进行上下文符号回归的方法，旨在提高模型的可解释性和验证性。传统方法在提取符号操作符时存在瓶颈，容易受到初始化和非凸参数拟合的影响。文中提出了两种互补方法：GSR通过端到端损失改进进行贪心上下文选择，而GMP则通过训练可微分门控操作层来优化选择过程。实验表明，该方法在多个任务中显著降低了中位数均方误差。

item.title: 基于单目热成像的无人机自主导航与SLAM方法研究
item.content:encoded:
使用单目热相机实现无人机在无GPS环境下的深度估计和SLAM。



摘要： 本文研究了在GPS不可用和视觉条件恶劣环境下，利用单目热成像相机实现无人机实时深度估计和同时定位与地图构建（SLAM）的方法。提出了一种轻量级监督网络，结合循环块以捕捉时间依赖性，提升预测鲁棒性。该网络融合轻量卷积主干与热精炼网络（T-RefNet），优化热图像特征。实验结果表明，在低光条件下，该方法在深度精度和SLAM性能上均优于现有基准，尤其在非辐射热数据集上表现突出。

item.title: 基于信号检测理论的大型语言模型校准分析
item.content:encoded:
LLMs校准分析揭示敏感性与偏差分离的重要性。



摘要： 本文通过信号检测理论（SDT）对三个大型语言模型进行校准评估，探讨温度调整对模型敏感性和偏差的影响。研究发现，温度不仅影响模型置信度，还改变了生成答案本身，导致敏感性（AUC）和判断标准同时变化。所有模型均表现出非等方差证据分布，且指令模型显示出更极端的不对称性。SDT分解显示，仅靠校准指标无法区分不同敏感性-偏差组合的模型，表明全参数框架能提供现有指标无法获取的诊断信息。

item.title: 跨域全景语义分割框架EDA-PSeg
item.content:encoded:
提出EDA-PSeg解决跨域全景分割中的几何失真和语义不确定性问题。



摘要： 本文针对跨域全景语义分割的挑战，提出了EDA-PSeg框架，该框架通过引入Euler-Margin Attention和Graph Matching Adapter，有效处理了视角变化和未见类别的语义不确定性。实验表明，该方法在多种场景下表现优异，具有良好的泛化能力和环境适应性。
