item.title: xplainfi: Feature Importance and Statistical Inference for Machine Learning in R
item.content:encoded: We introduce xplainfi, an R package built on top of the mlr3 ecosystem for global, loss-based feature importance methods for machine learning models. Various feature importance methods exist in R, but significant gaps remain, particularly regarding conditional importance methods and associated statistical inference procedures. The package implements permutation feature importance, conditional feature importance, relative feature importance, leave-one-covariate-out, and generalizations thereof, and both marginal and conditional Shapley additive global importance methods. It provides a modular conditional sampling architecture based on Gaussian distributions, adversarial random forests, conditional inference trees, and knockoff-based samplers, which enable conditional importance analysis for continuous and mixed data. Statistical inference is available through multiple approaches, including variance-corrected confidence intervals and the conditional predictive impact framework. We demonstrate that xplainfi produces importance scores consistent with existing implementations across multiple simulation settings and learner types, while offering competitive runtime performance. The package is available on CRAN and provides researchers and practitioners with a comprehensive toolkit for feature importance analysis and model interpretation in R.

item.title: A convolutional autoencoder and neural ODE framework for surrogate modeling of transient counterflow flames
item.content:encoded: A novel convolutional autoencoder neural ODE (CAE-NODE) framework is proposed for a reduced-order model (ROM) of transient 2D counterflow flames, as an extension of AE-NODE methods in homogeneous reactive systems to spatially resolved flows. The spatial correlations of the multidimensional fields are extracted by the convolutional layers, allowing CAE to autonomously construct a physically consistent 6D continuous latent manifold by compressing high-fidelity 2D snapshots (256x256 grid, 21 variables) by over 100,000 times. The NODE is subsequently trained to describe the continuous-time dynamics on the non-linear manifold, enabling the prediction of the full temporal evolution of the flames by integrating forward in time from an initial condition. The results demonstrate that the network can accurately capture the entire transient process, including ignition, flame propagation, and the gradual transition to a non-premixed condition, with relative errors less than ~2% for major species. This study, for the first time, highlights the potential of CAE-NODE for surrogate modeling of unsteady dynamics of multi-dimensional reacting flows.

item.title: A Hybrid Modeling Framework for Crop Prediction Tasks via Dynamic Parameter Calibration and Multi-Task Learning
item.content:encoded: Accurate prediction of crop states (e.g., phenology stages and cold hardiness) is essential for timely farm management decisions such as irrigation, fertilization, and canopy management to optimize crop yield and quality. While traditional biophysical models can be used for season-long predictions, they lack the precision required for site-specific management. Deep learning methods are a compelling alternative, but can produce biologically unrealistic predictions and require large-scale data. We propose a \emph{hybrid modeling} approach that uses a neural network to parameterize a differentiable biophysical model and leverages multi-task learning for efficient data sharing across crop cultivars in data limited settings. By predicting the \emph{parameters} of the biophysical model, our approach improves the prediction accuracy while preserving biological realism. Empirical evaluation using real-world and synthetic datasets demonstrates that our method improves prediction accuracy by 60\% for phenology and 40\% for cold hardiness compared to deployed biophysical models.

item.title: Directional Routing in Transformers
item.content:encoded: We introduce directional routing, a lightweight mechanism that gives each transformer attention head learned suppression directions controlled by a shared router, at 3.9% parameter cost. We train a 433M-parameter model alongside an identical baseline in a single run, then trace the resulting circuits through mechanistic interpretability. Routing becomes the model's dominant computational pathway. Disabling it collapses factual recall to near-zero probability across all 8 test prompts and drops induction accuracy from 93.4% to 0.0%. Knocking out individual attention heads has negligible effect: the primary mover head's removal actually increases target probability, and induction heads retain 98.6% accuracy without their strongest member. The coordination mechanism is irreplaceable; the components it coordinates are not. The model also self-organizes, without explicit pressure, into two regimes: domain-adaptive routing in early layers and fixed syntactic pruning in late layers, where the least-varying layer is the most critical (+42.6 PPL when disabled). Routing reduces perplexity 31-56% relative to the baseline, though downstream multiple-choice benchmarks do not yet reflect these gains.

item.title: Training-Free Generation of Protein Sequences from Small Family Alignments via Stochastic Attention
item.content:encoded: Most protein families have fewer than 100 known members, a regime where deep generative models overfit or collapse. We propose stochastic attention (SA), a training-free sampler that treats the modern Hopfield energy over a protein alignment as a Boltzmann distribution and draws samples via Langevin dynamics. The score function is a closed-form softmax attention operation requiring no training, no pretraining data, and no GPU, with cost linear in alignment size. Across eight Pfam families, SA generates sequences with low amino acid compositional divergence, substantial novelty, and structural plausibility confirmed by ESMFold and AlphaFold2. Generated sequences fold more faithfully to canonical family structures than natural members in six of eight families. Against profile HMMs, EvoDiff, and the MSA Transformer, which produce sequences that drift far outside the family, SA maintains 51 to 66 percent identity while remaining novel, in seconds on a laptop. The critical temperature governing generation is predicted from PCA dimensionality alone, enabling fully automatic operation. Controls confirm SA encodes correlated substitution patterns, not just per-position amino acid frequencies.

item.title: A Hybrid AI and Rule-Based Decision Support System for Disease Diagnosis and Management Using Labs
item.content:encoded: This research paper outlines the development and implementation of a novel Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) that integrates AI predictive modeling with medical knowledge bases. It utilizes the quantifiable information elements in lab results for inferring likely diagnoses a patient might have. Subsequently, suggesting investigations to confirm the likely diagnoses -- an assistive tool for physicians. The system fuses knowledge contained in a rule-base expert system with inferences of data driven predictors based on the features in labs. The data for 593,055 patients was collected from 547 primary care centers across the US to model our decision support system and derive Real-Word Evidence (RWE) to make it relevant for a large demographic of patients. Our Rule-Base comprises clinically validated rules, modeling 59 health conditions that can directly confirm one or more of diseases and assign ICD-10 codes to them. The Likely Diagnosis system uses multi-class classification, covering 37 ICD-10 codes, which are grouped together into 11 categories based on the labs that physicians prescribe to confirm the diagnosis. This research offers a novel system that assists a physician by utilizing medical profile of a patient and routine lab investigations to predict a group of likely diseases and then confirm them, coupled with providing explanations for inferences, thereby assisting physicians to reduce misdiagnosis of patients in clinical decision-making.

item.title: EditHF-1M: A Million-Scale Rich Human Preference Feedback for Image Editing
item.content:encoded: Recent text-guided image editing (TIE) models have achieved remarkable progress, while many edited images still suffer from issues such as artifacts, unexpected editings, unaesthetic contents. Although some benchmarks and methods have been proposed for evaluating edited images, scalable evaluation models are still lacking, which limits the development of human feedback reward models for image editing. To address the challenges, we first introduce \textbf{EditHF-1M}, a million-scale image editing dataset with over 29M human preference pairs and 148K human mean opinion ratings, both evaluated from three dimensions, \textit{i.e.}, visual quality, instruction alignment, and attribute preservation. Based on EditHF-1M, we propose \textbf{EditHF}, a multimodal large language model (MLLM) based evaluation model, to provide human-aligned feedback from image editing. Finally, we introduce \textbf{EditHF-Reward}, which utilizes EditHF as the reward signal to optimize the text-guided image editing models through reinforcement learning. Extensive experiments show that EditHF achieves superior alignment with human preferences and demonstrates strong generalization on other datasets. Furthermore, we fine-tune the Qwen-Image-Edit using EditHF-Reward, achieving significant performance improvements, which demonstrates the ability of EditHF to serve as a reward model to scale-up the image editing. Both the dataset and code will be released in our GitHub repository: https://github.com/IntMeGroup/EditHF.

item.title: Physics-Informed Neural Systems for the Simulation of EUV Electromagnetic Wave Diffraction from a Lithography Mask
item.content:encoded: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) and neural operators (NOs) for solving the problem of diffraction of Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) electromagnetic waves from contemporary lithography masks are presented. A novel hybrid Waveguide Neural Operator (WGNO) is introduced, based on a waveguide method with its most computationally expensive components replaced by a neural network. To evaluate performance, the accuracy and inference time of PINNs and NOs are compared against modern numerical solvers for a series of problems with known exact solutions. The emphasis is placed on investigation of solution accuracy by considered artificial neural systems for 13.5 nm and 11.2 nm wavelengths. Numerical experiments on realistic 2D and 3D masks demonstrate that PINNs and neural operators achieve competitive accuracy and significantly reduced prediction times, with the proposed WGNO architecture reaching state-of-the-art performance. The presented neural operator has pronounced generalizing properties, meaning that for unseen problem parameters it delivers a solution accuracy close to that for parameters seen in the training dataset. These results provide a highly efficient solution for accelerating the design and optimization workflows of next-generation lithography masks.

item.title: Federated Learning of Binary Neural Networks: Enabling Low-Cost Inference
item.content:encoded: Federated Learning (FL) preserves privacy by distributing training across devices. However, using DNNs is computationally intensive at the low-powered edge during inference. Edge deployment demands models that simultaneously optimize memory footprint and computational efficiency, a dilemma where conventional DNNs fail by exceeding resource limits. Traditional post-training binarization reduces model size but suffers from severe accuracy loss due to quantization errors. To address these challenges, we propose FedBNN, a rotation-aware binary neural network framework that learns binary representations directly during local training. By encoding each weight as a single bit $\{+1, -1\}$ instead of a $32$-bit float, FedBNN shrinks the model footprint, significantly reducing runtime (during inference) FLOPs and memory requirements in comparison to federated methods using real models. Evaluations across multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that FedBNN significantly reduces resource consumption while performing similarly to existing federated methods using real-valued models.

item.title: Training-free Detection of Generated Videos via Spatial-Temporal Likelihoods
item.content:encoded: Following major advances in text and image generation, the video domain has surged, producing highly realistic and controllable sequences. Along with this progress, these models also raise serious concerns about misinformation, making reliable detection of synthetic videos increasingly crucial. Image-based detectors are fundamentally limited because they operate per frame and ignore temporal dynamics, while supervised video detectors generalize poorly to unseen generators, a critical drawback given the rapid emergence of new models. These challenges motivate zero-shot approaches, which avoid synthetic data and instead score content against real-data statistics, enabling training-free, model-agnostic detection. We introduce \emph{STALL}, a simple, training-free, theoretically justified detector that provides likelihood-based scoring for videos, jointly modeling spatial and temporal evidence within a probabilistic framework. We evaluate STALL on two public benchmarks and introduce ComGenVid, a new benchmark with state-of-the-art generative models. STALL consistently outperforms prior image- and video-based baselines. Code and data are available at https://omerbenhayun.github.io/stall-video.

item.title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
item.content:encoded: Optimizing complex systems, ranging from LLM prompts to multi-turn agents, traditionally requires labor-intensive manual iteration. We formalize this challenge as a stochastic generative optimization problem where a generative language model acts as the optimizer, guided by numerical rewards and text feedback to discover the best system. We introduce Prioritized Optimization with Local Contextual Aggregation (POLCA), a scalable framework designed to handle stochasticity in optimization -- such as noisy feedback, sampling minibatches, and stochastic system behaviors -- while effectively managing the unconstrained expansion of solution space. POLCA maintains a priority queue to manage the exploration-exploitation tradeoff, systematically tracking candidate solutions and their evaluation histories. To enhance efficiency, we integrate an $\varepsilon$-Net mechanism to maintain parameter diversity and an LLM Summarizer to perform meta-learning across historical trials. We theoretically prove that POLCA converges to near-optimal candidate solutions under stochasticity. We evaluate our framework on diverse benchmarks, including $τ$-bench, HotpotQA (agent optimization), VeriBench (code translation) and KernelBench (CUDA kernel generation). Experimental results demonstrate that POLCA achieves robust, sample and time-efficient performance, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art algorithms in both deterministic and stochastic problems. The codebase for this work is publicly available at https://github.com/rlx-lab/POLCA.

item.title: Vietnamese Automatic Speech Recognition: A Revisit
item.content:encoded: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) performance is heavily dependent on the availability of large-scale, high-quality datasets. For low-resource languages, existing open-source ASR datasets often suffer from insufficient quality and inconsistent annotation, hindering the development of robust models. To address these challenges, we propose a novel and generalizable data aggregation and preprocessing pipeline designed to construct high-quality ASR datasets from diverse, potentially noisy, open-source sources. Our pipeline incorporates rigorous processing steps to ensure data diversity, balance, and the inclusion of crucial features like word-level timestamps. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our methodology by applying it to Vietnamese, resulting in a unified, high-quality 500-hour dataset that provides a foundation for training and evaluating state-of-the-art Vietnamese ASR systems. Our project page is available at https://github.com/qualcomm-ai-research/PhoASR.

item.title: MA-VLCM: A Vision Language Critic Model for Value Estimation of Policies in Multi-Agent Team Settings
item.content:encoded: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) commonly relies on a centralized critic to estimate the value function. However, learning such a critic from scratch is highly sample-inefficient and often lacks generalization across environments. At the same time, large vision-language-action models (VLAs) trained on internet-scale data exhibit strong multimodal reasoning and zero-shot generalization capabilities, yet directly deploying them for robotic execution remains computationally prohibitive, particularly in heterogeneous multi-robot systems with diverse embodiments and resource constraints. To address these challenges, we propose Multi-Agent Vision-Language-Critic Models (MA-VLCM), a framework that replaces the learned centralized critic in MARL with a pretrained vision-language model fine-tuned to evaluate multi-agent behavior. MA-VLCM acts as a centralized critic conditioned on natural language task descriptions, visual trajectory observations, and structured multi-agent state information. By eliminating critic learning during policy optimization, our approach significantly improves sample efficiency while producing compact execution policies suitable for deployment on resource-constrained robots. Results show good zero-shot return estimation on models with differing VLM backbones on in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios in multi-agent team settings

item.title: AURORA-KITTI: Any-Weather Depth Completion and Denoising in the Wild
item.content:encoded: Robust depth completion is fundamental to real-world 3D scene understanding, yet existing RGB-LiDAR fusion methods degrade significantly under adverse weather, where both camera images and LiDAR measurements suffer from weather-induced corruption. In this paper, we introduce AURORA-KITTI, the first large-scale multi-modal, multi-weather benchmark for robust depth completion in the wild. We further formulate Depth Completion and Denoising (DCD) as a unified task that jointly reconstructs a dense depth map from corrupted sparse inputs while suppressing weather-induced noise. AURORA-KITTI contains over \textit{82K} weather-consistent RGBL pairs with metric depth ground truth, spanning diverse weather types, three severity levels, day and night scenes, paired clean references, lens occlusion conditions, and textual descriptions. Moreover, we introduce DDCD, an efficient distillation-based baseline that leverages depth foundation models to inject clean structural priors into in-the-wild DCD training. DDCD achieves state-of-the-art performance on AURORA-KITTI and the real-world DENSE dataset while maintaining efficiency. Notably, our results further show that weather-aware, physically consistent data contributes more to robustness than architectural modifications alone. Data and code will be released upon publication.

item.title: TextOVSR: Text-Guided Real-World Opera Video Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: Many classic opera videos exhibit poor visual quality due to the limitations of early filming equipment and long-term degradation during storage. Although real-world video super-resolution (RWVSR) has achieved significant advances in recent years, directly applying existing methods to degraded opera videos remains challenging. The difficulties are twofold. First, accurately modeling real-world degradations is complex: simplistic combinations of classical degradation kernels fail to capture the authentic noise distribution, while methods that extract real noise patches from external datasets are prone to style mismatches that introduce visual artifacts. Second, current RWVSR methods, which rely solely on degraded image features, struggle to reconstruct realistic and detailed textures due to a lack of high-level semantic guidance. To address these issues, we propose a Text-guided Dual-Branch Opera Video Super-Resolution (TextOVSR) network, which introduces two types of textual prompts to guide the super-resolution process. Specifically, degradation-descriptive text, derived from the degradation process, is incorporated into the negative branch to constrain the solution space. Simultaneously, content-descriptive text is incorporated into a positive branch and our proposed Text-Enhanced Discriminator (TED) to provide semantic guidance for enhanced texture reconstruction. Furthermore, we design a Degradation-Robust Feature Fusion (DRF) module to facilitate cross-modal feature fusion while suppressing degradation interference. Experiments on our OperaLQ benchmark show that TextOVSR outperforms state-of-the-art methods both qualitatively and quantitatively. The code is available at https://github.com/ChangHua0/TextOVSR.

item.title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
item.content:encoded: Affordance prediction serves as a critical bridge between perception and action in embodied AI. However, existing research is confined to pinhole camera models, which suffer from narrow Fields of View (FoV) and fragmented observations, often missing critical holistic environmental context. In this paper, we present the first exploration into Panoramic Affordance Prediction, utilizing 360-degree imagery to capture global spatial relationships and holistic scene understanding. To facilitate this novel task, we first introduce PAP-12K, a large-scale benchmark dataset containing over 1,000 ultra-high-resolution (12k, 11904 x 5952) panoramic images with over 12k carefully annotated QA pairs and affordance masks. Furthermore, we propose PAP, a training-free, coarse-to-fine pipeline inspired by the human foveal visual system to tackle the ultra-high resolution and severe distortion inherent in panoramic images. PAP employs recursive visual routing via grid prompting to progressively locate targets, applies an adaptive gaze mechanism to rectify local geometric distortions, and utilizes a cascaded grounding pipeline to extract precise instance-level masks. Experimental results on PAP-12K reveal that existing affordance prediction methods designed for standard perspective images suffer severe performance degradation and fail due to the unique challenges of panoramic vision. In contrast, PAP framework effectively overcomes these obstacles, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and highlighting the immense potential of panoramic perception for robust embodied intelligence.

item.title: Video Detector: A Dual-Phase Vision-Based System for Real-Time Traffic Intersection Control and Intelligent Transportation Analysis
item.content:encoded: Urban traffic management increasingly requires intelligent sensing systems capable of adapting to dynamic traffic conditions without costly infrastructure modifications. Vision-based vehicle detection has therefore become a key technology for modern intelligent transportation systems. This study presents Video Detector (VD), a dual-phase vision-based traffic intersection management system designed as a flexible and cost-effective alternative to traditional inductive loop detectors. The framework integrates a real-time module (VD-RT) for intersection control with an offline analytical module (VD-Offline) for detailed traffic behavior analysis. Three system configurations were implemented using SSD Inception v2, Faster R-CNN Inception v2, and CenterNet ResNet-50 V1 FPN, trained on datasets totaling 108,000 annotated images across 6-10 vehicle classes. Experimental results show detection performance of up to 90% test accuracy and 29.5 mAP@0.5, while maintaining real-time throughput of 37 FPS on HD video streams. Field deployments conducted in collaboration with Istanbul IT and Smart City Technologies Inc. (ISBAK) demonstrate stable operation under diverse environmental conditions. The system supports virtual loop detection, vehicle counting, multi-object tracking, queue estimation, speed analysis, and multiclass vehicle classification, enabling comprehensive intersection monitoring without the need for embedded road sensors. The annotated dataset and training pipeline are publicly released to support reproducibility. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides a scalable and deployable vision-based solution for intelligent transportation systems and smart-city traffic management.

item.title: Persistence Spheres: a Bi-continuous Linear Representation of Measures for Partial Optimal Transport
item.content:encoded: We improve and extend persistence spheres, introduced in~\cite{pegoraro2025persistence}. Persistence spheres map an integrable measure $μ$ on the upper half-plane, including persistence diagrams (PDs) as counting measures, to a function $S(μ)\in C(\mathbb{S}^2)$, and the map is stable with respect to 1-Wasserstein partial transport distance $\mathrm{POT}_1$. Moreover, to the best of our knowledge, persistence spheres are the first explicit representation used in topological machine learning for which continuity of the inverse on the image is established at every compactly supported target. Recent bounded-cardinality bi-Lipschitz embedding results in partial transport spaces, despite being powerful, are not given by the kind of explicit summary map considered here. Our construction is rooted in convex geometry: for positive measures, the defining ReLU integral is the support function of the lift zonoid. Building on~\cite{pegoraro2025persistence}, we refine the definition to better match the $\mathrm{POT}_1$ deletion mechanism, encoding partial transport via a signed diagonal augmentation. In particular, for integrable $μ$, the uniform norm between $S(0)$ and $S(μ)$ depends only on the persistence of $μ$, without any need of ad-hoc re-weightings, reflecting optimal transport to the diagonal at persistence cost. This yields a parameter-free representation at the level of measures (up to numerical discretization), while accommodating future extensions where $μ$ is a smoothed measure derived from PDs (e.g., persistence intensity functions~\citep{wu2024estimation}). Across clustering, regression, and classification tasks involving functional data, time series, graphs, meshes, and point clouds, the updated persistence spheres are competitive and often improve upon persistence images, persistence landscapes, persistence splines, and sliced Wasserstein kernel baselines.

item.title: Unlocking the Value of Text: Event-Driven Reasoning and Multi-Level Alignment for Time Series Forecasting
item.content:encoded: Existing time series forecasting methods primarily rely on the numerical data itself. However, real-world time series exhibit complex patterns associated with multimodal information, making them difficult to predict with numerical data alone. While several multimodal time series forecasting methods have emerged, they either utilize text with limited supplementary information or focus merely on representation extraction, extracting minimal textual information for forecasting. To unlock the Value of Text, we propose VoT, a method with Event-driven Reasoning and Multi-level Alignment. Event-driven Reasoning combines the rich information in exogenous text with the powerful reasoning capabilities of LLMs for time series forecasting. To guide the LLMs in effective reasoning, we propose the Historical In-context Learning that retrieves and applies historical examples as in-context guidance. To maximize the utilization of text, we propose Multi-level Alignment. At the representation level, we utilize the Endogenous Text Alignment to integrate the endogenous text information with the time series. At the prediction level, we design the Adaptive Frequency Fusion to fuse the frequency components of event-driven prediction and numerical prediction to achieve complementary advantages. Experiments on real-world datasets across 10 domains demonstrate significant improvements over existing methods, validating the effectiveness of our approach in the utilization of text. The code is made available at https://github.com/decisionintelligence/VoT.

item.title: FairMed-XGB: A Bayesian-Optimised Multi-Metric Framework with Explainability for Demographic Equity in Critical Healthcare Data
item.content:encoded: Machine learning models deployed in critical care settings exhibit demographic biases, particularly gender disparities, that undermine clinical trust and equitable treatment. This paper introduces FairMed-XGB, a novel framework that systematically detects and mitigates gender-based prediction bias while preserving model performance and transparency. The framework integrates a fairness-aware loss function combining Statistical Parity Difference, Theil Index, and Wasserstein Distance, jointly optimised via Bayesian Search into an XGBoost classifier. Post-mitigation evaluation on seven clinically distinct cohorts derived from the MIMIC-IV-ED and eICU databases demonstrates substantial bias reduction: Statistical Parity Difference decreases by 40 to 51 percent on MIMIC-IV-ED and 10 to 19 percent on eICU; Theil Index collapses by four to five orders of magnitude to near-zero values; Wasserstein Distance is reduced by 20 to 72 percent. These gains are achieved with negligible degradation in predictive accuracy (AUC-ROC drop <0.02). SHAP-based explainability reveals that the framework diminishes reliance on gender-proxy features, providing clinicians with actionable insights into how and where bias is corrected. FairMed-XGB offers a robust, interpretable, and ethically aligned solution for equitable clinical decision-making, paving the way for trustworthy deployment of AI in high-stakes healthcare environments.

item.title: 基于信号到语义的故障诊断框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出S2S-FDD框架实现可解释的零样本故障诊断。



摘要： 本文针对工业系统中传统故障诊断模型无法回答‘为何’和‘如何修复’等问题，提出一种基于信号到语义的故障诊断框架（S2S-FDD）。该框架通过设计信号到语义的操作器，将高维时序信号转换为自然语言描述，再结合多轮树状诊断方法，参考历史维护文档并动态查询额外信号进行故障分析。框架还支持人机协同反馈以持续优化诊断结果。实验表明该方法在多相流过程中具有可行性与有效性，实现了可解释的零样本故障诊断。

item.title: SplitAgent：企业级隐私保护的云AI代理架构
item.content:encoded:
SplitAgent实现企业与云端AI协作，兼顾数据隐私与任务准确性。



摘要： 本文提出SplitAgent，一种分布式架构，使企业侧隐私代理与云端推理代理在保护数据隐私的前提下协同工作。其核心创新是基于任务语义的动态去敏技术，根据合同审查、代码审查或财务分析等不同任务调整隐私保护策略。SplitAgent引入差分隐私、零知识工具验证和隐私预算管理，实验证明其在企业场景中达到83.8%的任务准确率，同时保持90.1%的隐私保护，显著优于静态方法。上下文感知的去敏技术提升了24.1%的任务效用并减少了67%的隐私泄露。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的虚拟试穿框架BridgeDiff
item.content:encoded:
BridgeDiff提升虚拟试穿效果，增强服装结构稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为BridgeDiff的扩散模型框架，用于解决虚拟试穿（VTOFF）中的图像重建问题。该框架通过两个互补模块——服装条件桥接模块（GCBM）和扁平结构约束模块（FSCM）——来弥补人体外观与平铺服装布局之间的差异。GCBM通过全局外观和语义信息增强细节推理能力，而FSCM则在去噪过程中注入结构先验，提升服装结构的稳定性。实验表明，BridgeDiff在标准数据集上表现优异，能够生成高质量且结构完整的平铺服装图像。

item.title: 神经振荡器的泛化能力理论分析与验证
item.content:encoded:
研究神经振荡器在非线性系统中的泛化能力及优化方法。



摘要： 本文研究了基于二阶常微分方程和多层感知机的神经振荡器在复杂非线性结构系统中的学习能力。通过Rademacher复杂度框架，推导了其在连续时间函数空间上近似因果算子和渐进稳定动力系统的泛化上界。理论结果表明，估计误差随MLP规模和时间长度呈多项式增长，避免了参数复杂度的诅咒。此外，通过约束MLP的Lipschitz常数可提升泛化能力。数值实验验证了误差与样本量和时间长度之间的幂律关系，并证明了限制MLP矩阵和向量范数的有效性。

item.title: TA-GGAD: Testing-time Adaptive Graph Model for Generalist Graph Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: A significant number of anomalous nodes in the real world, such as fake news, noncompliant users, malicious transactions, and malicious posts, severely compromises the health of the graph data ecosystem and urgently requires effective identification and processing. With anomalies that span multiple data domains yet exhibit vast differences in features, cross-domain detection models face severe domain shift issues, which limit their generalizability across all domains. This study identifies and quantitatively analyzes a specific feature mismatch pattern exhibited by domain shift in graph anomaly detection, which we define as the \emph{Anomaly Disassortativity} issue ($\mathcal{AD}$). Based on the modeling of the issue $\mathcal{AD}$, we introduce a novel graph foundation model for anomaly detection. It achieves cross-domain generalization in different graphs, requiring only a single training phase to perform effectively across diverse domains. The experimental findings, based on fourteen diverse real-world graphs, confirm a breakthrough in the model's cross-domain adaptation, achieving a pioneering state-of-the-art (SOTA) level in terms of detection accuracy. In summary, the proposed theory of $\mathcal{AD}$ provides a novel theoretical perspective and a practical route for future research in generalist graph anomaly detection (GGAD). The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Anonymization-TA-GGAD/.

item.title: TeamHOI：基于物理的人形协作控制框架
item.content:encoded:
TeamHOI实现多智能体高效协作，提升人机交互性能。



摘要： 本文提出TeamHOI，一个用于多人形智能体协作人机交互的框架。该框架采用单一去中心化策略，通过Transformer网络实现团队间协调，支持不同规模的协作。为提高运动真实性并弥补数据不足，引入掩码对抗运动先验策略，利用单人参考动作生成多样且物理合理的协作行为。在涉及2至8个人形智能体的搬运任务中验证了其有效性，并设计了与团队规模和物体形状无关的形成奖励机制，实现了高成功率和协同表现。

item.title: 基于生物启发的持续强化学习方法ARROW
item.content:encoded:
ARROW提升持续强化学习性能，减少遗忘。



摘要： 本文提出ARROW（增强回放用于鲁棒世界模型），一种基于模型的持续强化学习算法，通过记忆高效的分布匹配回放缓冲区来解决灾难性遗忘问题。与传统固定大小的FIFO缓冲区不同，ARROW采用短期和长期两个互补缓冲区，以保持任务多样性。在Atari和Procgen CoinRun变体等任务中，ARROW表现出更少的遗忘并实现良好的知识迁移，验证了基于模型的RL和生物启发方法在持续学习中的潜力。

item.title: 强化学习中可验证奖励对齐任务的实证研究
item.content:encoded:
对齐任务不一定需要多样性算法，标准奖励最大化方法同样有效。



摘要： 本文通过在MoReBench数据集上的实验，比较了分布匹配方法与奖励最大化方法在对齐任务中的表现。研究发现，尽管道德推理任务存在多种合理答案，但奖励最大化方法并未表现出劣势。通过语义可视化分析，研究显示道德推理的高奖励分布更集中，而数学推理则有更多多样化的高奖励策略。这一结果表明，对齐任务并不一定需要保持多样性，标准的奖励最大化强化学习方法可以有效应用于道德推理。

item.title: Anime-like语音评估框架AnimeScore的提出
item.content:encoded:
提出AnimeScore评估框架，实现自动动漫语音评估。



摘要： 当前对‘动漫风格’语音的评估主要依赖主观判断，缺乏客观标准。为解决这一问题，本文提出AnimeScore，一种基于偏好排序的自动动漫语音评估框架。通过收集187名评价者提供的15,000组配对判断数据，结合声学分析发现，感知到的动漫风格主要由受控共振、语调连贯性和刻意发音驱动，而非简单规则如高音调。研究显示，手工提取的声学特征可达到69.3%的AUC，而基于SSL的排序模型则能达到90.8%的AUC，为生成式语音模型提供了有效的奖励信号。

item.title: 基于置信度的高效语言模型推理框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应推理框架，提升效率且保持准确率。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于置信度的决策框架，通过分析单一推理路径来决定采用单路径还是多路径推理，从而在保持与多路径方法相当准确性的同时，减少高达80%的token使用。该框架利用MedQA数据集中间推理状态的句级数值和语言特征进行训练，并在MathQA、MedMCQA和MMLU等任务中表现出良好的泛化能力，无需额外微调。实验表明，推理路径中包含丰富的不确定性信号，可用于平衡大语言模型的推理准确性和效率。

item.title: 基于健康数据的可解释性故障监测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种仅依赖健康数据的可解释性故障检测方法。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于健康数据的可解释性故障监测方法，通过学习健康状态的概率分布来检测异常。该方法利用概率偏差度量实现故障的早期识别，并结合贝叶斯框架进行不确定性量化，以支持决策。同时提供描述性工具提高结果的可解释性，适用于安全关键应用。在两个实际案例中验证了该方法的有效性，其检测性能优于现有先进方法。

item.title: 多流扰动攻击对大语言模型思考模式的威胁分析
item.content:encoded:
多流扰动攻击提升LLM越狱成功率并引发思考过程崩溃。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型（LLMs）在思考模式下面对越狱攻击时的安全风险。通过在单个提示中交织多个任务流，提出多流扰动攻击方法，包括多流交错、反转扰动和形状变换三种策略，分别通过并发任务干扰、字符反转和格式限制破坏模型的推理过程。实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上取得了超过多数现有方法的攻击成功率，并导致高达17%的思考崩溃率和60%的响应重复率，揭示了思考模式在处理复杂任务时的独特脆弱性。

item.title: TrianguLang：无需标定的3D物体与部分定位框架
item.content:encoded:
TrianguLang实现高效3D定位，提升机器人与AR应用性能。



摘要： TrianguLang是一种无需相机标定的3D定位框架，通过引入几何感知语义注意力机制（GASA），在跨视角特征匹配中抑制语义合理但几何不一致的匹配，从而提高定位精度。该方法在ScanNet++和uCO3D等五个基准数据集上取得最佳效果，将用户操作从多次点击简化为单个文本查询，每帧处理速度达57毫秒，适用于实时交互式机器人和增强现实应用。

item.title: 生成模型持续训练中的遗忘机制与优化分析
item.content:encoded:
研究生成模型持续训练中的遗忘现象及其优化方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了生成模型在持续训练过程中发生的遗忘现象，提出了一种基于双模混合抽象的理论框架，分析了两种主要形式的遗忘：质量遗忘和旧成分漂移。研究发现，正向KL目标会导致旧混合权重归零，而反向KL目标则能避免质量遗忘，并通过重叠门控概率控制旧成分漂移。文章进一步分析了回放机制对这两种目标的影响，并评估了三种近期提出的近策略后训练方法的遗忘表现。结果表明，遗忘可通过发散方向、几何行为重叠、采样制度及训练期间过去行为的可见性进行精确量化。

item.title: 基于客户端PCA统计的联邦学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需额外通信的联邦学习方法，提升数据异构性下的性能。



摘要： 本文针对数据异构性下的联邦学习挑战，提出一种新方法，通过将全局模型基于每个客户端本地计算的PCA统计量进行条件化，从而无需额外通信。该方法在四种数据集和七种基线方法上进行了评估，结果表明其在所有设置中表现与已知真实聚类分配的Oracle基线相当，在联合异构性场景下甚至优于该基线1-6%，且在稀疏数据情况下表现出独特鲁棒性。

item.title: 提升中文法律RAG系统的基准与框架研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出Legal-DC基准和LegRAG框架，提升法律文档检索生成效果。



摘要： 本文针对中文法律场景下检索增强生成（RAG）技术的局限性，提出了两个核心贡献：首先构建了Legal-DC基准数据集，包含480份法律文档和2,475个标注问题-答案对，填补了中文法律RAG评估资源的空白；其次提出LegRAG框架，结合法律自适应索引和双路径自我反思机制，提升答案准确性和条款完整性。此外，还引入了适用于大语言模型的自动化评估方法，以满足法律检索的高可靠性需求。实验表明，LegRAG在关键指标上优于现有方法1.3%至5.6%。研究为中文法律RAG系统的发展提供了基准、框架和实证支持。

item.title: TopoBench：评估大语言模型在拓扑网格谜题中的推理能力
item.content:encoded:
研究显示大语言模型在解决拓扑谜题中存在显著困难。



摘要： 本文介绍了TopoBench，一个包含六个谜题家族的基准测试，用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）在解决拓扑网格谜题中的推理能力。研究发现，即使是最先进的模型也仅能解决不到四分之一的难题，某些谜题几乎无法解决。通过分析750条思维链轨迹，研究者识别出四种可能的失败模式，并通过针对性干预验证了这些模式的影响。结果表明，提前做出承诺和遗忘约束是主要障碍，而重复推理则不影响解题能力。研究还探索了多种缓解策略，发现关键瓶颈在于从空间表示中提取约束，而非推理过程本身。

item.title: MEGC 2026：推动微表情分析的多模态模型挑战
item.content:encoded:
MEGC 2026提出两项任务，推动微表情识别与分析。



摘要： MEGC 2026是针对面部微表情（MEs）分析的最新研究挑战，旨在利用多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）和大视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）提升微表情的理解与检测能力。该挑战包含两项任务：ME视频问答（ME-VQA）和ME长视频问答（ME-LVQA），分别针对短时视频和长时视频中的微表情进行分析，要求模型具备跨时间的推理能力和对细微表情的识别能力。所有参赛算法需在公开排行榜提交结果，以促进相关技术的发展。

item.title: VarSplat：一种基于不确定性感知的3DGS-SLAM系统
item.content:encoded:
VarSplat通过显式学习每个点的外观方差，提升SLAM系统的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出VarSplat，一种基于3D高斯溅射（3DGS）的SLAM系统，旨在解决传统方法在低纹理区域、透明表面或复杂反射区域中因测量可靠性不足导致的位姿估计偏差问题。VarSplat通过引入总体方差定律与alpha混合技术，生成像素级不确定性图，用于指导跟踪、子地图配准和回环检测，从而提高优化稳定性。实验结果表明，VarSplat在Replica、TUM-RGBD、ScanNet等数据集上表现出更高的鲁棒性和优于或相当与现有方法的定位、建图及新视角渲染效果。

item.title: 基于物理因果推理的视频生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于物理因果推理的视频生成框架，提升视频的物理合理性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PPVG的视频生成方法，旨在生成符合物理规律的视频内容。现有方法依赖大语言模型的常识推理能力，但难以捕捉物理现象的因果演化过程。为此，作者设计了两个关键模块：物理驱动事件链推理和跨模态提示生成。前者通过分解物理现象为基本事件单元，并引入物理公式约束以减少因果歧义；后者则通过时间对齐的视觉-语言提示保持事件连续性。实验表明，该框架在多个物理视频生成基准上表现优异。

item.title: 基于PCA增强的医学图像分割方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PEP U-Net提升医学图像分割的不确定性处理能力。



摘要： 本文针对医学图像分割中的不确定性问题，提出一种基于PCA增强的概率U-Net（PEP U-Net）方法。该方法通过引入主成分分析（PCA）降低后验网络的高维潜在空间冗余，提高计算效率，并利用逆PCA操作重建关键信息以增强潜在空间的表达能力。与传统生成模型相比，该方法在保持生成多样分割假设能力的同时，实现了更高的分割准确性和预测变异性平衡，从而提升了生成建模在医学图像分割中的性能。

item.title: 揭示后门攻击中替代触发器的存在与防御策略
item.content:encoded:
研究发现后门攻击存在替代触发器，需从特征空间防御。



摘要： 本文指出，传统后门防御方法依赖于移除已知触发器，但这种以触发器为中心的视角并不完整。研究发现，存在与训练触发器在感知上不同的替代触发器，这些触发器同样能激活后门。通过对比干净样本和被触发样本的特征表示，作者估计了替代触发器在特征空间中的方向，并开发了一种结合目标预测和方向对齐的特征引导攻击方法。理论证明替代触发器是后门训练的必然结果，实验也验证了这一现象。此外，仅移除训练触发器的防御措施往往无法彻底消除后门，因为替代触发器可以利用潜在的后门特征空间。因此，研究建议应从特征空间而非输入空间的角度进行防御。

item.title: CEI-3D：一种用于精细3D编辑的协作显隐重建方法
item.content:encoded:
CEI-3D实现更真实、精细的3D编辑效果。



摘要： 本文提出CEI-3D，一种面向编辑的3D重建框架，通过结合显式和隐式的表示方式，提升3D模型编辑的精度与真实性。该方法利用隐式SDF网络提供全局几何先验，并通过显式控制点实现局部编辑。同时，设计物理属性解耦模块，分离颜色属性以避免编辑干扰。此外，引入空间感知编辑模块，通过跨视图传播策略实现部件级编辑。实验表明，该方法在真实和合成数据集上均优于现有技术，且编辑效率更高。

item.title: CacheSolidarity：在多租户LLM系统中安全利用APC缓存
item.content:encoded:
CacheSolidarity在不牺牲性能的前提下，解决了多租户LLM中的APC侧信道攻击问题。



摘要： 本文提出CacheSolidarity，一种用于多租户大型语言模型（LLM）服务系统的安全机制，能够在不牺牲性能的情况下防御APC（自动前缀缓存）侧信道攻击。APC通过重用请求前缀的计算状态来加速推理，但其缓存命中与未命中之间的延迟差异可能被攻击者利用以推断敏感信息。现有防御方法通常通过禁用APC和缓存共享来实现隔离，导致效率下降。而CacheSolidarity通过监控跨用户的缓存使用情况，仅在必要时对可疑前缀进行隔离，从而实现高效且安全的缓存管理。实验表明，该系统可提高70%的缓存重用率并降低30%的推理延迟，证明了安全性与性能可以兼得。

item.title: 基于画作目录的博物馆音视频内容自动化元数据生成
item.content:encoded:
利用视频语言模型实现博物馆音视频元数据自动化生成。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于现有画作目录的多模态归因方法，用于自动化生成博物馆音视频内容的目录式元数据。通过设计多阶段处理流程，包括视频中艺术品的摘要、目录风格描述与类别标签生成，以及通过保守相似性匹配进行标题和艺术家归属。该框架在绘画目录上的早期部署表明，它可以在尊重资源限制、数据主权和监管要求的前提下提升音视频档案的可发现性，并为其他高风险领域提供可迁移的机器学习应用模板。

item.title: 基于分段线性路径的全局逼近定理
item.content:encoded:
分段线性路径的签名线性泛函在L^p和加权范数下是稠密的。



摘要： 本文建立了分段线性路径空间上的全局通用逼近定理，表明在满足积分条件的权重函数下，对应的签名的线性泛函在L^p和加权范数下是稠密的。作为应用，证明了布朗运动的分段线性插值满足该积分条件，从而得到了路径依赖泛函、随机常微分方程和由布朗运动驱动的随机微分方程的L^p逼近结果。

item.title: Transformer在上下文学习中的统计决策机制研究
item.content:encoded:
Transformer通过构建任务自适应统计估计器实现上下文学习，而非简单相似性匹配。



摘要： 本文从统计决策理论角度研究Transformer在上下文学习（ICL）中的工作机制。通过二元假设检验设定，揭示了模型如何近似贝叶斯最优充分统计量，并在非线性任务中表现接近理想估计器。基于此分析，研究发现模型并未依赖固定核平滑策略，而是根据任务类型动态调整决策的线性可解性：线性任务呈现投票式集成特征，而非线性任务则依赖更深层的序列计算。这表明ICL源于任务自适应统计估计器的构建，而非简单的相似性匹配。

item.title: 基于工具增强的自主记忆检索框架TA-Mem
item.content:encoded:
TA-Mem提升长文本推理性能，支持灵活记忆检索。



摘要： 本文提出一种新型工具增强的自主记忆检索框架TA-Mem，以解决大语言模型在长文本推理任务中的上下文窗口限制问题。该框架包含三个核心组件：记忆提取LLM代理，用于根据语义相关性自适应切分输入并生成结构化笔记；多索引记忆数据库，支持基于关键字和相似度的多种查询方式；以及工具增强的记忆检索代理，可根据用户输入自主选择合适工具进行记忆探索，并决定是否继续迭代或输出最终结果。实验表明，TA-Mem在LoCoMo数据集上显著优于现有基线方法，并展示了对不同问题类型的适应性。

item.title: 基于GRACE策略的动态扩展框架在增量学习中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出GRACE框架提升增量学习性能并减少内存占用。



摘要： 本文针对增量学习中保持模型可塑性与稳定性之间的平衡问题，提出了一种基于动态扩展的GRACE框架。该框架通过‘增长、评估、压缩’的循环策略，自适应地管理模型容量，并引入饱和度评估阶段以判断模型是否需要扩展或压缩。实验表明，该方法在多个CIL基准测试中表现优异，同时相比纯扩展模型减少了73%的内存消耗。

item.title: AutoAgent：自进化多智能体框架提升长期学习与实时决策
item.content:encoded:
AutoAgent解决自主代理长期学习与实时决策的矛盾，提升适应性。



摘要： 文章介绍了AutoAgent，一个自进化的多智能体框架，旨在解决自主代理在长期经验学习和实时、上下文敏感决策之间的矛盾。该框架包含三个紧密耦合的组件：演化认知、即时上下文决策和弹性记忆协调。每个代理维护结构化的提示级认知，结合任务上下文选择动作，并通过弹性记忆协调器动态组织交互历史，减少令牌开销同时保留关键证据。实验表明，AutoAgent在检索增强推理、工具增强代理基准和具身任务环境中显著提升了任务成功率、工具使用效率和协作鲁棒性，为动态环境中的自适应自主代理提供了统一且实用的基础。

item.title: Computer Vision-Based Vehicle Allotment System using Perspective Mapping
item.content:encoded: Smart city research envisions a future in which data-driven solutions and sustainable infrastructure work together to define urban living at the crossroads of urbanization and technology. Within this framework, smart parking systems play an important role in reducing urban congestion and supporting sustainable transportation. Automating parking solutions have considerable benefits, such as increased efficiency and less reliance on human involvement, but obstacles such as sensor limitations and integration complications remain. To overcome them, a more sophisticated car allotment system is required, particularly in heavily populated urban areas. Computer vision, with its higher accuracy and adaptability, outperforms traditional sensor-based systems for recognizing vehicles and vacant parking spaces. Unlike fixed sensor technologies, computer vision can dynamically assess a wide range of visual inputs while adjusting to changing parking layouts. This research presents a cost-effective, easy-to-implement smart parking system utilizing computer vision and object detection models like YOLOv8. Using inverse perspective mapping (IPM) to merge images from four camera views, we extract data on vacant spaces. The system simulates a 3D parking environment, representing available spots with a 3D Cartesian plot to guide users.

item.title: Grow, Don't Overwrite: Fine-tuning Without Forgetting
item.content:encoded: Adapting pre-trained models to specialized tasks often leads to catastrophic forgetting, where new knowledge overwrites foundational capabilities. Existing methods either compromise performance on the new task or struggle to balance training stability with efficient reuse of pre-trained knowledge. We introduce a novel function-preserving expansion method that resolves this dilemma. Our technique expands model capacity by replicating pre-trained parameters within transformer submodules and applying a scaling correction that guarantees the expanded model is mathematically identical to the original at initialization, enabling stable training while exploiting existing knowledge. Empirically, our method eliminates the trade-off between plasticity and stability, matching the performance of full fine-tuning on downstream tasks without any degradation of the model's original capabilities. Furthermore, we demonstrate the modularity of our approach, showing that by selectively expanding a small subset of layers we can achieve the same performance as full fine-tuning at a fraction of the computational cost.

item.title: Scale-Plan: Scalable Language-Enabled Task Planning for Heterogeneous Multi-Robot Teams
item.content:encoded: Long-horizon task planning for heterogeneous multi-robot systems is essential for deploying collaborative teams in real-world environments; yet, it remains challenging due to the large volume of perceptual information, much of which is irrelevant to task objectives and burdens planning. Traditional symbolic planners rely on manually constructed problem specifications, limiting scalability and adaptability, while recent large language model (LLM)-based approaches often suffer from hallucinations and weak grounding-i.e., poor alignment between generated plans and actual environmental objects and constraints-in object-rich settings. We present Scale-Plan, a scalable LLM-assisted framework that generates compact, task-relevant problem representations from natural language instructions. Given a PDDL domain specification, Scale-Plan constructs an action graph capturing domain structure and uses shallow LLM reasoning to guide a structured graph search that identifies a minimal subset of relevant actions and objects. By filtering irrelevant information prior to planning, Scale-Plan enables efficient decomposition, allocation, and long-horizon plan generation. We evaluate our approach on complex multi-agent tasks and introduce MAT2-THOR, a cleaned benchmark built on AI2-THOR for reliable evaluation of multi-robot planning systems. Scale-Plan outperforms pure LLM and hybrid LLM-PDDL baselines across all metrics, improving scalability and reliability.

item.title: On the Width Scaling of Neural Optimizers Under Matrix Operator Norms I: Row/Column Normalization and Hyperparameter Transfer
item.content:encoded: A central question in modern deep learning is how to design optimizers whose behavior remains stable as the network width $w$ increases. We address this question by interpreting several widely used neural-network optimizers, including \textrm{AdamW} and \textrm{Muon}, as instances of steepest descent under matrix operator norms. This perspective links optimizer geometry with the Lipschitz structure of the network forward map, and enables width-independent control of both Lipschitz and smoothness constants. However, steepest-descent rules induced by standard $p \to q$ operator norms lack layerwise composability and therefore cannot provide width-independent bounds in deep architectures. We overcome this limitation by introducing a family of mean-normalized operator norms, denoted $\pmean \to \qmean$, that admit layerwise composability, yield width-independent smoothness bounds, and give rise to practical optimizers such as \emph{rescaled} \textrm{AdamW}, row normalization, and column normalization. The resulting learning rate width-aware scaling rules recover $μ$P scaling~\cite{yang2021tensor} as a special case and provide a principled mechanism for cross-width learning-rate transfer across a broad class of optimizers. We further show that \textrm{Muon} can suffer an $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{w})$ worst-case growth in the smoothness constant, whereas a new family of row-normalized optimizers we propose achieves width-independent smoothness guarantees. Based on the observations, we propose MOGA (Matrix Operator Geometry Aware), a width-aware optimizer based only on row/column-wise normalization that enables stable learning-rate transfer across model widths. Large-scale pre-training on GPT-2 and LLaMA shows that MOGA, especially with row normalization, is competitive with Muon while being notably faster in large-token and low-loss regimes.

item.title: Attention Gathers, MLPs Compose: A Causal Analysis of an Action-Outcome Circuit in VideoViT
item.content:encoded: The paper explores how video models trained for classification tasks represent nuanced, hidden semantic information that may not affect the final outcome, a key challenge for Trustworthy AI models. Through Explainable and Interpretable AI methods, specifically mechanistic interpretability techniques, the internal circuit responsible for representing the action's outcome is reverse-engineered in a pre-trained video vision transformer, revealing that the "Success vs Failure" signal is computed through a distinct amplification cascade. While there are low-level differences observed from layer 0, the abstract and semantic representation of the outcome is progressively amplified from layers 5 through 11. Causal analysis, primarily using activation patching supported by ablation results, reveals a clear division of labor: Attention Heads act as "evidence gatherers", providing necessary low-level information for partial signal recovery, while MLP Blocks function as robust "concept composers", each of which is the primary driver to generate the "success" signal. This distributed and redundant circuit in the model's internals explains its resilience to simple ablations, demonstrating a core computational pattern for processing human-action outcomes. Crucially, the existence of this sophisticated circuit for representing complex outcomes, even within a model trained only for simple classification, highlights the potential for models to develop forms of 'hidden knowledge' beyond their explicit task, underscoring the need for mechanistic oversight for building genuinely Explainable and Trustworthy AI systems intended for deployment.

item.title: Optimal Experimental Design for Reliable Learning of History-Dependent Constitutive Laws
item.content:encoded: History-dependent constitutive models serve as macroscopic closures for the aggregated effects of micromechanics. Their parameters are typically learned from experimental data. With a limited experimental budget, eliciting the full range of responses needed to characterize the constitutive relation can be difficult. As a result, the data can be well explained by a range of parameter choices, leading to parameter estimates that are uncertain or unreliable. To address this issue, we propose a Bayesian optimal experimental design framework to quantify, interpret, and maximize the utility of experimental designs for reliable learning of history-dependent constitutive models. In this framework, the design utility is defined as the expected reduction in parametric uncertainty or the expected information gain. This enables in silico design optimization using simulated data and reduces the cost of physical experiments for reliable parameter identification. We introduce two approximations that make this framework practical for advanced material testing with expensive forward models and high-dimensional data: (i) a Gaussian approximation of the expected information gain, and (ii) a surrogate approximation of the Fisher information matrix. The former enables efficient design optimization and interpretation, while the latter extends this approach to batched design optimization by amortizing the cost of repeated utility evaluations. Our numerical studies of uniaxial tests for viscoelastic solids show that optimized specimen geometries and loading paths yield image and force data that significantly improve parameter identifiability relative to random designs, especially for parameters associated with memory effects.

item.title: Agentic Critical Training
item.content:encoded: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents often begins with imitation learning, but it only teaches agents what to do without understanding why: agents never contrast successful actions against suboptimal alternatives and thus lack awareness of action quality. Recent approaches attempt to address this by introducing self-reflection supervision derived from contrasts between expert and alternative actions. However, the training paradigm fundamentally remains imitation learning: the model imitates pre-constructed reflection text rather than learning to reason autonomously. We propose Agentic Critical Training (ACT), a reinforcement learning paradigm that trains agents to identify the better action among alternatives. By rewarding whether the model's judgment is correct, ACT drives the model to autonomously develop reasoning about action quality, producing genuine self-reflection rather than imitating it. Across three challenging agent benchmarks, ACT consistently improves agent performance when combined with different post-training methods. It achieves an average improvement of 5.07 points over imitation learning and 4.62 points over reinforcement learning. Compared to approaches that inject reflection capability through knowledge distillation, ACT also demonstrates clear advantages, yielding an average improvement of 2.42 points. Moreover, ACT enables strong out-of-distribution generalization on agentic benchmarks and improves performance on general reasoning benchmarks without any reasoning-specific training data, highlighting the value of our method. These results suggest that ACT is a promising path toward developing more reflective and capable LLM agents.

item.title: FlexRec: Adapting LLM-based Recommenders for Flexible Needs via Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Modern recommender systems must adapt to dynamic, need-specific objectives for diverse recommendation scenarios, yet most traditional recommenders are optimized for a single static target and struggle to reconfigure behavior on demand. Recent advances in reinforcement-learning-based post-training have unlocked strong instruction-following and reasoning capabilities in LLMs, suggesting a principled route for aligning them to complex recommendation goals. Motivated by this, we study closed-set autoregressive ranking, where an LLM generates a permutation over a fixed candidate set conditioned on user context and an explicit need instruction. However, applying RL to this setting faces two key obstacles: (i) sequence-level rewards yield coarse credit assignment that fails to provide fine-grained training signals, and (ii) interaction feedback is sparse and noisy, which together lead to inefficient and unstable updates. We propose FlexRec, a post-training RL framework that addresses both issues with (1) a causally grounded item-level reward based on counterfactual swaps within the remaining candidate pool, and (2) critic-guided, uncertainty-aware scaling that explicitly models reward uncertainty and down-weights low-confidence rewards to stabilize learning under sparse supervision. Across diverse recommendation scenarios and objectives, FlexRec achieves substantial gains: it improves NDCG@5 by up to \textbf{59\%} and Recall@5 by up to \textbf{109.4\%} in need-specific ranking, and further achieves up to \textbf{24.1\%} Recall@5 improvement under generalization settings, outperforming strong traditional recommenders and LLM-based baselines.

item.title: Efficient RGB-D Scene Understanding via Multi-task Adaptive Learning and Cross-dimensional Feature Guidance
item.content:encoded: Scene understanding plays a critical role in enabling intelligence and autonomy in robotic systems. Traditional approaches often face challenges, including occlusions, ambiguous boundaries, and the inability to adapt attention based on task-specific requirements and sample variations. To address these limitations, this paper presents an efficient RGB-D scene understanding model that performs a range of tasks, including semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, orientation estimation, panoptic segmentation, and scene classification. The proposed model incorporates an enhanced fusion encoder, which effectively leverages redundant information from both RGB and depth inputs. For semantic segmentation, we introduce normalized focus channel layers and a context feature interaction layer, designed to mitigate issues such as shallow feature misguidance and insufficient local-global feature representation. The instance segmentation task benefits from a non-bottleneck 1D structure, which achieves superior contour representation with fewer parameters. Additionally, we propose a multi-task adaptive loss function that dynamically adjusts the learning strategy for different tasks based on scene variations. Extensive experiments on the NYUv2, SUN RGB-D, and Cityscapes datasets demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing methods in both segmentation accuracy and processing speed.

item.title: GGPT: Geometry Grounded Point Transformer
item.content:encoded: Recent feed-forward networks have achieved remarkable progress in sparse-view 3D reconstruction by predicting dense point maps directly from RGB images. However, they often suffer from geometric inconsistencies and limited fine-grained accuracy due to the absence of explicit multi-view constraints. We introduce the Geometry-Grounded Point Transformer (GGPT), a framework that augments feed-forward reconstruction with reliable sparse geometric guidance. We first propose an improved Structure-from-Motion pipeline based on dense feature matching and lightweight geometric optimisation to efficiently estimate accurate camera poses and partial 3D point clouds from sparse input views. Building on this foundation, we propose a geometry-guided 3D point transformer that refines dense point maps under explicit partial-geometry supervision using an optimised guidance encoding. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method provides a principled mechanism for integrating geometric priors with dense feed-forward predictions, producing reconstructions that are both geometrically consistent and spatially complete, recovering fine structures and filling gaps in textureless areas. Trained solely on ScanNet++ with VGGT predictions, GGPT generalises across architectures and datasets, substantially outperforming state-of-the-art feed-forward 3D reconstruction models in both in-domain and out-of-domain settings.

item.title: Reasoning as Compression: Unifying Budget Forcing via the Conditional Information Bottleneck
item.content:encoded: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting improves LLM accuracy on complex tasks but often increases token usage and inference cost. Existing "Budget Forcing" methods reducing cost via fine-tuning with heuristic length penalties, suppress both essential reasoning and redundant filler. We recast efficient reasoning as a lossy compression problem under the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle, and identify a key theoretical gap when applying naive IB to transformers: attention violates the Markov property between prompt, reasoning trace, and response. To resolve this issue, we model CoT generation under the Conditional Information Bottleneck (CIB) principle, where the reasoning trace Z acts as a computational bridge that contains only the information about the response Y that is not directly accessible from the prompt X. This yields a general Reinforcement Learning objective: maximize task reward while compressing completions under a prior over reasoning traces, subsuming common heuristics (e.g., length penalties) as special cases (e.g., uniform priors). In contrast to naive token-counting-based approaches, we introduce a semantic prior that measures token cost by surprisal under a language model prior. Empirically, our CIB objective prunes cognitive bloat while preserving fluency and logic, improving accuracy at moderate compression and enabling aggressive compression with minimal accuracy drop.

item.title: Parameterized Brushstroke Style Transfer
item.content:encoded: Computer Vision-based Style Transfer techniques have been used for many years to represent artistic style. However, most contemporary methods have been restricted to the pixel domain; in other words, the style transfer approach has been modifying the image pixels to incorporate artistic style. However, real artistic work is made of brush strokes with different colors on a canvas. Pixel-based approaches are unnatural for representing these images. Hence, this paper discusses a style transfer method that represents the image in the brush stroke domain instead of the RGB domain, which has better visual improvement over pixel-based methods.

item.title: Generation of maximal snake polyominoes using a deep neural network
item.content:encoded: Maximal snake polyominoes are difficult to study numerically in large rectangles, as computing them requires the complete enumeration of all snakes for a specific grid size, which corresponds to a brute force algorithm. This technique is thus challenging to use in larger rectangles, which hinders the study of maximal snakes. Furthermore, most enumerable snakes lie in small rectangles, making it difficult to study large-scale patterns. In this paper, we investigate the contribution of a deep neural network to the generation of maximal snake polyominoes from a data-driven training, where the maximality and adjacency constraints are not encoded explicitly, but learned. To this extent, we experiment with a denoising diffusion model, which we call Structured Pixel Space Diffusion (SPS Diffusion). We find that SPS Diffusion generalizes from small grids to larger ones, generating valid snakes up to 28x28 squares and producing maximal snake candidates on squares close to the current computational limit. The model is, however, prone to errors such as branching, cycles, or multiple components. Overall, the diffusion model is promising and shows that complex combinatorial objects can be understood by deep neural networks, which is useful in their investigation.

item.title: 基于最优控制的测试时规划层提升语言模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
引入TTC层实现推理与规划结合，提升语言模型数学推理性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Test-Time Control (TTC) 的新方法，将推理建模为最优控制问题，在推理阶段通过有限时间范围的LQR规划来优化模型行为。TTC层在潜在状态空间中表示价值函数，并将其作为嵌套目标用于规划，从而在预测前进行决策。研究还设计了一种基于对称形式的高效LQR求解器，并通过CUDA内核实现硬件加速。实验表明，TTC层显著提升了预训练语言模型在数学推理任务上的表现，如MATH-500数据集上提升27.8%，AMC和AIME数据集的Pass@8指标提升2-3倍，证明了将最优控制嵌入模型架构的有效性。

item.title: 高效数据混合方法提升大语言模型性能
item.content:encoded:
提出一种计算高效的混合方法，提升模型性能并降低优化成本。



摘要： 本文研究了如何通过有效的数据混合策略提升大语言模型的下游性能。针对现有方法在计算成本和可扩展性上的不足，作者提出了一种名为CAMEL的容量感知混合定律，并引入损失到基准预测法则，实现端到端的性能预测。进一步研究了在固定计算预算下如何分配模型规模以优化混合策略。实验验证了该方法在Mixture-of-Experts模型中的有效性，最终在55B-A1.2B目标模型上实现了性能提升，相比以往方法降低了50%的优化成本，提升了3%的基准性能。

item.title: 对比解码在大音频语言模型中的有效性分析
item.content:encoded:
对比解码提升大音频语言模型效果，但方法有效性因模型而异。



摘要： 本研究系统评估了四种对比解码策略在不同大音频语言模型架构中的表现，发现音频感知解码和音频对比解码最为有效。然而，这些方法的效果因模型而异。研究引入了转移矩阵框架来分析推理过程中的错误模式变化，表明对比解码能有效纠正模型误判无音频或依赖不确定猜测的错误，但无法修正错误推理或自信的错误断言。研究结果为选择适合对比解码增强的模型提供了指导。

item.title: 基于电子健康记录的老年人心血管风险自动分类框架
item.content:encoded:
研究提出一种自动化框架，提升老年心血管风险管理效率。



摘要： 本研究旨在解决传统人工编码在老年心血管风险管理中的局限性，提出一种基于非结构化电子健康记录（EHR）的自动化分类框架。通过分析3,482名患者的临床数据，对比了三种建模方法：传统机器学习、针对长序列优化的深度学习架构以及零样本设置下的通用生成大语言模型。同时评估了融合非结构化文本与结构化药物嵌入及人体测量数据的晚期融合策略。结果表明，定制的Transformer架构在F1分数和马修斯相关系数上均优于其他方法，证明了专用分层注意力机制在捕捉医学文本中长距离依赖关系方面的有效性，为临床风险分层提供了可靠的自动化替代方案。

item.title: 基于得分函数的生成模型对得分估计误差的鲁棒性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究显示Langevin动力学对得分估计误差不鲁棒，而扩散模型更可靠。



摘要： 本文探讨了基于得分函数的生成模型在得分估计存在误差时的鲁棒性。研究发现，Langevin动力学对L^2或更一般的L^p误差不鲁棒，即使得分估计误差极小，Langevin动力学在多项式时间范围内也无法生成接近目标分布的样本。相比之下，扩散模型在类似条件下仍能有效采样。由于实际中得分估计误差不可避免，该研究进一步支持扩散模型的应用，并警示避免使用基于估计得分的Langevin动力学。

item.title: 基于鲁棒策略优化的离线强化学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RRPI方法提升离线强化学习在分布偏移下的性能。



摘要： 本文针对离线强化学习在分布偏移下性能下降的问题，提出一种基于鲁棒策略优化的方法RRPI。该方法将转移核视为不确定集中的决策变量，通过KL正则化替代难以处理的极大极小目标，构建了一个高效的策略迭代过程。理论分析表明该方法具有γ-收缩性，并能保证原始鲁棒目标的单调改进和收敛。实验结果表明，RRPI在D4RL基准测试中表现优异，优于多种基线方法，且在高认知不确定性区域表现出更强的鲁棒性。

item.title: SynPlanResearch-R1提升模型网络信息检索能力
item.content:encoded:
SynPlanResearch-R1提升模型网络信息检索性能。



摘要： 本文提出SynPlanResearch-R1框架，通过合成工具使用轨迹来增强模型在冷启动阶段的探索能力，从而提高后续强化学习的效果。实验表明，在多个多跳和开放网络基准测试中，该框架相比现有最佳基线分别提升了Qwen3-8B和Qwen3-4B模型的性能6.0%和5.8%。研究还分析了工具使用模式和训练动态，揭示了性能提升的关键因素。

item.title: 基于AI的蜂窝流量需求空间预测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种减少空间泄漏的AI框架，提升流量预测准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了5G和6G网络规划中蜂窝流量需求的精准空间预测问题。传统机器学习方法在处理空间自相关数据时易出现邻域泄露，导致预测结果失真。为此，作者提出了一种基于上下文感知的两阶段划分策略，并结合残差空间误差修正机制，有效降低了泄露问题。实验基于加拿大五大城市的众包使用数据，验证了该框架在均方误差上的显著降低，为更可靠的带宽分配和频谱规划提供了支持。

item.title: EvoSchema：评估和增强文本到SQL系统在数据库模式演化中的鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
EvoSchema评估文本到SQL模型在数据库模式变化下的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出EvoSchema，一个全面的基准测试工具，用于评估和提升文本到SQL系统在真实世界数据库模式变化下的鲁棒性。EvoSchema引入了一种新的模式演化分类法，涵盖十种不同类型的列级和表级修改，以模拟数据库模式的动态特性。通过该基准，研究发现表级扰动对模型性能的影响远大于列级变化。此外，基于EvoSchema训练的模型能有效区分相同问题的不同模式，避免学习虚假模式，表现出更强的鲁棒性。该研究为设计适应动态环境的系统提供了重要见解。

item.title: 基于混合编码器与大语言模型的维度情感回归系统
item.content:encoded:
融合RoBERTa与LLM的集成学习提升情感分析性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了用于SemEval-2026任务3的维度情感回归系统。该系统结合了混合RoBERTa编码器与大语言模型（LLMs），通过预测级集成学习进行情感预测。混合编码器结合了连续和离散的情感表示，提高了预测稳定性。同时，研究探索了基于LLM的上下文学习和岭回归堆叠方法，以整合编码器与LLM的预测结果。实验结果显示，集成学习显著优于单一模型，在RMSE和相关性指标上均有提升。研究证明了编码器与LLM在维度情感分析中的互补优势。开发代码和资源已开源。

item.title: ToF NLOS成像方法的综合比较研究
item.content:encoded:
研究对比了多种ToF NLOS成像方法的性能与局限性。



摘要： 本文对多种时间飞行非视距（ToF NLOS）成像方法进行了全面分析，探讨了它们在相同理论模型和硬件条件下的异同。研究首先建立了统一的正向模型，分析了逆向模型的典型假设，并讨论了其与Radon变换的关系。同时，将这些模型迁移到频域，与基于相位的虚拟视距成像方法进行关联。实验表明，在相同硬件条件下，现有方法在空间分辨率、可见性和抗噪能力方面表现出相似的限制，但具体差异源于方法本身的参数设置。研究旨在为未来ToF NLOS成像方法的客观比较提供参考。

item.title: 提升大视觉语言模型鲁棒性的新框架与动态基准
item.content:encoded:
提出SCI框架和DRBench提升LVLM鲁棒性。



摘要： 文章介绍了大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）在多模态学习中的发展，指出当前训练方法对语言模型的依赖导致了语言偏见和敏感性问题。为此，作者提出了自批判推理（SCI）框架，通过多轮反事实推理增强鲁棒性，并引入动态鲁棒性基准（DRBench）以更准确评估模型可靠性。实验表明，SCI在DRBench上优于基线方法，且增加推理轮次可进一步提升性能。

item.title: 基于多摄像头输入的语义占用预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
M²-Occ提升多摄像头输入下的语义占用预测性能。



摘要： 本文研究在多摄像头输入不完整情况下的语义占用预测问题，提出M²-Occ框架，通过多视角掩码重建模块和特征记忆模块，有效恢复缺失视角信息并保持语义一致性。实验在nuScenes数据集上进行，结果表明在缺失后视图等场景下，M²-Occ显著提升了IoU指标，且不影响全视角表现。该方法具有良好的鲁棒性，适用于自动驾驶中的3D几何与语义理解任务。

item.title: EvalAct：通过显式评估提升多步推理的检索增强代理
item.content:encoded:
EvalAct通过显式评估提升多步问答的可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为EvalAct的方法，用于提升检索增强代理在多步骤推理任务中的可靠性。传统方法在多跳问答中容易受到噪声检索的影响，而仅基于结果的强化学习无法有效优化中间步骤。EvalAct将隐式的检索质量评估转化为显式的动作，并引入搜索到评估的耦合协议，使每次检索后立即进行结构化评估，从而获得与交互轨迹对齐的过程信号。此外，作者还提出了Process-Calibrated Advantage Rescaling (PCAR) 方法，通过GRPO优化方法根据评估分数在段级别重新校准优势，强调可靠段落并保守更新不确定部分。实验表明，EvalAct在七个开放域问答基准上表现最佳，特别是在多跳任务中取得显著提升，消融实验验证了显式评估循环是主要改进来源，而PCAR提供了持续的额外收益。

item.title: 单次遍历可能性聚类算法及其性能验证
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效单次遍历可能性聚类算法，适用于流数据处理。



摘要： 本文介绍了单次遍历可能性聚类（SPC）算法，该算法在处理流数据时表现出色，尤其适用于网络流量分析和传感器数据处理等场景。SPC通过引入模糊参数控制典型性衰减速度，能够有效建模非球形聚类，并采用多假设跟踪中的协方差合并方法来整合聚类均值与协方差估计。实验结果表明，SPC在聚类纯度和归一化互信息指标上优于其他五种流式聚类算法。

item.title: 大型语言模型中的隐蔽安全风险与对抗性攻击研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示LLM可能隐藏有害内容，通过隐写术实现安全对齐欺骗。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在安全对齐方面存在的隐蔽风险。研究发现，经过微调的模型可以利用隐写术技术，在看似正常的对话中隐藏有害内容。攻击者通过嵌入恶意问题，使模型生成包含有害信息的响应，而用户仅能看到表面的正常交互。该方法在GPT-4、Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct、Phi-4和Mistral-Small-24B-Base-2501等模型上均取得成功，并且在AdvBench数据集上的测试表明，这些隐藏的有害内容被错误地分类为安全内容。这表明当前的安全检测机制存在明显漏洞。

item.title: 基于大视觉语言模型的跨视角无人机定位方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型联合关系建模方法提升无人机与卫星图像匹配精度。



摘要： 本文研究如何通过跨视角无人机定位技术，将无人机拍摄的图像与地理参考卫星数据库进行精确匹配。现有方法在特征提取和相似性计算上存在局限，未能充分捕捉多视角间的交互关系。为此，作者提出一种可插拔的排名架构，结合大视觉语言模型（LVLM）学习深度视觉-语义关联，并设计了一种关系感知损失函数以优化训练过程。该方法通过软标签提供细粒度监督，避免对近似正样本过度惩罚，从而提高模型的判别能力和训练稳定性。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中显著提升了检索准确率，尤其在高挑战环境下表现优异。

item.title: TildeOpen LLM：提升欧洲语言多语言模型性能的开源解决方案
item.content:encoded:
TildeOpen LLM优化了欧洲语言的多语言模型性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了TildeOpen LLM，一款针对34种欧洲语言训练的300亿参数开源基础模型，旨在提升低资源语言的性能和语言公平性。通过数据集上采样和基于课程的训练策略，该模型在计算资源较少的情况下表现出色。评估结果显示，TildeOpen在文本生成和理解任务中优于其他多语言模型，尤其在波罗的海、芬诺-乌戈尔和斯拉夫语族中表现突出。人类评估也表明其语言错误减少了多达十倍。该模型及资源已公开可用，证明了精心的数据整理和平衡训练策略可以显著提升多语言模型的质量。

item.title: PII移除技术的隐私保护有效性分析
item.content:encoded:
PII移除技术可能无法有效防止隐私泄露，需更严谨评估。



摘要： 文章探讨了个人可识别信息（PII）从文本中移除的技术在实际应用中的隐私保护效果。尽管这些技术被广泛用于遵守数据保护法规和促进数据共享，但研究表明，经过PII移除的文档仍可能受到重建攻击。作者指出，现有攻击评估方法存在数据泄露和污染问题，导致对PII移除技术的有效性高估。研究强调，只有真正私有数据才能客观评估这些技术的漏洞，但由于数据获取受限，公共研究社区难以进行透明、可重复和可信的研究。

item.title: EQ-VMamba：首个具备旋转等变性的视觉Mamba架构
item.content:encoded:
提出首个旋转等变的视觉Mamba模型，提升鲁棒性与效率。



摘要： 本文针对当前基于Mamba的视觉架构缺乏旋转等变性的问题，提出EQ-VMamba，这是首个具备旋转等变性的视觉Mamba架构。该模型通过设计旋转等变的交叉扫描策略和分组Mamba块，有效提升了对图像旋转的鲁棒性。理论分析表明，该架构在端到端层面实现了旋转等变性。实验结果显示，EQ-VMamba在多个视觉任务中表现优于或与非等变基线相当，同时参数量减少约50%。这表明嵌入旋转等变性不仅增强了模型的鲁棒性，还提高了性能和参数效率。

item.title: 基于SELSM框架的隐私保护医学代理模型优化研究
item.content:encoded:
SELSM提升医学代理在隐私限制下的零样本能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种无需训练的SELSM框架，通过将模拟临床轨迹提炼为实体无关的操作规则，解决医学代理在数据稀缺和隐私约束下的部署难题。在MedAgentBench基准测试中，SELSM显著提升了本地部署基础模型的零样本能力，尤其在Qwen3-30B-A3B模型上实现了任务链无中断，成功率达到100%，整体成功率提升22.67%。该方法为医学AI代理在临床信息系统的本地化适应提供了一种高效且隐私保护的路径。

item.title: Wikidata限定符语义与分类研究
item.content:encoded:
研究Wikidata限定符的语义与使用，构建分类体系以提升知识图谱设计。



摘要： 本文深入分析了Wikidata中限定符的语义与实际使用情况，旨在构建一个能解决限定符选择、图查询和逻辑推理挑战的分类体系。通过改进的Shannon熵指数评估限定符的重要性，结合Wikidata数据集筛选出前300个限定符，并将其归类为上下文型、认知/不确定性型、结构型及其他类型。该分类体系有助于指导贡献者创建和查询语句，优化限定符推荐系统，并提升知识图谱的设计方法。研究结果表明，该分类体系有效覆盖了关键限定符，并提供了结构化的理解与应用方式。

item.title: 基于八叉树结构的3DGS渐进式编码方法
item.content:encoded:
提出ProGS实现高效3DGS数据压缩与渐进传输。



摘要： 本文针对3D高斯点云（3DGS）数据存储和传输中的压缩效率问题，提出一种基于八叉树结构的渐进式编码方法ProGS。该方法通过组织3DGS数据为八叉树结构，支持渐进式编码，提升压缩效率和视觉质量。ProGS引入互信息增强机制以减少结构冗余，并动态调整锚点节点以保证渲染质量。实验表明，ProGS在文件存储上实现了45倍的压缩率，同时提升了10%以上的视觉性能，适用于不同网络条件下的实时应用。

item.title: 家庭场景下视觉语言模型的安全检测基准与实时防护系统
item.content:encoded:
提出HomeSafe-Bench基准与HD-Guard系统，提升家庭机器人安全检测能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了HomeSafe-Bench，一个用于评估视觉语言模型在家庭场景中检测危险行为的基准，包含438个多样化案例和多维标注。同时，提出了HD-Guard系统，通过轻量级FastBrain和异步Large-Scale SlowBrain协同工作，实现高效的实时安全监控。实验表明该系统在延迟与性能之间取得良好平衡，并揭示了当前基于VLM的安全检测中的关键瓶颈。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的植物模拟配置生成研究
item.content:encoded:
利用VLM生成植物模拟配置，提升数字孪生农业应用效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种合成基准，用于评估视觉语言模型（VLMs）在生成植物模拟配置方面的性能。尽管功能结构植物模型（FSPMs）可用于模拟农业环境中的生物物理过程，但其复杂性和低吞吐量限制了大规模部署。研究采用Gemma 3和Qwen3-VL等先进开源VLM，从无人机遥感图像直接生成JSON格式的模拟参数。通过合成豇豆田数据集进行测试，评估了五种上下文学习方法，并在JSON完整性、几何评估和生物物理评估三个类别中进行了分析。结果显示，虽然VLM能够解析结构元数据并估计植物数量和太阳方位角，但在视觉线索不足时可能依赖数据集均值或受上下文偏差影响。通过真实无人机正射影像数据集验证和消融实验，进一步揭示了模型推理能力与对上下文先验的依赖关系。本研究首次将VLM应用于生成植物模拟的结构JSON配置，为农业数字孪生提供了可扩展的3D重建框架。

item.title: 基于强化学习的集合检索方法R4T
item.content:encoded:
R4T提升集合检索质量并降低延迟。



摘要： 本文提出R4T方法，通过强化学习优化集合级检索目标，解决传统方法在多样性、覆盖性等方面的不足。该方法分为三步：训练具有复合奖励的集合生成模型，生成目标一致的训练对，并训练轻量级扩散检索器。实验表明，R4T在时尚和音乐基准数据集上优于现有基线，同时显著降低查询时的扩展延迟。

item.title: 基于动态框架的网络影响力量化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出HawkesRank动态框架，提升网络影响力评估准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于多变量Hawkes点过程的动态框架HawkesRank，用于量化网络中的影响力。该框架结合外生驱动因素和内生放大效应，提供了一种更精确、可校准且适应性强的重要性度量方式。传统中心性指标如Katz中心性和PageRank被证明是该框架的均场极限，揭示了其有效性与局限性。相比静态平均方法，HawkesRank通过瞬时事件强度测量影响力，具备预测能力、透明的内外因分解以及对冲击的适应性。实验表明，HawkesRank在模拟和在线社交平台的情绪动态分析中表现优于传统静态指标。

item.title: 基于语义锚点的跨视角地理定位方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架提升无人机与卫星图像的地理定位鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对无人机图像与卫星图像之间的尺度不一致问题，提出了一种基于语义锚点的几何框架。该框架利用小车辆作为度量参考，通过解耦立体投影模型估计绝对图像尺度，以补偿透视失真并提高尺度估计精度。同时采用双维度融合策略减少类内尺寸差异和检测噪声，最终通过尺度自适应裁剪提升特征对齐效果。实验表明，该方法在未知尺度下显著提升了跨视角地理定位的鲁棒性，并具备在被动高度估计和三维模型尺度恢复等下游任务中的应用潜力。

item.title: BLooP：一种无需训练的解码干预方法提升摘要质量
item.content:encoded:
BLooP通过大词频查找提升语言模型摘要准确性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为BLooP的无需训练的解码干预方法，旨在提升大型语言模型生成摘要的质量。该方法通过在每个解码步骤中进行大词频查找，鼓励模型生成与源文档匹配的大词组。实验表明，BLooP在多个数据集上显著提升了ROUGE和BARTScore指标，并且在人类评估中显示出更高的忠实度而不影响可读性。该方法无需训练或微调模型，仅通过哈希表查找实现，具有良好的实用性。

item.title: 通过范畴论与检索增强生成消除大语言模型中的偏见
item.content:encoded:
文章提出结合范畴论与RAG技术以消除LLM中的性别和种族偏见。



摘要： 本文探讨了大语言模型（LLMs）中常见的系统性偏见问题，这些偏见常表现为对性别、种族和地理区域的刻板印象。文章提出一种双重方法，结合范畴论变换和检索增强生成（RAG），以实现公平的模型输出。范畴论提供了一种结构保持的数学框架，通过函子将有偏的语义域映射到无偏的规范形式，从而消除偏见并保持语义完整性。同时，RAG在推理过程中动态注入多样化的外部知识，直接对抗模型参数中的固有偏见。该方法结合了结构去偏和上下文强化，展示了其在提升模型公平性方面的有效性。
