item.title: Visualizing Coalition Formation: From Hedonic Games to Image Segmentation
item.content:encoded: We propose image segmentation as a visual diagnostic testbed for coalition formation in hedonic games. Modeling pixels as agents on a graph, we study how a granularization parameter shapes equilibrium fragmentation and boundary structure. On the Weizmann single-object benchmark, we relate multi-coalition equilibria to binary protocols by measuring whether the converged coalitions overlap with a foreground ground-truth. We observe transitions from cohesive to fragmented yet recoverable equilibria, and finally to intrinsic failure under excessive fragmentation. Our core contribution links multi-agent systems with image segmentation by quantifying the impact of mechanism design parameters on equilibrium structures.

item.title: Posterior Sampling Reinforcement Learning with Gaussian Processes for Continuous Control: Sublinear Regret Bounds for Unbounded State Spaces
item.content:encoded: We analyze the Bayesian regret of the Gaussian process posterior sampling reinforcement learning (GP-PSRL) algorithm. Posterior sampling is an effective heuristic for decision-making under uncertainty that has been used to develop successful algorithms for a variety of continuous control problems. However, theoretical work on GP-PSRL is limited. All known regret bounds either fail to achieve a tight dependence on a kernel-dependent quantity called the maximum information gain or fail to properly account for the fact that the set of possible system states is unbounded. Through a recursive application of the Borell-Tsirelson-Ibragimov-Sudakov inequality, we show that, with high probability, the states actually visited by the algorithm are contained within a ball of near-constant radius. To obtain tight dependence on the maximum information gain, we use the chaining method to control the regret suffered by GP-PSRL. Our main result is a Bayesian regret bound of the order $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(H^{3/2}\sqrt{γ_{T/H} T})$, where $H$ is the horizon, $T$ is the number of time steps and $γ_{T/H}$ is the maximum information gain. With this result, we resolve the limitations with prior theoretical work on PSRL, and provide the theoretical foundation and tools for analyzing PSRL in complex settings.

item.title: LLMs can construct powerful representations and streamline sample-efficient supervised learning
item.content:encoded: As real-world datasets become increasingly complex and heterogeneous, supervised learning is often bottlenecked by input representation design. Modeling multimodal data for downstream tasks, such as time-series, free text, and structured records, often requires non-trivial domain-specific engineering. We propose an agentic pipeline to streamline this process. First, an LLM analyzes a small but diverse subset of text-serialized input examples in-context to synthesize a global rubric, which acts as a programmatic specification for extracting and organizing evidence. This rubric is then used to transform naive text-serializations of inputs into a more standardized format for downstream models. We also describe local rubrics, which are task-conditioned summaries generated by an LLM. Across 15 clinical tasks from the EHRSHOT benchmark, our rubric-based approaches significantly outperform traditional count-feature models, naive text-serialization-based LLM baselines, and a clinical foundation model, which is pretrained on orders of magnitude more data. Beyond performance, rubrics offer several advantages for operational healthcare settings such as being easy to audit, cost-effectiveness to deploy at scale, and they can be converted to tabular representations that unlock a swath of machine learning techniques.

item.title: CINDI: Conditional Imputation and Noisy Data Integrity with Flows in Power Grid Data
item.content:encoded: Real-world multivariate time series, particularly in critical infrastructure such as electrical power grids, are often corrupted by noise and anomalies that degrade the performance of downstream tasks. Standard data cleaning approaches often rely on disjoint strategies, which involve detecting errors with one model and imputing them with another. Such approaches can fail to capture the full joint distribution of the data and ignore prediction uncertainty. This work introduces Conditional Imputation and Noisy Data Integrity (CINDI), an unsupervised probabilistic framework designed to restore data integrity in complex time series. Unlike fragmented approaches, CINDI unifies anomaly detection and imputation into a single end-to-end system built on conditional normalizing flows. By modeling the exact conditional likelihood of the data, the framework identifies low-probability segments and iteratively samples statistically consistent replacements. This allows CINDI to efficiently reuse learned information while preserving the underlying physical and statistical properties of the system. We evaluate the framework using real-world grid loss data from a Norwegian power distribution operator, though the methodology is designed to generalize to any multivariate time series domain. The results demonstrate that CINDI yields robust performance compared to competitive baselines, offering a scalable solution for maintaining reliability in noisy environments.

item.title: Influencing LLM Multi-Agent Dialogue via Policy-Parameterized Prompts
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as a new paradigm for multi-agent systems. However, existing research on the behaviour of LLM-based multi-agents relies on ad hoc prompts and lacks a principled policy perspective. Different from reinforcement learning, we investigate whether prompt-as-action can be parameterized so as to construct a lightweight policy which consists of a sequence of state-action pairs to influence conversational behaviours without training. Our framework regards prompts as actions executed by LLMs, and dynamically constructs prompts through five components based on the current state of the agent. To test the effectiveness of parameterized control, we evaluated the dialogue flow based on five indicators: responsiveness, rebuttal, evidence usage, non-repetition, and stance shift. We conduct experiments using different LLM-driven agents in two discussion scenarios related to the general public and show that prompt parameterization can influence the dialogue dynamics. This result shows that policy-parameterised prompts offer a simple and effective mechanism to influence the dialogue process, which will help the research of multi-agent systems in the direction of social simulation.

item.title: Beyond ReinMax: Low-Variance Gradient Estimators for Discrete Latent Variables
item.content:encoded: Machine learning models involving discrete latent variables require gradient estimators to facilitate backpropagation in a computationally efficient manner. The most recent addition to the Straight-Through family of estimators, ReinMax, can be viewed from a numerical ODE perspective as incorporating an approximation via Heun's method to reduce bias, but at the cost of high variance. In this work, we introduce the ReinMax-Rao and ReinMax-CV estimators which incorporate Rao-Blackwellisation and control variate techniques into ReinMax to reduce its variance. Our estimators demonstrate superior performance on training variational autoencoders with discrete latent spaces. Furthermore, we investigate the possibility of leveraging alternative numerical methods for constructing more accurate gradient approximations and present an alternative view of ReinMax from a simpler numerical integration perspective.

item.title: ALOOD: Exploiting Language Representations for LiDAR-based Out-of-Distribution Object Detection
item.content:encoded: LiDAR-based 3D object detection plays a critical role for reliable and safe autonomous driving systems. However, existing detectors often produce overly confident predictions for objects not belonging to known categories, posing significant safety risks. This is caused by so-called out-of-distribution (OOD) objects, which were not part of the training data, resulting in incorrect predictions. To address this challenge, we propose ALOOD (Aligned LiDAR representations for Out-Of-Distribution Detection), a novel approach that incorporates language representations from a vision-language model (VLM). By aligning the object features from the object detector to the feature space of the VLM, we can treat the detection of OOD objects as a zero-shot classification task. We demonstrate competitive performance on the nuScenes OOD benchmark, establishing a novel approach to OOD object detection in LiDAR using language representations. The source code is available at https://github.com/uulm-mrm/mmood3d.

item.title: Lost in Backpropagation: The LM Head is a Gradient Bottleneck
item.content:encoded: The last layer of neural language models (LMs) projects output features of dimension $D$ to logits in dimension $V$, the size of the vocabulary, where usually $D \ll V$. This mismatch is known to raise risks of limited expressivity in neural LMs, creating a so-called softmax bottleneck. We show the softmax bottleneck is not only an expressivity bottleneck but also an optimization bottleneck. Backpropagating $V$-dimensional gradients through a rank-$D$ linear layer induces unavoidable compression, which alters the training feedback provided to the vast majority of the parameters. We present a theoretical analysis of this phenomenon and measure empirically that 95-99% of the gradient norm is suppressed by the output layer, resulting in vastly suboptimal update directions. We conduct controlled pretraining experiments showing that the gradient bottleneck makes trivial patterns unlearnable, and drastically affects the training dynamics of LLMs. We argue that this inherent flaw contributes to training inefficiencies at scale independently of the model architecture, and raises the need for new LM head designs.

item.title: Evaluating Explainable AI Attribution Methods in Neural Machine Translation via Attention-Guided Knowledge Distillation
item.content:encoded: The study of the attribution of input features to the output of neural network models is an active area of research. While numerous Explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed to interpret these models, the systematic and automated evaluation of these methods in sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) models is less explored. This paper introduces a new approach for evaluating explainability methods in transformer-based seq2seq models. We use teacher-derived attribution maps as a structured side signal to guide a student model, and quantify the utility of different attribution methods through the student's ability to simulate targets. Using the Inseq library, we extract attribution scores over source-target sequence pairs and inject these scores into the attention mechanism of a student transformer model under four composition operators (addition, multiplication, averaging, and replacement). Across three language pairs (de-en, fr-en, ar-en) and attributions from Marian-MT and mBART models, Attention, Value Zeroing, and Layer Gradient $\times$ Activation consistently yield the largest gains in BLEU (and corresponding improvements in chrF) relative to baselines. In contrast, other gradient-based methods (Saliency, Integrated Gradients, DeepLIFT, Input $\times$ Gradient, GradientShap) lead to smaller and less consistent improvements. These results suggest that different attribution methods capture distinct signals and that attention-derived attributions better capture alignment between source and target representations in seq2seq models. Finally, we introduce an Attributor transformer that, given a source-target pair, learns to reconstruct the teacher's attribution map. Our findings demonstrate that the more accurately the Attributor can reproduce attribution maps, the more useful an injection of those maps is for the downstream task. The source code can be found on GitHub.

item.title: SoK: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): Taxonomy, Architectures, Evaluation, and Research Directions
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems are increasingly evolving into agentic architectures where large language models autonomously coordinate multi-step reasoning, dynamic memory management, and iterative retrieval strategies. Despite rapid industrial adoption, current research lacks a systematic understanding of Agentic RAG as a sequential decision-making system, leading to highly fragmented architectures, inconsistent evaluation methodologies, and unresolved reliability risks. This Systematization of Knowledge (SoK) paper provides the first unified framework for understanding these autonomous systems. We formalize agentic retrieval-generation loops as finite-horizon partially observable Markov decision processes, explicitly modeling their control policies and state transitions. Building upon this formalization, we develop a comprehensive taxonomy and modular architectural decomposition that categorizes systems by their planning mechanisms, retrieval orchestration, memory paradigms, and tool-invocation behaviors. We further analyze the critical limitations of traditional static evaluation practices and identify severe systemic risks inherent to autonomous loops, including compounding hallucination propagation, memory poisoning, retrieval misalignment, and cascading tool-execution vulnerabilities. Finally, we outline key doctoral-scale research directions spanning stable adaptive retrieval, cost-aware orchestration, formal trajectory evaluation, and oversight mechanisms, providing a definitive roadmap for building reliable, controllable, and scalable agentic retrieval systems.

item.title: Deep Learning-based Assessment of the Relation Between the Third Molar and Mandibular Canal on Panoramic Radiographs using Local, Centralized, and Federated Learning
item.content:encoded: Impaction of the mandibular third molar in proximity to the mandibular canal increases the risk of inferior alveolar nerve injury. Panoramic radiography is routinely used to assess this relationship. Automated classification of molar-canal overlap could support clinical triage and reduce unnecessary CBCT referrals, while federated learning (FL) enables multi-center collaboration without sharing patient data. We compared Local Learning (LL), FL, and Centralized Learning (CL) for binary overlap/no-overlap classification on cropped panoramic radiographs partitioned across eight independent labelers. A pretrained ResNet-34 was trained under each paradigm and evaluated using per-client metrics with locally optimized thresholds and pooled test performance with a global threshold. Performance was assessed using area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) and threshold-based metrics, alongside training dynamics, Grad-CAM visualizations, and server-side aggregate monitoring signals. On the test set, CL achieved the highest performance (AUC 0.831; accuracy = 0.782), FL showed intermediate performance (AUC 0.757; accuracy = 0.703), and LL generalized poorly across clients (AUC range = 0.619-0.734; mean = 0.672). Training curves suggested overfitting, particularly in LL models, and Grad-CAM indicated more anatomically focused attention in CL and FL. Overall, centralized training provided the strongest performance, while FL offers a privacy-preserving alternative that outperforms LL.

item.title: SurgCalib: Gaussian Splatting-Based Hand-Eye Calibration for Robot-Assisted Minimally Invasive Surgery
item.content:encoded: We present a Gaussian Splatting-based framework for hand-eye calibration of the da Vinci surgical robot. In a vision-guided robotic system, accurate estimation of the rigid transformation between the robot base and the camera frame is essential for reliable closed-loop control. For cable-driven surgical robots, this task faces unique challenges. The encoders of surgical instruments often produce inaccurate proprioceptive measurements due to cable stretch and backlash. Conventional hand-eye calibration approaches typically rely on known fiducial patterns and solve the AX = XB formulation. While effective, introducing additional markers into the operating room (OR) environment can violate sterility protocols and disrupt surgical workflows. In this study, we propose SurgCalib, an automatic, markerless framework that has the potential to be used in the OR. SurgCalib first initializes the pose of the surgical instrument using raw kinematic measurements and subsequently refines this pose through a two-phase optimization procedure under the RCM constraint within a Gaussian Splatting-based differentiable rendering pipeline. We evaluate the proposed method on the public dVRK benchmark, SurgPose. The results demonstrate average 2D tool-tip reprojection errors of 12.24 px (2.06 mm) and 11.33 px (1.9 mm), and 3D tool-tip Euclidean distance errors of 5.98 mm and 4.75 mm, for the left and right instruments, respectively.

item.title: Beyond Relevance: On the Relationship Between Retrieval and RAG Information Coverage
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems combine document retrieval with a generative model to address complex information seeking tasks like report generation. While the relationship between retrieval quality and generation effectiveness seems intuitive, it has not been systematically studied. We investigate whether upstream retrieval metrics can serve as reliable early indicators of the final generated response's information coverage. Through experiments across two text RAG benchmarks (TREC NeuCLIR 2024 and TREC RAG 2024) and one multimodal benchmark (WikiVideo), we analyze 15 text retrieval stacks and 10 multimodal retrieval stacks across four RAG pipelines and multiple evaluation frameworks (Auto-ARGUE and MiRAGE). Our findings demonstrate strong correlations between coverage-based retrieval metrics and nugget coverage in generated responses at both topic and system levels. This relationship holds most strongly when retrieval objectives align with generation goals, though more complex iterative RAG pipelines can partially decouple generation quality from retrieval effectiveness. These findings provide empirical support for using retrieval metrics as proxies for RAG performance.

item.title: Scaling Self-Supervised Speech Models Uncovers Deep Linguistic Relationships: Evidence from the Pacific Cluster
item.content:encoded: Similarities between language representations derived from Self-Supervised Speech Models (S3Ms) have been observed to primarily reflect geographic proximity or surface typological similarities driven by recent expansion or contact, potentially missing deeper genealogical signals. We investigate how scaling linguistic coverage of an S3M-based language identification system from 126 to 4,017 languages influences this topology. Our results reveal a non-linear effect: while phylogenetic recovery remains stagnant up to the 1K scale, the 4K model displays a dramatic qualitative shift, resolving both clear lineages and complex, long-term linguistic contact. Notably, our analysis reveals the emergence of a robust macro-cluster in the Pacific (comprising Papuan, Oceanic, and Australian languages) and investigates its latent drivers. We find that the 4K model utilizes a more concentrated encoding that captures shared, robust acoustic signatures such as global energy dynamics. These findings suggest that massive S3Ms can internalize multiple layers of language history, providing a promising perspective for computational phylogenetics and the study of language contact.

item.title: DSH-Bench: A Difficulty- and Scenario-Aware Benchmark with Hierarchical Subject Taxonomy for Subject-Driven Text-to-Image Generation
item.content:encoded: Significant progress has been achieved in subject-driven text-to-image (T2I) generation, which aims to synthesize new images depicting target subjects according to user instructions. However, evaluating these models remains a significant challenge. Existing benchmarks exhibit critical limitations: 1) insufficient diversity and comprehensiveness in subject images, 2) inadequate granularity in assessing model performance across different subject difficulty levels and prompt scenarios, and 3) a profound lack of actionable insights and diagnostic guidance for subsequent model refinement. To address these limitations, we propose DSH-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark that enables systematic multi-perspective analysis of subject-driven T2I models through four principal innovations: 1) a hierarchical taxonomy sampling mechanism ensuring comprehensive subject representation across 58 fine-grained categories, 2) an innovative classification scheme categorizing both subject difficulty level and prompt scenario for granular capability assessment, 3) a novel Subject Identity Consistency Score (SICS) metric demonstrating a 9.4\% higher correlation with human evaluation compared to existing measures in quantifying subject preservation, and 4) a comprehensive set of diagnostic insights derived from the benchmark, offering critical guidance for optimizing future model training paradigms and data construction strategies. Through an extensive empirical evaluation of 19 leading models, DSH-Bench uncovers previously obscured limitations in current approaches, establishing concrete directions for future research and development.

item.title: CausalTimePrior：生成时间序列因果模型的合成数据框架
item.content:encoded:
提出CausalTimePrior生成时间序列因果数据，支持因果推断模型训练。



摘要： 本文提出CausalTimePrior，一个用于生成具有观测和干预时间序列的合成时间序列因果模型（TSCMs）的框架。该框架支持可配置的因果图结构、非线性自回归机制、状态切换动态以及多种干预类型。通过在CausalTimePrior上训练的Prior-data fitted networks（PFNs），能够对未见的TSCMs进行上下文因果效应估计，为时间序列因果推断的基模模型提供了可行路径。

item.title: ComVo：基于复数运算的神经声码器提升语音合成质量
item.content:encoded:
ComVo采用复数运算提升语音合成质量，训练效率提高25%。



摘要： ComVo是一种基于复数运算的神经声码器，通过使用原生复数算术的生成器和判别器，实现了结构化的对抗训练框架。该方法引入相位量化以规范相位变换，并提出块矩阵计算方案以减少冗余操作，从而提升训练效率。实验表明，ComVo在语音合成质量上优于现有实数基基线模型，且训练时间减少了25%。相关音频样本和代码已公开。

item.title: Paced框架提升知识蒸馏效率
item.content:encoded:
Paced通过优化蒸馏过程提升模型训练效率。



摘要： 文章指出传统知识蒸馏在计算资源上存在浪费，主要体现在学生模型已掌握的问题和超出其能力范围的问题上。作者提出Paced框架，通过调整蒸馏的pass-rate权重，将注意力集中在学生模型的最近发展区，从而提高蒸馏效率。理论分析表明，该方法具有最小最大鲁棒性，并在多个任务中表现出色，包括正向KL和反向KL蒸馏，以及两阶段协同策略。所有配置仅需学生模型的输出即可估计pass率，无需架构修改。

item.title: FrameVGGT：基于帧驱动的有限内存3D感知框架
item.content:encoded:
FrameVGGT在有限内存下实现稳定3D感知，提升长序列任务性能。



摘要： 本文提出FrameVGGT，一种基于帧驱动的滚动显式记忆框架，用于解决流式3D感知中KV缓存无限制增长的问题。通过将每帧的增量KV贡献视为连贯证据块，并将其压缩为紧凑原型，FrameVGGT在固定容量下维护互补帧块，同时可选轻量锚点处理罕见长时间退化情况。实验表明，在长序列3D重建、视频深度估计和相机位姿任务中，FrameVGGT在有限内存下实现了良好的准确率与内存权衡，并保持更稳定的几何结构。

item.title: 基于物理的强化学习与仿真训练在外骨骼控制中的应用验证
item.content:encoded:
研究提出RL框架和验证流程，验证仿真训练外骨骼控制器的有效性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于物理的强化学习框架，用于学习减少生物关节力矩的外骨骼辅助策略，并开发了一个验证流程，利用公开步态数据集对训练后的控制网络进行推理和比较。研究中使用多层感知机控制器，针对平地和坡道行走，将双侧髋膝关节运动历史映射为归一化辅助扭矩。结果显示，预测的辅助扭矩在不同速度和坡度下保持任务强度趋势，尤其在髋关节表现出高度一致性。然而，在更高速度和更陡坡度下，差异增大，尤其是在膝关节处更为明显。延迟调优会影响正向功率注入，适度的时间偏移可提高特定步态阶段的一致性。该研究建立了仿真训练外骨骼控制器的定量验证框架，展示了良好的仿真到数据一致性，并指出了仿真到现实转移的潜力与挑战。

item.title: 基于残差动作的世界模型提升强化学习性能
item.content:encoded:
ResWM通过残差动作提升RL控制稳定性与效率。



摘要： 本文提出Residual-Action World Model（ResWM），将控制变量从绝对动作改为残差动作，即相对于上一步的增量调整。这种设计更符合现实世界控制的平滑性，减少搜索空间并提高长期规划的稳定性。同时引入Observation Difference Encoder，显式建模相邻帧的变化，生成与残差动作耦合的紧凑潜在动态。ResWM在Dreamer框架中实现，无需额外超参数。实验表明，ResWM在样本效率、最终回报和控制平滑性方面均优于Dreamer和TD-MPC，且生成的轨迹更稳定、能耗更低，对实际机器人部署具有重要意义。

item.title: PolyFormer：基于物理知识的优化模型新方法
item.content:encoded:
PolyFormer提升约束优化效率，实现快速求解。



摘要： 本文提出PolyFormer，一种将物理和几何知识直接用于简化优化问题的新方法。通过捕捉约束背后的几何结构并将其转化为高效的多面体形式，PolyFormer有效降低了问题复杂度，使传统优化求解器能高效生成可行解。在资源聚合、网络约束优化和不确定性优化等任务中，PolyFormer实现了高达6400倍的计算加速和99.87%的内存减少，同时保持了与先进方法相当或更优的解质量。该方法为科学发现和工程应用中的可扩展约束优化提供了高效可靠的解决方案。

item.title: 基于神经科学的视觉-语言-动作系统设计与优化
item.content:encoded:
提出一种神经科学启发的视觉-语言-动作系统框架。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经科学原理的视觉-语言-动作系统设计，包括大脑皮层、桥脑和小脑的模块化结构。该系统通过固定比例调度和两阶段特征缓存实现计算高效性和可重复性。研究展示了在LIBERO数据集上的初步实验结果，显示特征缓存分割可显著减少训练时间并提高成功率，SaiVLA0模型达到99.0%的平均成功率。

item.title: 基于测试时自适应的在线类别发现方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架提升在线类别发现效果。



摘要： 本文针对在线类别发现（OCD）中现有方法的局限性，提出了一种测试时自适应框架。该框架通过语义感知原型更新和稳定测试时编码器更新，实现模型在发现新类别时的持续学习。同时，在离线阶段引入边界感知的logit校准，增强类间差异并提升类内紧凑性，为未来类别发现预留嵌入空间。实验表明，该方法在标准基准上显著优于现有哈希方法，有效提升了新类别识别准确率并缓解了类别爆炸问题。

item.title: 跨持久图密度分析与机器学习应用研究
item.content:encoded:
研究跨持久图密度及其在点云区分中的应用。



摘要： 本文首次系统研究了跨持久图（cross-persistence diagrams）的密度，证明了其存在性并建立了统计基础。作者设计了一个机器学习框架，直接从点云坐标和距离矩阵预测跨持久图密度。该方法利用跨持久图的线性特性，有效区分来自不同流形的点云数据。实验表明，引入噪声可以提升区分能力，且该方法在多种数据集上优于现有技术。研究为跨持久图的应用提供了新方向，有望拓展至时间序列和AI生成文本的几何分析。

item.title: 基于边界感知的流式TTS优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种流式TTS优化策略，提升语音合成质量与稳定性。



摘要： 本文针对流式文本语音合成（Streaming TTS）面临的韵律不自然和长文本崩溃问题，提出了一种基于韵律边界感知的后训练策略。该方法通过弱时间对齐数据微调预训练的LLM-TTS模型，使模型在有限未来文本下能提前停止。推理阶段采用滑动窗口提示，保持上下文受限并实现无缝拼接。实验表明，该方法在短文本和长文本合成中均优于现有基线，特别是在长文本合成中，词错误率降低了66.2%，同时提升了说话人和情感相似度。

item.title: 量子机器学习中的频谱表达增强方法
item.content:encoded:
量子模型通过残差学习提升多频成分学习能力。



摘要： 本文研究了基于参数化电路的量子机器学习模型在学习多频率函数时的局限性，提出通过残差学习方法迭代训练额外量子模块以提升模型对高频或非主导频率的捕捉能力。实验表明，残差学习显著提升了模型的测试均方误差，且量子比特数量、编码方案和残差结构对多频解析至关重要。该工作为增强量子模型的频谱表达能力提供了实用框架，并揭示了其频率学习行为的新见解。

item.title: 结构化通用人工智能理论SMGI的提出与分析
item.content:encoded:
SMGI提出了一种结构化通用人工智能理论，强调学习接口的动态演化。



摘要： 本文提出了SMGI（结构化通用人工智能）理论，将传统学习问题从固定环境中的假设优化重新定义为学习接口的受控演化。通过引入一个类型化的元模型θ，该模型将表示映射、假设空间、结构先验、多模式评估器和记忆操作器作为动态组件进行形式化。文章定义了通用人工智能为满足四类条件的耦合动力系统(θ, T_θ)，包括结构闭包、动态稳定性、有限统计能力以及评估不变性。同时，证明了一个结构化泛化边界，连接了序列PAC-Bayes分析与Lyapunov稳定性，并展示了经典方法如经验风险最小化、强化学习等均为SMGI的结构受限实例。

item.title: VisionCreator-R1：一种具有显式反思机制的视觉生成代理
item.content:encoded:
提出具备反思机制的视觉生成代理VisionCreator-R1，提升多图像生成效果。



摘要： 文章介绍了VisionCreator-R1，这是一种具有显式反思机制的视觉生成代理，旨在解决现有生成模型在多图像工作流中缺乏系统性反思和纠错能力的问题。通过引入Reflection-Plan Co-Optimization (RPCO) 训练方法，该模型在自建数据集VCR-SFT和VCR-RL上进行训练与优化，有效提升了生成质量。实验结果显示，VisionCreator-R1在多个基准测试中表现优于Gemini2.5Pro，并在单图与多图任务中均展现出优越性能。

item.title: Backdoor4Good：利用后门机制提升大语言模型的可信性
item.content:encoded:
后门机制可用于提升模型安全性与可控性，而非仅作为威胁。



摘要： 本文提出 Backdoor4Good (B4G)，一个统一的基准和框架，用于在大语言模型中实现有益的后门应用。不同于传统研究关注攻击与防御，B4G 将后门机制用于增强安全性和可控制性。该框架基于三元组形式 (T, A, U)，分别表示触发器、激活机制和效用函数，并涵盖四个以信任为核心的用途。实验表明，有益后门在保持任务性能的同时，具备高可控性、抗篡改性和隐蔽性。研究揭示后门不一定是恶意的，合理设计下可成为可信 AI 系统的重要组成部分。

item.title: MM-CondChain：面向多模态大语言模型的深度组合推理基准
item.content:encoded:
提出MM-CondChain，评估MLLM在视觉组合条件下的深度推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出了MM-CondChain，一个用于评估多模态大语言模型（MLLM）在视觉基础上进行深度组合推理能力的基准。该基准通过多层推理链组织每个实例，每层包含基于视觉证据的复杂组合条件。为构建此类数据，作者设计了代理合成流程，包括规划器、可验证程序中间表示（VPIR）和合成器。实验表明，即使最强的模型也仅达到53.33的Path F1分数，且在复杂任务中表现显著下降，证明深度组合推理仍是重大挑战。

item.title: 基于动态代理的图像去雨方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Derain-Agent框架提升图像去雨效果。



摘要： 尽管深度学习在单图像去雨方面取得进展，但现有模型因采用静态推理方式，难以应对真实场景中的复杂退化问题，导致复原图像存在残留伪影和感知质量不一致。本文提出Derain-Agent，一种可插拔的精修框架，将去雨过程从静态处理转向动态代理式恢复。该框架通过规划网络智能调度修复工具序列，并利用强度调节机制实现空间自适应应用，从而在不增加计算成本的情况下精准修正残余错误。实验表明，该方法在合成与真实数据集上均显著提升了先进去雨模型的性能。

item.title: MedPruner：一种高效的3D医学图像理解令牌剪枝框架
item.content:encoded:
MedPruner提升3D医学图像处理效率，减少计算负担。



摘要： 本文提出MedPruner，一种无需训练且适用于多种模型的分层令牌剪枝框架，用于提升3D医学图像理解的效率。该框架通过两阶段机制：首先利用基于切片锚点的过滤模块消除时间冗余，再通过动态信息核心选择策略实现自适应令牌压缩。实验表明，MedPruner在多个3D医学基准和模型上显著减少冗余令牌，使MedGemma等模型在保留少于5%视觉令牌的情况下仍保持或超越原性能，大幅降低计算开销，验证了动态令牌选择在临床部署中的必要性。

item.title: JAX中自缩放布罗伊登族拟牛顿方法的实现
item.content:encoded:
提供JAX兼容的自缩放布罗伊登族优化器实现。



摘要： 本文介绍了在JAX框架下实现的自缩放布罗伊登族拟牛顿方法，包括BFGS、DFP、Broyden及其自缩放变体（SSBFGS、SSDFP、SSBroyden），并集成满足强 Wolfe 条件的 Zoom 线搜索。该技术笔记旨在记录实现细节，便于JAX社区采用这些优化器，但不声称任何新贡献。代码已开源。

item.title: 大型语言模型在安全补丁生成中的有效性分析
item.content:encoded:
LLM生成的安全补丁有效性低，需严格验证。



摘要： 本研究分析了319个由大型语言模型生成的Java安全补丁，涉及Vul4J基准中的64个漏洞。通过编译性、安全性（通过PoV测试）和功能性的三轴评估发现，仅有24.8%的补丁完全正确，51.4%同时失败于安全性和功能性。主要失败原因是语义理解错误，即生成的代码语法正确但修复策略错误。研究提出的SRS评分显示，LLMs在保持功能方面表现较好（平均0.832），但在安全性方面较差（平均0.251）。不同类型的漏洞修复难度差异显著，输入验证漏洞修复率为0%，而无限循环漏洞修复率最高为45%。研究强调，LLM生成的安全补丁在部署前需经过严格验证。

item.title: 基于方差感知的扩散模型训练优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过方差感知自适应加权提升扩散模型训练稳定性与生成性能。



摘要： 本文研究了扩散模型在不同噪声水平下的训练动态不平衡问题，从损失方差角度分析并提出一种基于方差感知的自适应加权策略。该方法根据方差分布动态调整训练权重，实现噪声水平间的平衡优化。实验表明，该方法在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上显著提升了生成性能，降低了FID分数，并减少了随机种子间的性能波动。通过损失-对数SNR可视化、方差热图和消融实验进一步验证了该方法的有效性，证明了方差感知训练策略在改进扩散模型优化中的潜力。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的文本到点云定位方法
item.content:encoded:
提出VLM-Loc框架提升文本到点云定位精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于视觉语言模型（VLM）的文本到点云定位方法VLM-Loc，通过将点云转换为鸟瞰图和场景图，结合几何与语义信息，增强空间推理能力。引入部分节点分配机制，实现文本与场景图节点的显式关联，提高定位准确性。构建CityLoc基准数据集用于评估，实验表明VLM-Loc在复杂环境中表现优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的Rb-82动态心脏PET去噪方法DECADE
item.content:encoded:
DECADE提升Rb-82 PET图像质量，无需配对数据。



摘要： 本文提出DECADE，一种用于Rb-82动态心脏PET去噪的无监督扩散框架。该方法通过时间一致性设计，在不依赖配对清洁与噪声数据的情况下，有效降低噪声并保持心肌血流和储备的定量准确性。在Siemens Vision 450和Biograph Vision Quadra设备的数据集上验证，DECADE在动态和参数图像质量以及K1/MBF量化方面优于现有方法，为临床诊断提供更清晰、可靠的影像支持。

item.title: 向量球张量乘积的积分公式与应用
item.content:encoded:
简化向量球张量乘积，提升SO(3)等变神经网络效率。



摘要： 本文推导了Xie等人提出的向量球张量乘积的积分公式，该乘积将Gaunt张量乘积推广至反对称耦合。我们获得了反对称Gaunt系数的显式闭式表达式，从而可以用单个向量球张量乘积模拟Clebsch-Gordan张量乘积，使所需张量乘积计算量减少9倍。研究还探讨了Gaunt和向量球张量乘积在控制表达性与运行时间权衡中的作用，并分析了其归一化低秩分解，为等变神经网络的应用提供了高效实现路径。

item.title: 基于自由文本的结构化放射报告生成方法 ProtoSR
item.content:encoded:
ProtoSR 利用自由文本提升结构化报告准确性。



摘要： 文章提出 ProtoSR 方法，通过从自由文本报告中提取信息，增强结构化报告的生成效果。该方法利用大规模数据构建多模态知识库，并通过视觉原型增强模型预测，显著提升了对细节属性的识别能力。在 Rad-ReStruct 基准测试中，ProtoSR 表现出色，尤其在细粒度问题上效果最佳。

item.title: 基于结构化解释的深度学习模型可解释性框架I2X
item.content:encoded:
I2X框架通过结构化解释提升模型透明度与预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为I2X的深度学习模型可解释性框架，旨在解决现有方法依赖辅助模型导致的不忠实问题。I2X通过从后验XAI方法中提取原型，并在训练过程中量化关键检查点的进展，构建结构化的解释。该框架不仅揭示了图像分类模型的原型推理过程，还能通过识别不确定原型并进行样本扰动优化，提升不同模型架构和数据集上的预测准确率。I2X既忠实解释模型行为，又为模型优化提供了实用路径。

item.title: CLIPO：提升大语言模型推理能力的对比学习方法
item.content:encoded:
CLIPO通过对比学习提升LLM推理鲁棒性与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出CLIPO方法，通过引入对比学习机制改进基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR），以解决仅依赖最终答案导致的推理步骤错误问题。CLIPO通过优化成功轨迹上的对比损失，使模型捕捉正确推理路径中的不变结构，从而增强跨轨迹的正则化效果，有效减少推理不一致和幻觉现象。实验表明，CLIPO在多个推理基准上均优于现有RLVR基线，显著提升了LLM的泛化能力和鲁棒性。

item.title: InSpatio-WorldFM：一种实时空间智能的开源帧模型
item.content:encoded:
InSpatio-WorldFM实现低延迟实时空间推理。



摘要： InSpatio-WorldFM是一种开源的实时帧模型，用于空间智能。与依赖顺序帧生成的视频世界模型不同，它采用帧级独立生成方式，减少延迟，提高实时性。通过显式3D锚点和隐式空间记忆确保多视角一致性，保持全局场景几何和细节。研究还提出一个渐进式三阶段训练流程，将预训练图像扩散模型转化为可控帧模型，并最终成为实时生成器。实验表明，该模型在消费级GPU上实现强多视角一致性和交互探索，为实时世界模拟提供高效替代方案。

item.title: 基于随机平滑的轨迹预测模型防御机制研究
item.content:encoded:
随机平滑提升轨迹预测模型抗攻击能力。



摘要： 本文研究了轨迹预测模型在对抗攻击下的脆弱性，并提出一种基于随机平滑的防御机制。该方法在多个数据集上进行了测试，结果表明其能够在不牺牲正常场景下预测精度的前提下，有效提升模型的鲁棒性。研究展示了随机平滑作为一种简单且计算成本低的技术，在缓解轨迹预测中的对抗攻击方面具有良好的应用前景。

item.title: 基于数据驱动的激光点火火箭发动机仿真方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种数据驱动的代理模型，提升火箭发动机仿真效率。



摘要： 文章提出一种结合卷积自编码器和神经微分方程的数据驱动代理建模方法，用于高效模拟激光点火火箭发动机的复杂物理过程。该方法通过压缩高维流场数据到低维潜在空间，并利用神经微分方程学习系统时间演化，从而大幅降低仿真计算成本，实现对输入参数空间的高效探索与不确定性量化。该方法为实时数字孪生技术提供了新路径，并推动了代理模型在复杂系统中的应用。

item.title: 4DEquine: Disentangling Motion and Appearance for 4D Equine Reconstruction from Monocular Video
item.content:encoded: 4D reconstruction of equine family (e.g. horses) from monocular video is important for animal welfare. Previous mainstream 4D animal reconstruction methods require joint optimization of motion and appearance over a whole video, which is time-consuming and sensitive to incomplete observation. In this work, we propose a novel framework called 4DEquine by disentangling the 4D reconstruction problem into two sub-problems: dynamic motion reconstruction and static appearance reconstruction. For motion, we introduce a simple yet effective spatio-temporal transformer with a post-optimization stage to regress smooth and pixel-aligned pose and shape sequences from video. For appearance, we design a novel feed-forward network that reconstructs a high-fidelity, animatable 3D Gaussian avatar from as few as a single image. To assist training, we create a large-scale synthetic motion dataset, VarenPoser, which features high-quality surface motions and diverse camera trajectories, as well as a synthetic appearance dataset, VarenTex, comprising realistic multi-view images generated through multi-view diffusion. While training only on synthetic datasets, 4DEquine achieves state-of-the-art performance on real-world APT36K and AiM datasets, demonstrating the superiority of 4DEquine and our new datasets for both geometry and appearance reconstruction. Comprehensive ablation studies validate the effectiveness of both the motion and appearance reconstruction network. Project page: https://luoxue-star.github.io/4DEquine_Project_Page/.

item.title: Reinforcement learning-based dynamic cleaning scheduling framework for solar energy system
item.content:encoded: Advancing autonomous green technologies in solar photovoltaic (PV) systems is key to improving sustainability and efficiency in renewable energy production. This study presents a reinforcement learning (RL)-based framework to autonomously optimize the cleaning schedules of PV panels in arid regions, where soiling from dust and other airborne particles significantly reduces energy output. By employing advanced RL algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Soft Actor-Critic (SAC), the framework dynamically adjusts cleaning intervals based on uncertain environmental conditions. The proposed approach was applied to a case study in Abu Dhabi, UAE, demonstrating that PPO outperformed SAC and traditional simulation optimization (Sim-Opt) methods, achieving up to 13% cost savings by dynamically responding to weather uncertainties. The results highlight the superiority of flexible, autonomous scheduling over fixed-interval methods, particularly in adapting to stochastic environmental dynamics. This aligns with the goals of autonomous green energy production by reducing operational costs and improving the efficiency of solar power generation systems. This work underscores the potential of RL-driven autonomous decision-making to optimize maintenance operations in renewable energy systems. In future research, it is important to enhance the generalization ability of the proposed RL model, while also considering additional factors and constraints to apply it to different regions.

item.title: "Better Ask for Forgiveness than Permission": Practices and Policies of AI Disclosure in Freelance Work
item.content:encoded: The growing use of AI applications among freelance workers is reshaping trust and relationships with clients. This paper investigates how both workers and clients perceive AI use and disclosure in the freelance economy through a three-stage study: interviews with workers and two survey studies with workers and clients. Findings first reveal a key expectation gap around disclosure: Workers often adopt passive disclosure practices, revealing AI use only when asked, as they assume clients can already detect it. Clients, however, are far less confident in recognizing AI-assisted work and prefer proactive disclosure. A second finding highlights the role of unclear or absent client AI policies, which leave workers consistently misinterpreting clients' expectations for AI use and disclosure. Together, these gaps point to the need for clearer guidelines and practices for AI disclosure. Insights extend beyond freelancing, offering implications for trust, accountability, and policy design in other AI-mediated work domains.

item.title: From Word2Vec to Transformers: Text-Derived Composition Embeddings for Filtering Combinatorial Electrocatalysts
item.content:encoded: Compositionally complex solid solution electrocatalysts span vast composition spaces, and even one materials system can contain more candidate compositions than can be measured exhaustively. Here we evaluate a label-free screening strategy that represents each composition using embeddings derived from scientific texts and prioritizes candidates based on similarity to two property concepts. We compare a corpus-trained Word2Vec baseline with transformer-based embeddings, where compositions are encoded either by linear element-wise mixing or by short composition prompts. Similarities to `concept directions', the terms conductivity and dielectric, define a 2-dimensional descriptor space, and a symmetric Pareto-front selection is used to filter candidate subsets without using electrochemical labels. Performance is assessed on 15 materials libraries including noble metal alloys and multicomponent oxides. In this setting, the lightweight Word2Vec baseline, which uses a simple linear combination of element embeddings, often achieves the highest number of reductions of possible candidate compositions while staying close to the best measured performance.

item.title: Bio-Inspired Self-Supervised Learning for Wrist-worn IMU Signals
item.content:encoded: Wearable accelerometers have enabled large-scale health and wellness monitoring, yet learning robust human-activity representations has been constrained by the scarcity of labeled data. While self-supervised learning offers a potential remedy, existing approaches treat sensor streams as unstructured time series, overlooking the underlying biological structure of human movement, a factor we argue is critical for effective Human Activity Recognition (HAR). We introduce a novel tokenization strategy grounded in the submovement theory of motor control, which posits that continuous wrist motion is composed of superposed elementary basis functions called submovements. We define our token as the movement segment, a unit of motion composed of a finite sequence of submovements that is readily extractable from wrist accelerometer signals. By treating these segments as tokens, we pretrain a Transformer encoder via masked movement-segment reconstruction to model the temporal dependencies of movement segments, shifting the learning focus beyond local waveform morphology. Pretrained on the NHANES corpus (approximately 28k hours; approximately 11k participants; approximately 10M windows), our representations outperform strong wearable SSL baselines across six subject-disjoint HAR benchmarks. Furthermore, they demonstrate stronger data efficiency in data-scarce settings. Code and pretrained weights will be made publicly available.

item.title: Prompt-Based Caption Generation for Single-Tooth Dental Images Using Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Digital dentistry has made significant advances with the advent of deep learning. However, the majority of these deep learning-based dental image analysis models focus on very specific tasks such as tooth segmentation, tooth detection, cavity detection, and gingivitis classification. There is a lack of a specialized model that has holistic knowledge of teeth and can perform dental image analysis tasks based on that knowledge. Datasets of dental images with captions can help build such a model. To the best of our knowledge, existing dental image datasets with captions are few in number and limited in scope. In many of these datasets, the captions describe the entire mouth, while the images are limited to the anterior view. As a result, posterior teeth such as molars are not clearly visible, limiting the usefulness of the captions for training vision-language models. Additionally, the captions focus only on a specific disease (gingivitis) and do not provide a holistic assessment of each tooth. Moreover, tooth disease scores are typically assigned to individual teeth, and each tooth is treated as a separate entity in orthodontic procedures. Therefore, it is important to have captions for single-tooth images. As far as we know, no such dataset of single-tooth images with dental captions exists. In this work, we aim to bridge that gap by assessing the possibility of generating captions for dental images using Vision-Language Models (VLMs) and evaluating the extent and quality of those captions. Our findings suggest that guided prompts help VLMs generate meaningful captions. We show that the prompts generated by our framework are better anchored in describing the visual aspects of dental images. We selected RGB images as they have greater potential in consumer scenarios.

item.title: PONTE: Personalized Orchestration for Natural Language Trustworthy Explanations
item.content:encoded: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) seeks to enhance the transparency and accountability of machine learning systems, yet most methods follow a one-size-fits-all paradigm that neglects user differences in expertise, goals, and cognitive needs. Although Large Language Models can translate technical explanations into natural language, they introduce challenges related to faithfulness and hallucinations. To address these challenges, we present PONTE (Personalized Orchestration for Natural language Trustworthy Explanations), a human-in-the-loop framework for adaptive and reliable XAI narratives. PONTE models personalization as a closed-loop validation and adaptation process rather than prompt engineering. It combines: (i) a low-dimensional preference model capturing stylistic requirements; (ii) a preference-conditioned generator grounded in structured XAI artifacts; and (iii) verification modules enforcing numerical faithfulness, informational completeness, and stylistic alignment, optionally supported by retrieval-grounded argumentation. User feedback iteratively updates the preference state, enabling quick personalization. Automatic and human evaluations across healthcare and finance domains show that the verification-refinement loop substantially improves completeness and stylistic alignment over validation-free generation. Human studies further confirm strong agreement between intended preference vectors and perceived style, robustness to generation stochasticity, and consistently positive quality assessments.

item.title: Separable neural architectures as a primitive for unified predictive and generative intelligence
item.content:encoded: Intelligent systems across physics, language and perception often exhibit factorisable structure, yet are typically modelled by monolithic neural architectures that do not explicitly exploit this structure. The separable neural architecture (SNA) addresses this by formalising a representational class that unifies additive, quadratic and tensor-decomposed neural models. By constraining interaction order and tensor rank, SNAs impose a structural inductive bias that factorises high-dimensional mappings into low-arity components. Separability need not be a property of the system itself: it often emerges in the coordinates or representations through which the system is expressed. Crucially, this coordinate-aware formulation reveals a structural analogy between chaotic spatiotemporal dynamics and linguistic autoregression. By treating continuous physical states as smooth, separable embeddings, SNAs enable distributional modelling of chaotic systems. This approach mitigates the nonphysical drift characteristics of deterministic operators whilst remaining applicable to discrete sequences. The compositional versatility of this approach is demonstrated across four domains: autonomous waypoint navigation via reinforcement learning, inverse generation of multifunctional microstructures, distributional modelling of turbulent flow and neural language modelling. These results establish the separable neural architecture as a domain-agnostic primitive for predictive and generative intelligence, capable of unifying both deterministic and distributional representations.

item.title: $Δ$VLA: Prior-Guided Vision-Language-Action Models via World Knowledge Variation
item.content:encoded: Recent vision-language-action (VLA) models have significantly advanced robotic manipulation by unifying perception, reasoning, and control. To achieve such integration, recent studies adopt a predictive paradigm that models future visual states or world knowledge to guide action generation. However, these models emphasize forecasting outcomes rather than reasoning about the underlying process of change, which is essential for determining how to act. To address this, we propose $Δ$VLA, a prior-guided framework that models world-knowledge variations relative to an explicit current-world knowledge prior for action generation, rather than regressing absolute future world states. Specifically, 1) to construct the current world knowledge prior, we propose the Prior-Guided WorldKnowledge Extractor (PWKE). It extracts manipulable regions, spatial relations, and semantic cues from the visual input, guided by auxiliary heads and prior pseudo labels, thus reducing redundancy. 2) Building upon this, to represent how world knowledge evolves under actions, we introduce the Latent World Variation Quantization (LWVQ). It learns a discrete latent space via a VQ-VAE objective to encode world knowledge variations, shifting prediction from full modalities to compact latent. 3)Moreover, to mitigate interference during variation modeling, we design the Conditional Variation Attention (CV-Atten), whichpromotes disentangled learning and preserves the independence of knowledge representations. Extensive experiments on both simulated benchmarks and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate $Δ$VLA achieves state-of-the-art performance while improving efficiency. Code and real-world execution videos are available at https://github.com/JiuTian-VL/DeltaVLA.

item.title: InFusionLayer: a CFA-based ensemble tool to generate new classifiers for learning and modeling
item.content:encoded: Ensemble learning is a well established body of methods for machine learning to enhance predictive performance by combining multiple algorithms/models. Combinatorial Fusion Analysis (CFA) has provided method and practice for combining multiple scoring systems, using rank-score characteristic (RSC) function and cognitive diversity (CD), including ensemble method and model fusion. However, there is no general-purpose Python tool available that incorporate these techniques. In this paper we introduce \texttt{InFusionLayer}, a machine learning architecture inspired by CFA at the system fusion level that uses a moderate set of base models to optimize unsupervised and supervised learning multiclassification problems. We demonstrate \texttt{InFusionLayer}'s ease of use for PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn workflows by validating its performance on various computer vision datasets. Our results highlight the practical advantages of incorporating distinctive features of RSC function and CD, paving the way for more sophisticated ensemble learning applications in machine learning. We open-sourced our code to encourage continuing development and community accessibility to leverage CFA on github: https://github.com/ewroginek/Infusion

item.title: A Bipartite Graph Approach to U.S.-China Cross-Market Return Forecasting
item.content:encoded: This paper studies cross-market return predictability through a machine learning framework that preserves economic structure. Exploiting the non-overlapping trading hours of the U.S. and Chinese equity markets, we construct a directed bipartite graph that captures time-ordered predictive linkages between stocks across markets. Edges are selected via rolling-window hypothesis testing, and the resulting graph serves as a sparse, economically interpretable feature-selection layer for downstream machine learning models. We apply a range of regularized and ensemble methods to forecast open-to-close returns using lagged foreign-market information. Our results reveal a pronounced directional asymmetry: U.S. previous-close-to-close returns contain substantial predictive information for Chinese intraday returns, whereas the reverse effect is limited. This informational asymmetry translates into economically meaningful performance differences and highlights how structured machine learning frameworks can uncover cross-market dependencies while maintaining interpretability.

item.title: Cognitively Layered Data Synthesis for Domain Adaptation of LLMs to Space Situational Awareness
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate exceptional performance on general-purpose tasks. however, transferring them to complex engineering domains such as space situational awareness (SSA) remains challenging owing to insufficient structural alignment with mission chains, the absence of higher-order cognitive supervision, and poor correspondence between data quality criteria and engineering specifications. The core bottleneck is the construction of high-quality supervised fine-tuning (SFT) datasets. To this end, we propose BD-FDG (Bloom's Taxonomy-based Domain-specific Fine-tuning Data Generation), a framework that addresses incomplete knowledge coverage, shallow cognitive depth, and limited quality controllability through three mechanisms: structured knowledge organization, cognitively layered question modeling, and automated quality control. The framework uses a knowledge tree to ensure structured corpus coverage, designs a question generation scheme spanning nine categories and six cognitive levels from Remember to Create to produce samples with a continuous difficulty gradient, and applies a multidimensional scoring pipeline to enforce domain rigor and consistency. Using BD-FDG, we construct SSA-SFT, a domain dataset of approximately 230K samples, and fine-tune Qwen3-8B to obtain SSA-LLM-8B. Experiments show that SSA-LLM-8B achieves relative BLEU-1 improvements of 144\% (no-think) and 176\% (think) on the domain test set and a win rate of 82.21\% over the baseline in arena comparisons, while largely preserving general benchmark performance (MMLU-Pro, MATH-500). These results validate SFT data construction driven by cognitive layering as an effective paradigm for complex engineering domains and provide a transferable framework for domain-specific LLM adaptation.

item.title: NN-OpInf: an operator inference approach using structure-preserving composable neural networks
item.content:encoded: We propose neural network operator inference (NN-OpInf): a structure-preserving, composable, and minimally restrictive operator inference framework for the non-intrusive reduced-order modeling of dynamical systems. The approach learns latent dynamics from snapshot data, enforcing local operator structure such as skew-symmetry, (semi-)positive definiteness, and gradient preservation, while also reflecting complex dynamics by supporting additive compositions of heterogeneous operators. We present practical training strategies and analyze computational costs relative to linear and quadratic polynomial OpInf (P-OpInf). Numerical experiments across several nonlinear and parametric problems demonstrate improved accuracy, stability, and robustness over P-OpInf and prior NN-ROM formulations, particularly when the dynamics are not well represented by polynomial models. These results suggest that NN-OpInf can serve as an effective drop-in replacement for P-OpInf when the dynamics to be modeled contain non-polynomial nonlinearities, offering potential gains in accuracy and out-of-distribution performance at the expense of higher training computational costs and a more difficult, non-convex learning problem.

item.title: Characterization and upgrade of a quantum graph neural network for charged particle tracking
item.content:encoded: In the forthcoming years the LHC experiments are going to be upgraded to benefit from the substantial increase of the LHC instantaneous luminosity, which will lead to larger, denser events, and, consequently, greater complexity in reconstructing charged particle tracks, motivating frontier research in new technologies. Quantum machine learning models are being investigated as potential new approaches to high energy physics (HEP) tasks. We characterize and upgrade a quantum graph neural network (QGNN) architecture for charged particle track reconstruction on a simulated high luminosity dataset. The model operates on a set of event graphs, each built from the hits generated in tracking detector layers by particles produced in proton collisions, performing a classification of the possible hit connections between adjacent layers. In this approach the QGNN is designed as a hybrid architecture, interleaving classical feedforward networks with parametrized quantum circuits. We characterize the interplay between the classical and quantum components. We report on the principal upgrades to the original design, and present new evidence of improved training behavior, specifically in terms of convergence toward the final trained configuration.

item.title: 基于多维情感信号的原油价格预测研究
item.content:encoded:
多维情感信号提升原油期货收益预测准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了利用大语言模型提取的多维情感信号是否能提高周度WTI原油期货收益的预测能力。通过2020年至2025年的能源行业新闻文章，构建了涵盖相关性、极性、强度、不确定性和前瞻性五个维度的情感指标，并结合GPT-4o、Llama 3.2-3b、FinBERT和AlphaVantage等模型进行分析。结果表明，将GPT-4o与FinBERT结合可获得最佳预测效果，说明大语言模型与传统金融情感模型具有互补性。SHAP分析进一步显示，强度和不确定性特征是关键预测因子，表明新闻情感的预测价值超越了简单极性分析。研究结果表明，多维情感测量有助于提升商品收益预测和能源市场风险监控。

item.title: 基于注意力机制的后门样本检测方法BackdoorIDS
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无需训练的后门样本检测方法BackdoorIDS。



摘要： 本文提出BackdoorIDS，一种无需训练、在推理阶段即可检测预训练视觉编码器中后门样本的方法。该方法基于注意力劫持和恢复现象，在逐步输入掩码过程中，后门图像会先关注恶意触发特征，随后注意力转移至正常内容，导致嵌入向量发生显著变化。通过密度聚类分析嵌入序列，若形成多个簇则判定为后门样本。实验表明，BackdoorIDS在多种攻击类型、数据集和模型架构中均表现优异，且无需重新训练，具有良好的兼容性。

item.title: 基于Transformer的DeepHistoViT在组织病理学图像分类中的应用
item.content:encoded:
DeepHistoViT在癌症组织病理学图像分类中表现优异。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的DeepHistoViT框架，用于自动化分类组织病理学图像。该模型通过集成注意力机制，能够捕捉细粒度的细胞结构，并提高诊断相关区域的可解释性。在三个公开数据集（肺癌、结肠癌和急性淋巴细胞白血病）上进行了评估，结果显示其分类准确率、精确率、召回率、F1分数和ROC-AUC均达到或接近100%。这些结果表明，Transformer架构在组织病理学图像分析中具有显著优势，DeepHistoViT有望作为辅助病理诊断工具，提升临床决策效率。

item.title: 新型特征提取库Nyxus提升大规模图像数据处理效率
item.content:encoded:
Nyxus提升图像数据分析效率与可扩展性。



摘要： 现代成像设备产生的海量图像数据对计算能力提出了更高要求。传统图像分析算法在处理大规模数据时效率不足，且难以在准确性和鲁棒性之间取得平衡。深度学习虽提升了区域分割的准确性，但各领域特征提取工具的多样化使性能比较变得复杂。为此，研究者开发了名为Nyxus的新特征提取库，该库专为2D和3D图像数据设计，支持跨CPU和GPU的高效计算，并覆盖多个生物医学领域。Nyxus提供了多种使用方式，包括Python包、命令行工具、Napari插件及OCI容器，适用于不同技能水平的用户。此外，Nyxus还支持程序化调整特征集，以优化计算效率或覆盖范围，适用于新型机器学习和深度学习应用。

item.title: 基于Transformer的多变量时间序列概率预测框架EnTransformer
item.content:encoded:
EnTransformer通过引入随机噪声和能量评分目标，提升多变量时间序列预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出EnTransformer，一种结合生成模型与Transformer架构的多变量时间序列概率预测框架。该方法通过注入随机噪声并优化能量评分目标，直接学习条件预测分布，无需参数假设。EnTransformer在多个基准数据集上表现优异，能够生成一致的多变量预测轨迹，并有效捕捉长时序依赖和跨序列交互。实验结果表明，该模型在概率预测校准性和准确性方面均优于现有基准模型。

item.title: ELIT：一种可动态调整计算资源的扩散Transformer机制
item.content:encoded:
ELIT提升生成质量并优化计算资源分配。



摘要： 本文提出ELIT，一种与扩散Transformer（DiT）兼容的机制，通过引入可学习的潜在接口序列，实现输入图像大小与计算量的解耦。ELIT利用轻量级读写交叉注意力层，将信息在空间标记和潜在标记之间传递，并优先处理重要区域。训练中通过随机丢弃尾部潜在标记，使模型生成按重要性排序的表示。推理时可根据计算约束动态调整潜在标记数量，从而提升生成质量并优化资源分配。实验显示，ELIT在多个数据集和架构上均取得显著提升。

item.title: 多模态基准数据集CoMMET评估大语言模型的心智理论能力
item.content:encoded:
提出多模态基准CoMMET评估LLM的心智理论能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种新的多模态基准数据集CoMMET，用于评估大型语言模型（LLMs）在心智理论（ToM）方面的能力。该数据集扩展了传统评测范围，涵盖更广泛的心理状态，并引入多轮对话测试，是首个在多轮对话场景中评估ToM的多模态数据集。通过全面评估不同家族和规模的LLMs，研究分析了现有模型的优势与不足，并指出了未来改进方向，有助于深入理解现代LLMs的社会认知能力。

item.title: 基于风险可控的多视角驾驶场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出RiskMV-DPO方法，实现高风险驾驶场景的自动生成。



摘要： 本文提出RiskMV-DPO，一种物理驱动、风险可控的多视角驾驶场景生成框架。该方法通过整合目标风险等级与物理风险建模，自动生成多样化且高风险的动态轨迹，作为扩散视频生成的几何锚点。为保证时空一致性和几何真实性，引入了几何-外观对齐模块和区域感知直接偏好优化策略。实验表明，RiskMV-DPO在nuScenes数据集上显著提升了3D检测性能并改善了视觉质量，为具身智能的安全开发提供了可扩展工具链。

item.title: 基于神经网络的高维非线性结构系统不确定性建模研究
item.content:encoded:
提出三种元模型框架用于处理结构系统中的不确定性。



摘要： 本文针对高维非线性动态结构系统在自然灾害下的不确定性建模问题，提出了三种元模型框架，分别结合多层感知机（MLP）、消息传递神经网络（MPNN）和自编码器（AE）与长短期记忆网络（LSTM）进行结构响应预测。这些框架通过蒙特卡洛dropout和负对数似然损失函数来处理不确定性，并在两个案例研究中验证了其有效性。结果表明，MLP-LSTM在低维系统中表现最佳，而MPNN-LSTM和AE-LSTM在更复杂的钢框架模型中更具优势。同时，预测方差与实际误差之间的一致性表明这些框架适用于主动学习策略和模型置信度评估。

item.title: AutoGaze：提升长时高分辨率视频理解的轻量级模块
item.content:encoded:
AutoGaze通过减少冗余图像块提升视频处理效率和效果。



摘要： 本文提出AutoGaze，一种用于多模态大语言模型的轻量级模块，旨在解决长时高分辨率视频处理中的冗余问题。AutoGaze通过自回归方式选择最少的多尺度图像块，在保证信息完整性的前提下，将视觉标记减少4倍至100倍，使视频处理速度提升最多19倍。该方法在VideoMME等视频基准测试中取得优异成绩，并引入了首个高分辨率、长视频问答基准HLVid，验证了其有效性。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的可组合内在概念提取方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CI-ICE任务与HyperExpress方法，实现图像中可组合概念的准确提取。



摘要： 本文针对无监督概念提取中存在的无法提取可组合内在概念的问题，提出了一个新的任务——可组合且可解释的内在概念提取（CI-ICE）。该任务利用基于扩散的文本到图像模型，从单张图像中提取对象级和属性级的概念，并通过这些概念的组合重建原始概念。为实现这一目标，作者提出了一种名为HyperExpress的方法，该方法通过两个核心方面解决CI-ICE任务：一是利用双曲空间的层次建模能力进行概念解耦，二是引入概念级优化方法以保持复杂的概念间关系并确保概念的可组合性。实验表明，该方法在提取可组合的内在概念方面表现优异。

item.title: 日本病理报告写作中大语言模型的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
评估七种开源大语言模型在病理报告写作中的表现。



摘要： 本文评估了七种开源大语言模型在支持日语病理报告写作中的性能，从三个角度进行分析：（A）按照预定义格式生成和提取病理诊断文本，（B）修正日语病理报告中的拼写错误，（C）由病理科医生和临床医生对模型生成的解释性文本进行主观评价。结果显示，思维型模型和医学专业模型在需要推理的结构化报告任务和拼写修正中表现出优势，但解释性文本的偏好因评估者而异。尽管不同任务中模型的实用性有所差异，研究结果表明开源大语言模型在有限但具有临床意义的场景中可辅助日语病理报告写作。

item.title: FLUX.1中潜在空间颜色表示的解释与控制方法
item.content:encoded:
FLUX.1潜在空间颜色结构可被解释并用于颜色控制。



摘要： 本文研究了FLUX.1变分自编码器潜在空间中的颜色表示，揭示了其反映色相、饱和度和明度的结构。通过验证，该潜在颜色子空间（LCS）能够预测并直接控制图像颜色，提出了一种无需训练的完全基于封闭形式潜在空间操作的方法。该方法为图像生成中的颜色控制提供了新思路，相关代码已公开。

item.title: 基于数字孪生的水下自主对接深度强化学习方法
item.content:encoded:
深度强化学习提升水下自主对接成功率与适应性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于高保真数字孪生环境的自主水下对接方法，利用Stonefish模拟器构建多进程强化学习框架，加速训练过程并引入真实AUV动力学、碰撞模型和传感器噪声。采用PPO算法训练六自由度控制策略，通过距离、姿态、动作平滑性和自适应碰撞惩罚构建奖励机制。实验表明，在仿真中成功率达到90%以上，并在物理测试中验证了从仿真到现实的迁移有效性，DRL控制器展现出如俯仰制动和偏航振荡等自适应行为。

item.title: 生成式AI技术与社会系统的协同演进研究
item.content:encoded:
生成式AI推动创新，但面临技术、伦理和治理挑战。



摘要： 本文通过系统文献综述分析了2023年以来的28篇相关研究，指出生成式AI虽具有提升生产力和创新的潜力，但其应用受到技术不可靠性、社会伦理风险及治理空白等多重因素的制约。研究强调技术子系统与社会子系统之间的不匹配问题，并提出IS（信息科学）研究应从分析影响转向主动塑造技术与组织流程、社会价值观及监管制度的共同演进，关注人机协作、情境验证、概率系统设计原则及适应性治理。

item.title: 儿童手写动态与学习特征的关系研究
item.content:encoded:
手写动态可反映儿童发展和个体差异，尤其在年级预测中表现显著。



摘要： 本文通过分析日本小学生至初中生的大规模在线手写数据，探讨手写动态是否能编码学生特征。研究比较了三种手写特征：基本统计描述符、基于熵的变异性度量以及sigma-lognormal模型参数。这些特征被用于年级预测、性别分类和学业表现分类任务，并采用线性回归、逻辑回归和随机森林模型进行评估。结果表明，手写动态包含与发育阶段和个体差异相关的信息，尤其是在年级预测任务中表现突出。研究证实了手写运动模式随儿童成长逐渐趋于对数正态分布的组织方式。

item.title: 轻量级实时边缘分割模型PicoSAM3在传感器上的应用
item.content:encoded:
PicoSAM3实现高精度实时边缘分割，适用于智能设备。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为PicoSAM3的轻量级可提示视觉分割模型，专为边缘计算和传感器端部署设计。该模型仅包含130万参数，结合密集CNN架构、区域提示编码、高效通道注意力机制，并通过SAM2和SAM3进行知识蒸馏，显著提升了分割性能。在COCO和LVIS数据集上分别达到65.45%和64.01%的mIoU，优于现有同类模型。量化后的INT8版本在Sony IMX500传感器上实现11.82毫秒的实时推理，满足内存和算子限制。实验表明，从大模型蒸馏可提升14.5%的mIoU，证明了在传感器端实现高质量、灵活提示分割的可行性。

item.title: 基于自回归扩散模型的实时人类动画生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Neighbor Forcing和ConvKV机制提升实时动画生成效率与质量。



摘要： 本文针对自回归扩散模型在实时人类动画生成中的效率问题，提出了Neighbor Forcing方法，通过传播时间相邻帧作为潜在邻居，实现更稳定的学习信号。同时引入结构化ConvKV记忆机制，压缩注意力键值对，实现常数内存推理和无限视频生成。实验表明该方法在训练收敛性、生成质量和推理效率方面均优于现有方法，支持20 FPS实时流式推理。

item.title: 基于流匹配的虚拟试穿框架PROMO提升图像编辑效率与质量
item.content:encoded:
PROMO通过流匹配技术实现高效高质的虚拟试穿，提升图像编辑性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PROMO的虚拟试穿框架，采用流匹配DiT架构，并结合潜在多模态条件拼接和自参考机制，有效降低推理开销。该方法在标准基准测试中表现出色，在保持高质量的同时提升了速度，证明了流匹配变压器在虚拟试穿任务中的有效性。PROMO不仅适用于虚拟试穿，还可扩展到更广泛的图像编辑任务，具有良好的通用性和训练效率。

item.title: OmniForcing：实现高效多模态流式生成的扩散模型蒸馏框架
item.content:encoded:
OmniForcing解决多模态扩散模型延迟问题，提升实时生成效率。



摘要： 本文提出OmniForcing，一种将离线双流双向扩散模型蒸馏为高质量流式自回归生成器的框架。针对传统方法在因果蒸馏中因模态时间不对称和令牌稀疏性导致的训练不稳定问题，引入了非对称块因果对齐与零截断全局前缀，以防止多模态同步漂移。同时通过音频Sink Token机制和Identity RoPE约束解决音频令牌稀疏性引发的梯度爆炸问题。最后采用联合自强制蒸馏策略，使模型在长序列生成中动态修正跨模态误差。OmniForcing在单GPU上实现约25 FPS的流式生成，保持与双向教师模型相当的多模态同步性和视觉质量。

item.title: 基于因果自编码与治疗条件的反事实估计方法
item.content:encoded:
CAETC方法提升反事实估计效果，适用于多种时间序列模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为因果自编码与治疗条件（CAETC）的新方法，用于解决观测数据中时间依赖性混杂偏差的问题。该方法基于对抗性表示学习，利用自编码架构学习部分可逆且治疗不变的表示，并通过治疗特定条件进行结果预测。该设计不依赖于底层序列模型，可应用于LSTM或TCN等现有架构。实验表明，CAETC在合成、半合成和真实数据上均优于现有方法，显著提升了反事实估计的准确性。

item.title: Video Streaming Thinking：一种实时视频理解的新范式
item.content:encoded:
提出VST范式，实现视频流中同步推理，提升响应效率与理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出Video Streaming Thinking（VST），一种支持边观看边思考的视频理解新范式。该方法在视频流中激活推理机制，通过摊销大语言模型的推理延迟，实现了及时理解和连贯认知，同时保持实时响应。研究还引入了VST-SFT和VST-RL后训练流程，结合视频知识图谱生成高质量的问答对，增强多证据推理和持续注意力。实验表明，VST-7B在多个在线基准测试中表现优异，相比现有方法响应速度提升15.7倍，且在多种视频理解任务中展现出强大泛化能力。

item.title: 基于有效电阻的图神经网络拓扑重连方法
item.content:encoded:
有效电阻重连提升图神经网络长程依赖建模能力。



摘要： 本文针对图神经网络在捕捉长程依赖时面临的过压缩问题，提出一种基于有效电阻的拓扑重连策略（ERR）。该方法通过全局有效电阻检测结构瓶颈，迭代添加高电阻节点对之间的边，同时移除低电阻边，以增强通信路径并控制图密度。实验表明，ERR在保持边数不变的情况下提升了信息传播效率，并与PairNorm等归一化技术结合进一步优化性能。研究还分析了重连对消息传递和嵌入表示演化的影响，揭示了过压缩与过平滑之间的权衡关系。

item.title: 基于神经微分方程的动态场景预测方法
item.content:encoded:
Node-RF通过神经微分方程实现场景动态的长期外推。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Node-RF的方法，结合神经微分方程（NODEs）和动态神经辐射场（NeRF），以实现对场景动态的连续时间、时空表示。该方法能够在不增加内存成本的情况下，超越观测轨迹进行长期预测。从视觉输入中，Node-RF通过ODE求解器学习隐式场景状态，并通过微积分传播特征嵌入。NeRF渲染器根据计算出的嵌入生成任意视角，实现长距离外推。实验表明，Node-RF可以在不依赖显式模型的情况下表征抽象系统行为，识别未来预测的关键点。

item.title: 基于最小最大优化的鲁棒强化学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MMDDPG方法提升强化学习在干扰下的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文针对强化学习在面对外部干扰和模型不确定性时性能不稳定的问题，提出了一种基于最小最大优化的深度确定性策略梯度方法（MMDDPG）。该方法通过用户策略与对抗扰动策略之间的博弈，使用户策略在最小化目标函数的同时，对抗策略则最大化该函数。为稳定这种交互，引入了分数目标函数以平衡任务性能和扰动强度，防止过激扰动并促进稳健学习。实验结果表明，MMDDPG在MuJoCo环境中对力扰动和模型参数变化表现出显著增强的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于逆向神经算子的隐式ODE参数恢复方法
item.content:encoded:
INO通过两阶段框架高效恢复稀疏观测下的ODE参数。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为逆向神经算子（INO）的两阶段框架，用于从稀疏、部分观测中恢复隐藏的常微分方程（ODE）参数。第一阶段使用带有交叉注意力机制的条件傅里叶神经算子（C-FNO），通过频谱正则化抑制高频伪影，重建完整的ODE轨迹。第二阶段通过摊销漂移模型（ADM）在参数空间中学习加权速度场，将随机参数初始化引导至真实值，避免了梯度反传带来的雅可比不稳定性问题。实验表明，在真实世界的刚性大气化学基准数据（POLLU，25个参数）和合成基因调控网络（GRN，40个参数）上，INO在参数恢复精度上优于基于梯度和摊销的基线方法，且推理时间仅需0.23秒，比迭代梯度下降快487倍。

item.title: 语言模型的正确性偏好源于压缩与一致性原则
item.content:encoded:
语言模型偏好正确陈述，因压缩与一致性原则影响。



摘要： 本文提出“压缩-一致性原则”，解释为何语言模型在训练数据质量混杂时仍偏好正确陈述。研究通过小规模GPT-2风格字符级Transformer模型，在合成数学语料库上测试发现，模型在随机错误设置中强烈倾向于正确补全，准确率高达83.1%。当错误具有逻辑一致性但不正确时，偏好显著减弱。实验还表明，嵌入验证步骤可恢复对正确的偏好，而增加一致规则数量则提升准确性。结果表明，所谓的“真理偏见”更多是压缩压力和内部一致性偏好所致，而非内在追求真理。
